adv_u01.text: 第一单元 - 现在进行时和一般现在时（一）：静态动词
adv_u01.note: 我想与我在想——有两个意思
adv_u01.exp:
Unit 1(Advanced)— 状态动词与现在进行/一般现在：双重解读。

状态动词通常用一般时态，但许多可以用进行时并改变意义。Think：I think you're right(观点)↔ I'm thinking about your offer(正在考虑)。See：I see what you mean(理解)↔ I'm seeing a specialist(正在看/咨询)。Have：I have three brothers(拥有)↔ I'm having lunch(过程)。Taste/smell：The soup tastes salty(性质)↔ She's tasting the soup(主动尝)。Feel：I feel it's unfair(认为)↔ I'm feeling dizzy(身体感受，也可用一般时)。

语域：状态动词用进行时给句子以动感，常带口语色彩。语用：I'm loving it — 广告口号，强调式，非中性。正式写作通常保持一般时以陈述事实和观点。这一层次将 C1 与 B2 区别开：能够为语感有意识地切换形式。
adv_u01.exc:
状态动词(know, believe, belong, own, contain)通常不用进行时。

但 think, have, see, feel, taste 会变义：I'm thinking(考虑)vs. I think(认为)。
mistakes: I am knowing him → I know him — know 是状态动词。
I am having a car → I have a car — 拥有 — simple。
This is tasting good → This tastes good — 感知 — simple。

adv_u02.text: 第 2 单元 – 现在进行时和一般现在时 (2)
adv_u02.note: 临时情况与永久情况 - 高级案例
adv_u02.exp:
Unit 2(Advanced)— 临时 vs 长期及进行时的情感色彩。

现在进行时不仅表示「此刻」：
  • 临时时段：I'm living with my parents while my flat is being renovated — 暂时住在父母家(长期：I live with my parents)。
  • 带 always, constantly, forever 的情感色彩：She's always losing her keys — 老是丢钥匙(恼怒，中性一般时无此含义)。
  • 发展趋势：The climate is getting warmer — 气候变暖。
  • 现在时的生动叙事(讲段子)：So he walks in and he's waving his arms…
一般时 — 用于长期状态、科学真理、时刻表、普遍特征：Water boils at 100°C; The Earth orbits the Sun.

语用：时态选择是风格工具。进行时 + always 表情感；学术文本中不宜。能在描述性一般时与投入性进行时之间切换 — 高级水平的标志。
adv_u02.exc:
always + 进行时 = 恼人习惯：She's always complaining。

进行时强调临时性：I'm living in Paris(临时)vs. I live in Paris(长期)。
mistakes: The Earth is going round the sun → The Earth goes round the sun — 科学事实 — simple。
He always is complaining → He is always complaining — 语序：to be + always + -ing。
I'm usually working late → I usually work late — 习惯 — simple。

adv_u03.text: 第三单元 – 一般过去时和现在完成时
adv_u03.exp:
Unit 3(Advanced)— 一般过去 vs 现在完成：语境、语域、用法。

基本规则「具体过去 → 一般过去，与现在有关 → 现在完成」因语境因素而复杂化。话语结构：发布新闻用完成时：A man has been arrested in connection with the robbery. 细节用一般过去：He was arrested at his home this morning. 这种「标题 — 展开」配对在新闻报道中很典型。

地区差异：美式更自由地在 just, already, yet 后用一般过去：Did you eat yet?(美)vs Have you eaten yet?(英)。英式学术语域中这些副词须用完成时。

视角切换：It's the first/second/only time + Present Perfect：It's the first time I've seen snow. 信号词：ever, never, so far, up to now, recently, lately, since → 完成时；ago, in 1999, yesterday, last week, when → 一般过去。

例：I've lived in Rome for ten years(至今)↔ I lived in Rome for ten years(已结束)。叙事中以一般过去为主；完成时会打断叙事，回到当下。
adv_u03.exc: 美式更常在 BrE 用现在完成处用一般过去：Did you eat yet?(美)vs. Have you eaten yet?(英)。
mistakes: I have seen him yesterday → I saw him yesterday — 具体过去 — Past Simple。
When have you arrived? → When did you arrive? — when → Past Simple。
I live here since 2010 → I have lived here since 2010 — since → Perfect。

adv_u04.text: 第 4 单元 - 过去进行时和过去简单
adv_u04.exp:
Unit 4(Advanced)— 复杂叙事中的过去进行与一般过去。

过去进行设置背景，一般过去为点事件：The sun was setting and birds were singing as we walked home — 太阳落下，鸟儿鸣叫，我们走回家。中断：I was reading when the phone rang. 同时进行的持续动作：While I was cooking, she was setting the table.

风格功能：
  • 委婉/外交：I was wondering if you could help me — 比 I wonder 更礼貌。
  • 未实现的打算：I was going to call you, but I forgot(本打算，但)。
  • 反复恼人动作：He was always turning up late — 老是迟到。

限制：状态动词(know, believe, understand, belong)通常不用进行时，即使作背景：I knew him then(不 was knowing)。

一般过去与进行之间的选择决定「讲述者镜头」：事件点或过程。
adv_u04.exc: 状态动词通常用 Past Simple，即使作「背景」：I knew him then(不 was knowing)。
mistakes: When I came in, she read a book (background) → she was reading a book — 背景动作 — Continuous。
I was knowing the answer → I knew the answer — 状态 — simple。
While I cooked, the phone rang → While I was cooking, the phone rang。

adv_u05.text: 单元 5 - 过去完成时和过去简单
adv_u05.exp:
Unit 5(Advanced)— 过去完成作为表达先后顺序的手段。

公式：had + V3。作用 — 表明某过去事件先于另一过去事件：When I arrived, the meeting had already started — 我到时会议已开始(开始早于我到)。

必需情况：与 by the time：By the time we reached the theatre, the play had ended. 间接引语中 Past Simple/Present Perfect 的回移：She said she had seen him. 文本中事件顺序倒置(结果先于原因叙述)：He was exhausted. He had run ten miles.

无需情况：若 after/before/when 已指明顺序：After he left, I called a taxi — 一般过去足够。

文学语域：过去完成常见于叙事小说：She had always wanted to travel. 口语中若顺序从上下文可知，常用一般过去。

例：I didn't recognise her — she had changed so much — 我没认出她：她变化太大(变化在未遇之前发生)。
adv_u05.exc:
若 after, before, when 已表明顺序，不需 Past Perfect：After he left, I called。

与 by the time：Past Perfect 必需：By the time we arrived, they had gone。
mistakes: When I arrived, he left (= before me) → When I arrived, he had left — 早于 — Past Perfect。
I had ate already → I had eaten already — 第三式。
By the time she came, I finished → I had finished。

adv_u06.text: 单元 6 – 现在完成进行时和现在完成时
adv_u06.exp:
Unit 6(Advanced)— 现在完成进行 vs 现在完成：过程 vs 结果。

PPC (have been + -ing) 聚焦持续、过程、近期活动：I've been painting the kitchen — 在刷厨房(可能未完；关注过程/痕迹)。PP (have + V3) 聚焦结果、数量、完成：I've painted three rooms — 刷了三间房(结果)。

两者皆可用于持续性动词(live, work, study, teach, wait)描述当前状态：I've lived / I've been living here for ten years。状态动词 — 只用完成时：I've known her for years(不用 PPC)。

语用：I've been trying to reach you — 带歉意/解释口吻。Have you been crying? — 见到痕迹。Someone has been using my computer — 有介入迹象。

信号：for, since, all day, recently, lately — 两种皆可；how many, twice, three times — 仅完成(计数)。How long 通常倾向 PPC 以强调过程。
adv_u06.exc:
状态动词不用 PPC：I've known him for years(不 been knowing)。

PPC 重过程，Perfect 重结果：I've been painting(仍进行)vs. I've painted the room(完成)。
mistakes: I've been knowing her → I've known her — 状态。
I've read three books this week (process focus) — 数量 — Perfect；过程 — I've been reading。
She has been finished → She has finished。

adv_u07.text: 第七单元 - 过去完成进行时、过去完成时和过去进行时
adv_u07.note: 一个叙述中的三个过去时态
adv_u07.exp:
Unit 7(Advanced)— 三种过去层次：过去进行、过去完成、过去完成进行在连贯叙事中。

过去进行 — 场景/背景；过去完成 — 已完成的先前事件；过去完成进行(had been + -ing) — 带因果关系的先前持续动作。

典型叙事模式：She looked exhausted. She had been working since 6 a.m. and hadn't eaten all day when the call finally came. — 此处 PPC 解释她状态，过去完成记录「没吃」的事实，一般过去标记事件点。

PPC vs 过去完成：When I arrived, they had had dinner(事实：晚餐已结束)↔ When I arrived, they had been having dinner(持续到我到，可能未结束)。The roads were wet because it had rained(事实)↔ because it had been raining(强调持续性)。

限制：状态动词不构成 PPC：I had known him，不 had been knowing。语域：PPC 常见于文学和传记散文，学术文本较少。
adv_u07.exc:
过去完成进行不用于状态动词。

过去完成进行强调到某过去时点前的持续性：She had been waiting for an hour when he arrived。
mistakes: She had been knowing him → She had known him。
He was tired because he ran → he had been running — 原因持续到该时点 — PPC。
When I came, she had been leaving (action done) → she had left。

adv_u08.text: 第 8 单元 - 现在和过去的时间：概述
adv_u08.note: 对现在和过去的系统回顾
adv_u08.exp:
Unit 8(Advanced)— 现在与过去时态综述：系统选择。

沿四个维度决策：
  1. 点/时段：点 → 一般；持续 → 进行。
  2. 完成性：完成并有当前关联 → 完成；与现在无关 → 过去。
  3. 过程 vs 结果：进行 vs 完成。
  4. 与现在的关联：有 → 完成；无 → 过去。
这些轴相交：现在完成进行 = 时段 + 过程 + 关联。

语域偏离：
  • 媒体标题常以一般时替代完成时：Plane crashes in storm。
  • 司法公文偏好完成时：The defendant has admitted…
  • 文学叙事以过去为主，现在完成出现在对话/内心独白中。

典型陷阱：since/for 要求完成时；ago/具体日期要求一般过去；when 问精确时刻 — 一般过去(When did you arrive?)。

总结：完成时是连接过去与现在的「桥」；一般过去是「封闭」过去；任何时区的进行时都是「镜头聚焦过程」。
adv_u08.exc: 新闻标题常用 Past Simple 代替 Present Perfect：Plane crashes in storm。
mistakes: I am here since morning → I have been here since morning — since → Perfect。
She has left an hour ago → She left an hour ago — ago → Past Simple。
Did you ever be there? → Have you ever been there?

adv_u09.text: 第 9 单元 – 将会和将会
adv_u09.note: 在“意愿”和“将要”之间进行选择的微妙之处
adv_u09.exp:
Unit 9(Advanced)— will vs be going to：超越基本规则。

「临时 vs 计划」的基本对立只是其中一轴。C1 层级关注：
  • 预测的证据类型：be going to 基于可见迹象(Look at those clouds — it's going to rain)；will 基于常识/经验/推断(It will probably rain — it usually does in April)。
  • 计划：be going to — 说话前已决定的意图；will — 当下做出的决定：— The phone's ringing. — I'll get it.
  • 语域：will 在正式/书面中更中性；be going to 偏口语，尤其美式。
  • 承诺、建议、威胁、拒绝：只用 will/won't：I'll help you. I won't tell anyone.

微妙点：was/were going to 常表未实现意图：I was going to call, but I forgot. There will be 用于预报(There will be rain tomorrow)，但 there is going to be 用于预期事件(There's going to be trouble)。
adv_u09.exc:
will 用于临时决定和预测；be going to 用于计划和可见迹象。

两者都可预测，但 going to 基于当前事实：Look at those clouds — it's going to rain。
mistakes: I will meet her tomorrow (plan) → I'm going to meet her / I'm meeting her — 计划。
The phone rings — I'm going to get it → I'll get it — 临时。
Look, it will rain → it's going to rain — 有迹象。

adv_u10.text: 第 10 单元 – 现在的简单和未来的连续
adv_u10.note: 时间表和计划 - 高级细微差别
adv_u10.exp:
Unit 10(Advanced)— 以现在时表达未来。

一般现在表未来 — 只用于说话人无法控制的时刻表、日程、固定日期：The flight leaves at 7.45. The conference begins on Monday. The film starts at 8. 也用于时间/条件从句：When he arrives, I'll tell him; If it rains, we'll stay home.

现在进行表未来 — 用于已经具体安排的个人约定(通话、预订、邀请)：I'm meeting Ann at 6. We're having dinner with the Smiths on Friday. 需要有约定/安排。

固定度层次：The train leaves at 5(时刻表 — 几近法则)> I'm leaving at 5(坚定意图/约定)> I'm going to leave at 5(意图，但不那么确定)> I'll leave at 5(当下决定/承诺)。

限制：现在进行表未来不用于天气/自然预测：不能 It's raining tomorrow，而 It's going to rain / It will rain. 公共交通时刻表不能用进行时：The train leaves at 6，不 is leaving。
adv_u10.exc:
Present Simple 表未来 — 仅用于时刻表：The train leaves at 6。

Present Continuous — 用于个人约定：I'm meeting Tom at 5。
mistakes: I meet Tom tomorrow (arranged) → I'm meeting Tom tomorrow — 约定 — Continuous。
The film is starting at 8 (schedule) → The film starts at 8 — 时刻表 — Simple。
When will he comes? → When does he come? / When is he coming?

adv_u100.text: 单元 100 - 反演（第 2 课）
adv_u100.note: 这就是/太棒了/我几乎没有……
adv_u100.exp:
Unit 100 — 倒装（第 2 课）：so/such, hardly, no sooner。

现象：第二组倒装结构 — 极正式，常带文学色彩。

模式：
  • So + adj/adv + be/助动词 + 主语 + that-从句：So great was his anger that he left. — 他的愤怒如此强烈，以致离开了。
  • Such + be + 主语 (+ that)：Such was the force of the explosion that windows shattered.
  • Hardly/Scarcely + had + 主语 + V3 + when…：Hardly had I arrived when the phone rang. — 我刚到电话就响了。
  • No sooner + had + 主语 + V3 + than…：No sooner had she spoken than he replied.
  • 条件句中的倒装：Had I known… Were I you… Should you need…（参见 Unit 85）。
关键配对：Hardly/Scarcely … when（不用 than）；No sooner … than（不用 when）。

语体：文学叙事、官方文件、演讲。

对比：中性等价 — As soon as I arrived, the phone rang. 倒装版本使事件序列戏剧化。
mistakes: No sooner he had left when it rained. → No sooner had he left than it rained.
Hardly I had arrived than → Hardly had I arrived when…
Such was a his anger → Such was his anger that… — 不加冠词。

adv_u11.text: 第 11 单元 - 将来进行时和将来完成时（进行时）
adv_u11.note: 我会工作/我会完成/我会一直在工作
adv_u11.exp:
Unit 11(Advanced)— 将来进行、将来完成和将来完成进行。

将来进行(will be + -ing)：未来某时点的过程(This time tomorrow I'll be flying to Tokyo)；按事件发展的计划/预期(I'll be seeing her later — 顺便见)；无压力的礼貌询问计划(Will you be using the car tomorrow? — 比 Do you need the car? 更委婉)。

将来完成(will have + V3)：动作在未来某时点前完成。标志：by + time, by the time + clause。By 2030 I'll have graduated. By the time you arrive, we'll have finished dinner.

将来完成进行(will have been + -ing)：过程延续到未来某时点。In June, I'll have been working here for ten years. 强调持续性。

语域和限制：将来完成进行相对少见，常见于传记和纪念语境。状态动词不接进行时：I will have known him for 20 years，不 will have been knowing。将来进行减轻请求压力 — 商务邮件的重要语用策略。
adv_u11.exc:
Future Perfect 通常带 by + 时间：By 2030 I'll have graduated。

Future Continuous 可表示「计划/预期的未来动作」：I'll be seeing him tomorrow。
mistakes: At 5 tomorrow I will work → I will be working — 某时点的过程。
By next year I will finish → I will have finished — 到某时 — Perfect。
I will have been knowing → I will have known — 状态。

adv_u12.text: 第 12 单元 - Be to + 不定式
adv_u12.exp:
Unit 12(Advanced)— be to + infinitive 结构。

公式：am/is/are/was/were + to + V。主要用于书面、正式语域(报纸、官方公告、外交、指令)。

含义：
  • 正式计划/约定：The Prime Minister is to visit Japan next week — 首相下周访日。
  • 指令：You are to report to security by 9 a.m. — 你须 9 点前到保安处报到。
  • 禁止：No one is to enter the room — 任何人不得进入。
  • 条件(if 从句)：If we are to succeed, we must act now — 若想成功，必须现在行动。
  • 命运(文学)：He was to become a great artist — 他注定成为伟大艺术家。

过去：was/were to + V 可表示实现或未实现的意图。「本应但没做」用 was/were to have + V3：He was to have arrived yesterday(本应到但未到)。

口语中该形式几乎不用 — 其领域是正式风格。
adv_u12.exc:
be to + inf 用于新闻和正式语境：The President is to visit Japan。

也用于条件句：If we are to succeed, we must...。
mistakes: I am to meet my friend (personal plan) → I'm meeting my friend — be to 不用于个人计划。
The PM is visit France → The PM is to visit France — to 必需。
He was to died young → He was to die young — 不定式无 -ed。

adv_u13.text: 第 13 单元 - 表达未来的其他方式
adv_u13.exp:
Unit 13(Advanced)— 表达未来的其他手段。

Be about to + inf — 「即将」：The train is about to leave — 火车就要开了。常与 just 连用：I was just about to call you. 非常接近的未来，无日程绑定。

Be due to + inf / be due + time — 「按计划、预计」：The flight is due to arrive at 18.00; The book is due out in May. 中性偏正式。

Be bound to + inf — 「必定、无可避免」：She's bound to win — she's the best. 自信预测。

Be likely/unlikely to + inf — 可能性：It's likely to rain tonight. 替代：It's likely that it will rain.

Be on the point/verge/brink of + -ing — 濒临：The company is on the verge of bankruptcy.

Be set to + inf — 即将(新闻风格)：Prices are set to rise.

这些结构丰富表达，按确定度和时间临近度分：be about to(立即)→ be due to(按期)→ be likely to(大概)→ be bound to(几乎必定)。
adv_u13.exc: be about to = 即将；be due to = 按日程；be bound to = 必会发生；be on the verge/point of + -ing。
mistakes: I'm about to leaving → I'm about to leave — 不定式。
The train is due arrive → The train is due to arrive — to 必需。
He is bound fail → He is bound to fail。

adv_u14.text: 第 14 单元 - 过去的未来
adv_u14.note: 她说她会来。他要辞职了。
adv_u14.exp:
Unit 14(Advanced)— 过去中的未来：从过去看向未来。

叙事以过去进行时，但要指向相对那一时点的未来，便用特殊形式。

Would + V — 「注定/日后」：In 1920 she published her first novel. It would become a bestseller.

Was/were going to + V — 过去的意图，常未实现：He was going to apologise, but he changed his mind.

Was/were to + V — 预定的命运：He was to become president ten years later. 完成式 was to have + V3 强调未实现：The meeting was to have taken place on Monday, but was cancelled.

过去进行 — 用于已安排事件：He was leaving the next day.

Was about to + V — 即将：She was about to speak when the door opened.

主要用于间接引语(He said he would come)、传记叙事和小说。过去中的未来凸显成熟的叙述者 — 能将未来视角投射于过去。
adv_u14.exc:
过去中的未来：would、was/were going to、Past Continuous、was/were to。

was going to 常 = 计划但没做：I was going to call, but forgot。
mistakes: He said he will come → He said he would come — 回移。
I was going call → I was going to call — to。
She was to died → She was to die。

adv_u15.text: 第 15 单元 – 可以、能够、能够、被允许
adv_u15.note: 容量和分辨率：细微差别
adv_u15.exp:
Unit 15(Advanced)— 能力与许可：can, could, be able to, be allowed to, manage to。

过去的能力：could — 一般性能力(She could read at the age of four)。对一次具体成功动作首选 was/were able to, managed to, succeeded in -ing：The door was locked, but I managed to open it(非 could open)。例外 — 否定及感知动词(see, hear, smell, understand, remember)：I couldn't see him in the dark — 可用。

许可：can — 中性口语；may — 正式礼貌；be allowed to — 强调外部规则(You're not allowed to smoke here)。

认识/义务：can 同时表能力(「能」)、许可(「可以」)、可能(「有时」：It can be cold here in winter)。Could 也表温和可能(That could be true)。

语域：be able to 在情态不能用时不可或缺：另一情态前(I'd like to be able to swim)、不定式/动名词(being able to drive)、完成时(I've been able to solve it)。
adv_u15.exc:
could — 过去一般能力；一次成功 — was/were able to / managed to：I was able to escape(不 could escape)。

但否定/感知动词中 couldn't 可用：I couldn't see him。
mistakes: Yesterday I could win the match → I was able to win / managed to win — 具体事件。
He is allowed entering → He is allowed to enter — to。
I can to swim → I can swim — 无 to。

adv_u16.text: 第 16 单元 – 将会、将会和曾经
adv_u16.note: 过去的习惯和现在的特征
adv_u16.exp:
Unit 16(Advanced)— will, would, used to 用于习惯与典型模式。

Will(现在)— 典型行为或特性：Oil will float on water. He'll sit for hours, staring out of the window. 重读 will 可表恼怒：He WILL keep interrupting me!

Would(过去)— 过去反复行为，叙事中典型片段：Every summer we would go to the seaside. She would always bring me flowers. 重要限制：would 不与状态义连用：不能 *I would have a red bike(拥有状态)；此类用 used to。

Used to + inf — 过去动作和状态，现已不然：I used to live in Oxford. She used to have long hair. 含内置对比。

语域：叙事性 would 典型于小说和回忆录，赋文本回忆节奏。Used to 中性。Would always/forever + V 常带怀旧或轻微恼怒色彩。
adv_u16.exc: used to — 仅过去；would — 过去重复动作，但不含状态：would visit 可，但 would have a car 不可。
mistakes: I would live in Paris (state) → I used to live in Paris — 状态 — used to。
He use to smoke → He used to smoke。
Did he used to? → Did he use to? — did 后 — use。

adv_u17.text: 第 17 单元 – 五月和可能：概率
adv_u17.note: 分级不确定性和义务论
adv_u17.exp:
Unit 17(Advanced)— may 和 might：可能性与许可。

认识义(可能性)：确定度梯度：must(确信)> will(强推测)> should(预期)> may(可能 ~50%)> might/could(或许，更低)。She may be in her office — 她可能在办公室。He might come later — 他也许稍后来。

过去：may/might have + V3 — 「可能做过」：She may have left already — 或许已经走了。Might have done 也表责备/未实现的可能：You might have told me! — 你本可以告诉我！

义务义(许可)：May I come in? — 正式礼貌。May 用于允许规则：Students may use the library until 10. Might 此义少见且古雅。

may vs might 区别：might 常显得更不确定、更假设；条件语境中 might 几乎必需：If you asked him, he might help. 中性陈述两者可互换。May not = 也许不；might not — 相似，略更弱。
adv_u17.exc:
may/might have + V3 = 或许过去做过。

might 常比 may 听起来更不确定。

正式请求中 may I 比 can I 更礼貌。
mistakes: He may to come → He may come — 无 to。
She might is right → She might be right — 不定式。
He may came → He may have come — 过去式。

adv_u18.text: 第 18 单元 – 必须并且必须（得）
adv_u18.note: C1 上的内部强制与外部强制
adv_u18.exp:
Unit 18(Advanced)— must 与 have (got) to：义务与推断。

义务义(必须)：must — 主观、内在或说话人作为权威：I must call my mother(自决)；Students must wear uniforms(机构/说话人规则)。Have to / have got to — 外部义务：I have to work on Saturdays(老板要求)。美式中 have to 几乎取代 must。

过去：must 在义务义上无过去式 — 用 had to：I had to stay late yesterday.

认识义(推断)：must 表强烈推断：He must be tired — he's been working all day. 过去：must have + V3：She must have forgotten — 肯定是忘了。

否定 — 关键陷阱：
  • mustn't = 禁止(「不能」)：You mustn't smoke here.
  • don't have to = 不必要(「不必」)：You don't have to come.
  • can't — 认识 must 的否定：He can't be serious(= 不可能)。

语域：must 更正式、书面；have to 中性口语；have got to 口语。
adv_u18.exc:
must — 内在信念；have to — 外部义务。

否定：mustn't = 禁止；don't have to = 不必。
mistakes: You mustn't come (= no need) → You don't have to come — 不必。
I must to go → I must go。
I must called her yesterday → I had to call — 过去义务 — had to。

adv_u19.text: 第 19 单元 - 不需要、不需要、不必
adv_u19.exp:
Unit 19(Advanced)— 三种「不必」：needn't, don't need to, don't have to。

Needn't + V(情态，无 to)— 说话人视角的不必要：You needn't wait — I'll catch up. 英式偏多，较正式。

Don't need to + V(实义)— 事实上不必：You don't need to book in advance. 中性。

Don't have to + V — 无义务：You don't have to answer if you don't want to. 口语中最常用。

过去的关键区别：
  • Needn't have + V3 = 做了但没必要：You needn't have cooked — we've eaten(饭做了，但白做)。
  • Didn't need to + V = 没必要(通常也没做)：I didn't need to go to the bank(也就没去)。
常需语境以辨是否实际做了。

语用：needn't have 带「可惜你做了，本多余」意味；这是 C1 文章和对话中重要的微妙点。
adv_u19.exc: needn't + V = 不必；needn't have + V3 = 白做；didn't need to + V = 当时没必要(通常未做)。
mistakes: You needn't to go → You needn't go — needn't 无 to。
I needn't went → I needn't have gone — 过去 — have V3。
She doesn't need go → She doesn't need to go — don't need + to。

adv_u20.text: 第 20 单元 – 应该、应该、最好
adv_u20.exp:
Unit 20(Advanced)— should, ought to, had better：建议与劝告。

Should / ought to — 近义，弱的道德或理性义务：You should/ought to see a doctor. Ought to 略正式，常依客观规范；should 更通用。否定：shouldn't / ought not to。ought to 疑问句少见 — 几乎都用 should。

Had better(+ V，无 to) — 针对具体情境的强建议，常暗示后果：You'd better leave now, or you'll miss the train. 否定：had better not：You'd better not be late. 非一般规范，而是临时建议。

过去(责备)：
  • should/ought to have + V3 = 本该做而没做：You should have told me.
  • shouldn't/ought not to have + V3 = 本不该但做了：I shouldn't have eaten so much.

语域：ought to 在美式略显老派；英式仍活。Had better — 口语强硬语气，不适合中性礼貌建议(用 You might want to / It might be a good idea to)。
adv_u20.exc:
had better — 强建议，常带警告；should — 中性建议。

ought to = should(稍正式)；否定：ought not to。
mistakes: You had better to go → You had better go — 无 to。
I would better go → I had better go — had，不是 would。
You should to call → You should call。

adv_u21.text: 第 21 单元 - 连接动词：be、appear、seem、become、get
adv_u21.note: 她看起来很累。没有生气。天黑了。
adv_u21.exp:
Unit 21(Advanced)— 连系动词(linking / copular verbs)。

连系动词连接主语与谓语的名词部分(形容词或名词)，描述主语而非动作。其后用形容词，不用副词。

静态连系：be, seem, appear, look, feel, sound, smell, taste, remain, stay, keep：She seems happy. This cheese tastes odd. He remained silent.

状态变化连系：
  • become — 通用，常接名词/形容词：He became a doctor.
  • get — 口语，接形容词：It's getting dark; I got tired.
  • grow — 渐变：grow old, grow tired.
  • turn — 突变/颜色：The leaves turned yellow; He turned pale.
  • go — 负面/反常：go bad, go blind, go mad.
  • come — 积极结果：dreams come true, wishes come true.
  • fall — 突然：fall ill, fall silent, fall asleep.

语境：look good(连系，看起来好)↔ look carefully(动作，仔细看)。同一动词可为连系或实义 — 由句法位置决定。
adv_u21.exc:
连系动词后用形容词，不用副词：She looks tired，不 tiredly。

get 常 = 变成：It's getting dark。
mistakes: She seems tiredly → She seems tired — 形容词。
He became to angry → He became angry — 无 to。
The milk turned to sour → The milk turned sour。

adv_u22.text: 第 22 单元 - 被动（第 1 课）
adv_u22.note: 被动语态：C1 上的功能和选择
adv_u22.exp:
Unit 22(Advanced)— 被动语态：功能与选择。

公式：be + V3 贯穿所有时态。选被动的三种话语原因：
  1. 施事未知/不重要：My bike has been stolen.
  2. 施事明显或泛化：She was arrested yesterday(当然是警察)。
  3. 信息焦点：主位为动作对象：The Sistine Chapel was painted by Michelangelo.

学术和官方语域大量使用被动以求无人称：The experiment was conducted under controlled conditions.

Get-passive — 口语变体，常带意外或负面色彩：She got fired last week; We got caught in the rain. 多伴「偶然/情感」事件。

限制：不及物动词无被动(happen, arrive, die, come, go, fall)：不能 *It was happened。表「拥有状态」的及物动词(have, lack, possess, fit, suit, resemble)通常也无被动。

By vs with：by — 施事者(written by Dickens)；with — 工具(cut with a knife)；filled with(充满)— 特殊情况。
adv_u22.exc:
并非所有动词可被动(如 have 「拥有」、fit、suit)。

by — 施事；with — 工具：cut by him with a knife。
mistakes: The book was wrote → was written — V3。
It was happened → It happened — happen 无被动。
He was died → He died — die 无被动。

adv_u23.text: 第 23 单元 - 被动语态（第 2 课）：-ing 和 to-不定式
adv_u23.note: 已完成/待完成/已经完成
adv_u23.exp:
Unit 23(Advanced)— 被动非谓语形式。

被动不定式 — (to) be + V3：The report needs to be finished by Monday. This problem has to be solved. 常见于情态动词后及 need, want, have 之后。

被动动名词 — being + V3：She hates being criticised. He remembers being praised as a child. 当隐含主语是动作对象时使用。

完成被动形式：
  • 完成不定式被动：to have been + V3：He claims to have been robbed.
  • 完成动名词被动：having been + V3：Having been warned, she was careful.

被动分词(过去分词)用作精简从句：The letter written yesterday is on the desk(= which was written)；Being followed, she walked faster(= because she was being followed)。

语域：被动 -ing 和不定式形式常见于正式/学术文本。口语常以主动或从句替代：After I was told → Having been told / After being told. 掌握这些是 C1+ 标志。
adv_u23.exc: 被动动名词：being done。被动不定式：to be done。完成被动动名词：having been done。
mistakes: I hate be criticized → I hate being criticized — -ing。
This needs to done → This needs to be done。
After been told → After being told / Having been told。

adv_u24.text: 单元 24 - 使用被动的不同方法
adv_u24.note: 被动的两个物体和其他高级情况
adv_u24.exp:
Unit 24(Advanced)— 非常规被动结构。

双宾语被动。give, send, offer, tell, show, teach, pay, lend, promise 两宾语 → 两种被动形式：She gave me a present → I was given a present (by her) / A present was given to me. 口语中以人作主语的形式常优先 — 主题化更强。

例外动词：explain, describe, suggest, mention, announce, say 不允许被动主语为人：The rule was explained to me，不 *I was explained the rule。

It 引导的非人称被动：It is said/believed/thought that… — 常见于新闻和学术：It was decided that the meeting would be postponed. 替代 — personal passive(见 Unit 25)。

Get-passive：口语，常有负面含义：He got sacked; We got soaked.

伪被动/描述性被动：需区分状态与动作：The door was open(状态)↔ The door was opened by Tom(动作)。be 后的形容词非被动，虽形似。

使役 have/get：I had/got my hair cut — 「被剪了头发」，主动形式的被动意。
adv_u24.exc: 双宾语动词可有两种被动：She was given a book / A book was given to her。以人为主语优先。
mistakes: A book was given her → A book was given to her — 需 to。
I was explained the rule → The rule was explained to me — explain 不允许人作主语。
He was said that… → He was told that… / It was said that… — say ≠ tell。

adv_u25.text: 第 25 单元 – 被动报告：据说……
adv_u25.exp:
Unit 25(Advanced)— reporting 与被动：It is said that… / He is said to…

在非人称转述观点和信息 — 新闻、学术、综述中 — 使用两种同义结构：
  1. It + be + V3 + that 从句：It is said that she's the best in her field.
  2. 主语 + be + V3 + to 不定式：She is said to be the best in her field.
两者都传达「众所周知/据称」意，不指明来源。

适用动词：say, believe, think, consider, know, report, claim, allege, suppose, understand, expect, find, rumour。

不定式时态：
  • 现在/普遍：He is said to live in Paris(现在居住)。
  • 过去：to have + V3：He is said to have lived in Paris(过去曾住)。
  • 持续：to be + -ing：He is said to be living in Paris(正住着)。
  • 完成持续：to have been + -ing：He is said to have been living there for ten years.

语域与距离：此结构表谨慎与疏离 — 作者不确认，仅转述。在新闻业不可或缺：The suspect is alleged to have entered through the window.
adv_u25.exc:
两种结构：It is said that he is rich = He is said to be rich。

过去：He is said to have left。
mistakes: He is said that he is rich → He is said to be rich / It is said that he is rich。
He is said to left → He is said to have left — 过去 — have V3。
It said that… → It is said that…。

adv_u26.text: 第 26 单元 – Wh 问题：who、who、which、how、whos
adv_u26.note: 带介词和 who/whom 选择的复杂问题
adv_u26.exp:
Unit 26(Advanced)— 复杂 wh-问题：who/whom, which, how, whose, what。

Who vs whom：现代英语中 who 占主流；whom — 严正式，仅紧跟介词后必需：To whom should I address the complaint? 口语中介词移至句末：Who should I address the complaint to?

Which vs what：which — 有限范围内选择(Which of these books do you prefer?)；what — 无范围的开放问句(What do you want to read?)。

Whose — 所有权问句：Whose bag is this?

How + 形/副表程度：How tall is he? How often do you go there? How long have you lived here?

间接/嵌入式问句用陈述语序，无 do/does/did：Do you know where he lives?(不是 where does he live)。此规则高级学习者也常违反。

What…like vs How：What is she like? — 她性格如何？How is she? — 她怎样(身体状况)？What does she look like? — 长相如何？How does it work? — 如何运作？

语用：whom 在书面投诉和正式信函中保留古雅权威。电邮中多用 who。
adv_u26.exc:
whom — 正式宾格；非正式 — who：Who did you see?。

介词置句末(非正式)或 whom 前(正式)：To whom did you speak?。
mistakes: Who did you give it? → Who(m) did you give it to? — 需要介词。
Whose is this car yours? → Is this car yours? / Whose car is this?。
How is she like? → What is she like? — 性格 — what。

adv_u27.text: 第 27 单元 - 否定问题；回应问题；带有聊天条款的问题
adv_u27.note: 她不是很可爱吗？她要走了，是吗？
adv_u27.exp:
Unit 27(Advanced)— 非标准问句类型。

否定问句(Isn't she lovely? Didn't you know?)表达的非信息请求，而是情感：惊讶、恼怒、确认。Haven't we met before? — 我们好像见过？Why didn't you tell me? — 责备。感叹语调：Isn't it beautiful! — 多美啊！

回声问句以升调重复前句部分，表惊讶或再问：— I'm moving to Tokyo. — You're moving WHERE? 陈述语序加重读。

陈述式问句 — 陈述句形式带升调表疑问：You've finished already? 表惊讶或求确认。

附加问句(反意)：确认预期答案。规则：肯定 + 否定附加(She's Russian, isn't she?)；否定 + 肯定(You don't smoke, do you?)。特殊：I am → aren't I?；Let's → shall we?；祈使 → will/would/can you?

语用：附加降调 — 「我知道，请确认」；升调 — 「真的如此吗？」
adv_u27.exc:
否定问句表惊讶/情感：Isn't she lovely!。

Tag questions：肯定 → 否定 tag，否定 → 肯定 tag。
mistakes: You like it, don't you like? → …, don't you? — 简短形式。
He is tired, isn't it? → …, isn't he? — 代词匹配主语。
Let's go, don't we? → Let's go, shall we?。

adv_u28.text: 第 28 单元 - 动词后面的不同类型宾语
adv_u28.exp:
Unit 28(Advanced)— 动词后宾语类型：补足类型。

动词选择其补足结构。基本模式：
  • Verb + object + noun(宾语补足):They elected him president; We call him Bob.
  • Verb + object + adjective：She painted the door red; I find this book fascinating; He drove me crazy.
  • Verb + object + 带 to 不定式：She asked him to help; I want you to listen.
  • Verb + object + 不带 to 不定式(在 make, let, have + 感知动词后)：Let me go; I saw him leave.
  • Verb + object + -ing：I heard her singing; I saw them running.
  • Verb + object + 过去分词：I want it done by noon; She had her car repaired.

完全 vs 部分感知：I saw him cross the street(看完整)vs I saw him crossing the street(见过程中)。

「宾语谓述」语义：elect, appoint, name, call, consider, find, declare, prove, keep, leave + object + complement 形成内嵌小句：I consider him [a friend] / [honest] / [to be reliable].

语域：consider/find sb to be + adj 比不加 to be 更正式。
adv_u28.exc: 某些动词需 object + complement：elect him president, paint it red, consider him a friend。
mistakes: They elected him as president (in formal BrE ok, but often) → They elected him president — 无 as。
She painted the wall in red → She painted the wall red。
I find him as rude → I find him rude。

adv_u29.text: 第 29 单元 - 动词 + 两个宾语
adv_u29.note: 她给了我一本书。 / 她给了我一本书。
adv_u29.exp:
Unit 29(Advanced)— 双宾动词(ditransitive)。

管两宾语的动词涉及赠、送、告知：give, send, offer, show, teach, tell, lend, buy, make, cook, get, pay, promise。

两种句法模式：
  1. Verb + 间接宾语(接受者) + 直接宾语(物):I gave Tom the book.
  2. Verb + 直接宾语 + to/for + 接受者：I gave the book to Tom.

To vs for：to 用于 give, send, tell, show, pay, offer, lend, promise, hand, pass, read；for 用于 buy, get, make, cook, find, order, save, leave(受益者，非接收者)。

信息结构：语序选择关乎话题/述题：新信息通常在末。I gave Tom the book — 新 = 书。I gave the book to Tom — 新 = 汤姆。代词宾语多要求第二种语序：Give it to me(美式不用 *give me it)。

例外动词 — 只能用 to 结构：explain, describe, suggest, announce, mention, introduce, admit, confess, report。不能 *explain me the rule — 只能 explain the rule to me。Suggest 要求 that 从句或 -ing：She suggested that I go / She suggested going.
adv_u29.exc: explain, describe, suggest 不接人作宾语：explain it to me，不 explain me it。
mistakes: He explained me the rule → He explained the rule to me。
She suggested me to go → She suggested (that) I (should) go。
Give to me the book → Give me the book / Give the book to me。

adv_u30.text: 第 30 单元 - 带 -ing 或不定式的动词（第 1 课）
adv_u30.note: 记住/理解/决定：什么形式以及为什么
adv_u30.exp:
Unit 30(Advanced)— 动词 + -ing 或 + to 不定式：系统综述。

只接动名词(-ing)：enjoy, avoid, finish, mind, miss, practise, suggest, consider, deny, admit, delay, postpone, resist, risk, imagine, involve, keep, fancy, can't help, can't stand, give up, put off, feel like。I enjoy reading; She suggested going; We can't afford to waste time.

只接不定式(to + V)：want, hope, decide, plan, expect, promise, refuse, offer, manage, afford, agree, fail, seem, appear, tend, pretend, intend, threaten, choose, learn, deserve。I hope to see you soon.

两种意义不同：
  • remember/forget + -ing = 过去记忆；+ to V = 将来义务：I remember locking the door vs Remember to lock the door.
  • stop + -ing = 停止过程；+ to V = 停下来以…：He stopped smoking vs He stopped to smoke.
  • regret + -ing = 为过去后悔；+ to inform/say/tell = 正式地遗憾通知：We regret to inform you…
  • go on + -ing = 继续同一事；+ to V = 转到他事：He went on talking vs He went on to become a minister.

两种意义相同：begin, start, continue, bother, love, like, hate, prefer(细微差别：-ing 一般享受，to V 具体意图)。
adv_u30.exc:
remember/forget/regret + -ing — 过去；+ to — 将来/义务。

stop + -ing — 停止；+ to — 停下以。
mistakes: I remember to lock the door (I did it) → I remember locking it — 过去 — -ing。
He stopped to smoke (= quit) → He stopped smoking — 戒烟。
I regret to tell him yesterday → I regret telling him。

adv_u31.text: 单元 31 - 带 -ing 或不定式的动词（第 2 课）
adv_u31.note: 开始做/要做：没有区别和有区别的情况
adv_u31.exp:
Unit 31(Advanced)— 动名词 vs 不定式，特殊结构。

感知动词 + 宾语 + 不带 to 不定式 / -ing：
  • see/hear/watch/feel/notice + sb + V — 完整事件：I saw her cross the street(全过程)。
  • + sb + -ing — 过程中：I saw her crossing the street(途中看到)。

Need/want/require/deserve + -ing 具被动义：The car needs servicing = needs to be serviced; This shirt wants ironing. -ing 与被动不定式可互换。

-ing 后的习惯短语：It's worth + -ing; It's no use / no good + -ing; There's no point (in) + -ing; Have difficulty/trouble + -ing; Spend/waste time + -ing; Be busy + -ing.

Try to V vs try -ing：try to V = 努力尝试；try -ing = 试着看看：Try to open the door(努力开)vs Try opening it(试开，或许能开)。

Mean to V vs mean -ing：mean to = 意图(I meant to call you)；mean -ing = 意味着(Being a parent means making sacrifices)。

Be used to + -ing(习惯于)vs used to + V(过去曾)— 完全不同，常错。
adv_u31.exc:
无差别：begin/start/continue + -ing / to V。

有差别：try to(努力)vs. try -ing(尝试看)；mean to(意图)vs. mean -ing(意味)。
mistakes: I tried to open the window (= experimented) → I tried opening the window — 实验。
It means to wait → It means waiting — 意味。
I didn't mean hurting you → I didn't mean to hurt you。

adv_u32.text: 第 32 单元 – 间接引语、报告
adv_u32.exp:
Unit 32(Advanced)— 间接引语：完整综述。

时态回移(backshift)当 reporting 动词为过去时：Present → Past，Past → Past Perfect，Present Perfect → Past Perfect，will → would，can → could，may → might，must → had to/must(保留)。«I am tired,» she said → She said (that) she was tired.

回移非必需，若陈述仍然属实：He said the Earth is round。reporting verb 为现在时时也不回移：She says she is tired.

指示词切换：this → that, here → there, now → then, today → that day, tomorrow → the next day/the following day, yesterday → the day before, last week → the week before, next month → the following month。代词依语境：«I love my job,» → He said he loved his job.

Say vs tell：tell 需接受者(She told me (that)…)；say — 无接受者或带 to + sb：She said to me.

That 在口语中常省略：She said she was tired.

语域：学术和新闻语篇中 reporting verbs 精心选择：claim, allege, argue, suggest, admit, deny, insist, maintain — 各带评价色彩。
adv_u32.exc:
Present → Past，will → would，can → could，must → had to。

事实仍真时不需回移：She said the Earth is round。
mistakes: He said he is tired → He said he was tired — 时态回移。
She told that she left → She said (that) she had left / She told me that… — tell 需宾语。
He asked where do I live → He asked where I lived — 陈述语序。

adv_u33.text: 第 33 单元 - 报告演讲：续
adv_u33.note: 间接引语中的问题、命令、感叹
adv_u33.exp:
Unit 33(Advanced)— 间接疑问、命令、感叹。

间接 wh-问句：陈述语序，无助动词 do/does/did，无问号：«Where do you live?» → She asked (me) where I lived.

间接是非问句：用 if 或 whether：«Are you coming?» → He asked if/whether I was coming. Whether 更正式；必需时配 or not：I don't know whether or not to go.

命令与请求：tell/ask/order/warn/advise/beg/urge + sb + (not) to V：«Don't be late!» → She told me not to be late; «Please help me,» → He asked me to help.

建议与劝告：suggest + -ing / suggest that + clause；recommend + -ing / + that；propose + -ing / + that。She suggested going for a walk / She suggested (that) we go for a walk. 虚拟形式 that + base V 在正式美式中保留：He insisted that she be there.

感叹：exclaim, cry, shout + that：«What a mess!» → She exclaimed that it was a mess.

语用：reporting 动词的选择传达原句语气：snap, mutter, whisper, protest, concede — 不仅是「说」，而带方式特征。
adv_u33.exc:
Reported 问句：无倒装无 do/does/did：She asked if I was ready。

命令：tell/ask sb to do。
mistakes: She asked me do I want tea → She asked if I wanted tea。
He told me don't go → He told me not to go。
She said me to sit → She told me to sit。

adv_u34.note: 我想知道她是什么意思。问题是谁……
adv_u34.exp:
Unit 34(Advanced)— wh-从句(wh-clauses)作名词成分。

Wh-从句(what, who, where, when, why, how, whether, which, whose + clause)充当名词，占据名词位置。

作宾语：I don't know what he wants; Tell me where you've been; She wondered how he had managed it.

作主语：What she said surprised everyone; How they found us remains a mystery. 典型正式语域。

介词后：It depends on what you want; We argued about why it happened.

名词后(在 question, issue, fact, reason, idea 后)：The question of who should lead is unresolved; I have no idea why she left.

It-外置：It is unclear whether she'll come; It's amazing how fast he learns. 学术文本中常用此法将长从句后置。

关键规则 — 从句内陈述语序：I don't know where he lives(不 *where does he live)。此规则错误暴露学习者。

Whether vs if：whether 通用(主语、介词后、to V 前)；if — 只作宾语：I don't know if/whether he'll come，但只 Whether he comes is uncertain.
adv_u34.exc: wh-clauses 中语序为陈述语序：I wonder what she meant(不 what did she mean)。
mistakes: I don't know where is he → I don't know where he is。
Tell me what did you do → Tell me what you did。
The question is who will come? (embedded) → The question is who will come.

adv_u35.text: 第 35 单元 – 时态的报告和变换
adv_u35.note: 何时调整时间，何时不调整时间
adv_u35.exp:
Unit 35(Advanced)— 间接引语中时态选择：何时回移，何时不回。

回移(backshift)必需，当 reporting 动词为过去时，且内容绑定那一时刻或可能已不真：«I'm busy,» she said → She said she was busy(或许现已不忙)。

回移非必需，若：
  • 信息仍然适用：She said she is/was pregnant(仍怀孕)。
  • 这是普遍真理/长期事实：He said water boils at 100°C.
  • Reporting verb 为现在/现在完成/将来：She says/has said/will say she is tired.
  • 事件和转述间隔很短。

回移不可行：已是过去完成(无法再退)，及情态 would, could, might, should, ought to, had better, used to — 保持不变。

微妙语义：He said he works in London(示意：仍在)vs He said he worked in London(中性，不评论现状)。形式选择 — 转述者关于真实性的作者立场。

语域：新闻和学术文本偏严格回移；口语中说话人自由使用现在时呈现鲜活信息。
adv_u35.exc:
若信息仍真，可不回移：He said he is married。

过去完成不再回移。
mistakes: He said he had had left → He said he had left。
She told yesterday she comes → She said yesterday that she would come。
He said water boiled at 100 → He said water boils at 100°C — 普遍真理可不回移。

adv_u36.text: 第 36 单元 – 报告：建议、命令、意图
adv_u36.note: 她建议去/她建议我们去
adv_u36.exp:
Unit 36 — 转述建议、命令和意图。

现象：通过特殊动词和不同的补语形式转述第三方的意愿和计划。

结构：
  • suggest/recommend/propose + -ing 或 + that-从句（常用虚拟式/should）：She suggested going / She suggested (that) we go. — 她建议去。
  • order/tell/urge/advise/warn/beg + obj + to-inf：He urged us to reconsider. — 他敦促我们重新考虑。
  • say/claim + to-inf 表示意图：She said she intended to resign. — 她说她打算辞职。
语体：that 从句比动名词更正式。Recommend, advise 两种形式均可。对比：suggest 绝对不接 obj + to-inf（不同于 tell, advise）。C1 级需区分 advise sb to do（直接指示某人）与 advise doing（一般性建议）。
adv_u36.exc: Suggest 不接 to + inf 加宾语的结构：suggest me to go → suggest (that) I go / suggest going。
mistakes: She suggested me to leave. → She suggested (that) I leave / suggested leaving. — suggest 接 -ing 或 that-从句。
He proposed me a plan. → He proposed a plan to me. — propose 不接双宾语。
They ordered that he leaves. → They ordered that he leave / to leave. — order 后用虚拟式或不定式。

adv_u37.text: 单元 37 - 报告中的情态动词
adv_u37.note: 她说她可以/会/可能/应该
adv_u37.exp:
Unit 37 — 间接引语中的情态动词。

现象：转述为间接引语时，情态动词的时态回移并不对称：部分动词回移，部分保持不变。

回移规则：will→would, can→could, may→might, shall→should, must（义务）→had to。不变：would, could, should, might, ought to, used to, had better。

示例：
  • "I can help." → She said she could help. — 她说她能帮忙。
  • "You must leave." → He said I had to leave. — 他说我必须离开。
  • "It must be true." → He said it must be true. — 他说那必定是真的（认知性 must 保留）。
对比：表示逻辑推断的 must 通常不变；表示义务时变为 had to。话语与事件同时发生时可不回移：She says she will come。
adv_u37.exc: 义务性的 must 通常变为 had to；推断性的则保留 must：He said it must be true.
mistakes: He said he must go yesterday. → He said he had to go. — 义务性 must 变为 had to。
She said she can swim. → She said she could swim. — 间接引语中 can → could。
He told that he would come. → He said (that) he would come. — tell 须带宾语。

adv_u38.text: 第 38 单元 – 用名词和形容词进行报告
adv_u38.exp:
Unit 38 — 通过名词和形容词进行转述。

现象：用紧凑的名词和形容词结构转述他人的话语/立场 — 学术和新闻语体的典型手法。

结构：
  • 名词 + that-从句：claim, belief, suggestion, admission, denial, acknowledgement, announcement：The claim that he lied was unfounded. — 关于他撒谎的说法并无根据。
  • 名词 + to-inf：refusal, decision, willingness, reluctance, intention：Her refusal to cooperate surprised us. — 她拒绝合作令我们惊讶。
  • 形容词 + that-从句/to-inf：adamant that, reluctant to, insistent that：He was adamant that he was right. — 他坚持认为自己是对的。
语体：名词化提升正式度。对比：the fact that 后不加 of；advice/news 后只接 that-从句，不接不定式。
adv_u38.exc: the fact that 后不加 of：the fact of that → the fact that。news / advice 后接 that-从句，不接 to-inf。
mistakes: His refusal of help us → His refusal to help us — refusal + to-inf。
The claim of he was innocent → The claim that he was innocent — claim + that-从句。
An advice that he gave → The advice (that) he gave — advice 不可数。

adv_u39.text: 第 39 单元——that 条款中的“应该”；现在虚拟语气
adv_u39.exp:
Unit 39 — that-从句中的 should 与现在虚拟式。

现象：表示要求/重要性的动词和表达之后，使用特殊形式：should + inf（英式英语）或动词原形不加 -s（虚拟式，美式英语和正式用法）。

触发词：动词 suggest, recommend, insist, demand, require, propose, request, urge；形容词 essential, important, vital, crucial, necessary, imperative；名词 demand, recommendation, insistence。

示例：
  • I insist that he be present.（美）/ …that he should be present.（英）— 我坚持他必须出席。
  • It is essential that she not miss the meeting. — 她必须不错过会议（注意 not 位于动词之前）。
对比：insist that + 陈述语气 = 陈述事实（He insisted that he was right）；+ 虚拟式/should = 要求（He insisted that she leave）。虚拟式为书面、学术语体。
adv_u39.exc: 英式英语多用 should，美式英语用动词原形（虚拟式）：I suggest (that) he be present。
mistakes: It is essential that he comes. → It is essential that he come / should come. — essential 后用虚拟式。
I demand that she is here. → I demand that she be here. — demand 后用动词原形。
He insisted that she went. → He insisted that she go / should go. — insist 表示要求。

adv_u40.text: 第 40 单元 – 主语和动词之间的一致 (1)
adv_u40.exp:
Unit 40 — 主谓一致（第 1 部分）。

现象：复杂或形式上有歧义的主语如何选择动词的数。

规则：
  • 集合名词（team, government, family, committee）：英式英语按语义用单数或复数（the team are arguing = 成员个体），美式英语只用单数。
  • Neither…nor / Either…or：与最近的主语一致：Neither the students nor the teacher was aware.
  • 主语为不定式/从句：单数：To err is human. What matters is effort.
  • 距离、金额、时间作为整体：单数：Ten miles is a long walk. £500 is enough.
例外：news, mathematics, physics, politics, economics — 形式复数但用单数动词；police, cattle, people — 始终用复数。对比：a pair of trousers is / trousers are — 取决于结构。
adv_u40.exc:
The news / mathematics / politics — 形式复数但用单数动词。
Police / cattle — 始终用复数。
mistakes: The news are bad. → The news is bad. — news 为单数。
Police is coming. → Police are coming. — police 为复数。
Everyone are here. → Everyone is here. — everyone 为单数。

adv_u41.text: 第 41 单元 – 主语和动词之间的一致 (2)
adv_u41.exp:
Unit 41 — 主谓一致（第 2 部分）。

现象：本主题的延续 — 数量表达和不定代词。

规则：
  • A number of + 复数名词 = 复数动词：A number of students were absent. — 一些学生缺席。
  • The number of + 复数名词 = 单数动词：The number of applicants is rising. — 申请人数在增加。
  • 分数和百分比：与所指名词一致：Half of the cake is gone / Half of the students are here.
  • 不定代词 everyone, someone, nobody, each, either, neither — 单数：Everyone is ready.
语体：正式用 neither of them knows；口语中也见 know。对比：a lot of 和 plenty of — 按所指名词（a lot of water is / a lot of people are）。
adv_u41.exc: A number of + 复数动词；the number of + 单数动词。
mistakes: A number of students was absent. → A number of students were absent. — a number of + 复数。
The number of errors are rising. → The number of errors is rising. — the number = 单数。
Neither of them know. → Neither of them knows. — neither 正式用单数。

adv_u42.text: 第 42 单元 – 主语和动词之间的一致 (3)
adv_u42.note: 相关条款和复杂主题的协议
adv_u42.exp:
Unit 42 — 主谓一致（第 3 部分）。

现象：定语从句中的一致以及复杂数字/群体主语。

规则：
  • one of the X who/that + V 中：动词用复数，因为先行词是 X（复数）：She is one of those people who care deeply. — 她是深切关怀的那类人之一。
  • the only one of the X who/that 中：动词用单数：He's the only one of us who knows.
  • 数词作为总和/度量：单数：Three million pounds was raised. — 筹集了三百万英镑。
  • The majority/minority of：按所指名词：The majority of voters support…
对比：more than one + 单数动词（与语义相反）：More than one student has complained. 正式学术语体要求明确所指对象。
adv_u42.exc: one of the X who/that + V 中动词用复数：He is one of the people who work here。
mistakes: He's one of those who likes tea. → …who like tea. — 先行词为复数。
Either you or I is wrong. → Either you or I am wrong. — 与最近主语一致。
The team are winning the cup (AmE). → The team is winning. — 美式英语中集合名词 = 单数。

adv_u43.text: 单元 43 – 复合名词和名词短语
adv_u43.note: 停车场/延迟两小时/最先进
adv_u43.exp:
Unit 43 — 复合名词和名词短语。

现象：正式文本中的复杂名词结构。作为介词-限定结构的紧凑替代。

结构：
  • 复合词：car park, science fiction, bookshop — 拼写（连写/加连字符/分开）无统一规则。
  • 定语位置：第一个名词用单数，常带连字符：a ten-minute walk, a five-year-old child, a two-hour meeting.
  • 通过 of 后置修饰：a walk of ten minutes（不够紧凑）。
示例：
  • A state-of-the-art facility. — 最先进的设施。
  • Student accommodation problems. — 学生住宿问题（三个名词串联）。
语体：长串复合名词在报纸和学术语体中常见。对比：a woman doctor（类型）与 a doctor's office（所属）— 不同关系。
adv_u43.exc: 复合名词中，第一个词通常为单数：a two-hour delay，而非 a two-hours delay。
mistakes: A five-years-old boy → A five-year-old boy — 定语位置不加 -s。
A cars park → A car park — 第一个名词用单数。
An information centre（若指"信息之中心"）→ An information centre — information 不可数。

adv_u44.text: 第 44 单元 - A/an 和一
adv_u44.note: 我的一位朋友/我认识的一个人
adv_u44.exp:
Unit 44 — a/an 与 one。

现象：两者都可表示"一"，但功能不同：冠词 vs. 数词/强调代词。

区别：
  • A/an — 中性不定冠词：I need a pen. — 我需要一支笔。
  • One — 强调数目或区分：I need one pen, not two. — 我需要一支笔，不是两支。
  • One + 名词 = "某个/特定"：One day you will understand. — 总有一天你会明白。
  • A/an 在职业、度量单位前必须使用：She's a teacher. £50 an hour.
one 的惯用语：one day, one morning, one of these days, one and the same。对比：Wait a minute（中性）与 Wait one minute!（强调，不耐烦）。时间/价格表达中只用 a/an：twice a week，不用 twice one week。
adv_u44.exc: One 强调"就是一个"；a/an 中性：Wait a minute 与 Wait one minute（强调）。
mistakes: I have one brother and two sister. → …two sisters. — 数字大于 1 后用复数。
She is a my friend. → She is a friend of mine. — 不允许双重限定词。
One day or another → One day or other / some day. — 固定表达。

adv_u45.text: 第 45 单元 - A/an、零文章 (1)
adv_u45.exp:
Unit 45 — a/an, the 与零冠词（第 1 部分）。

现象：通过定指性、特指性和泛指来系统化冠词用法。

所指类型：
  • 特指 vs. 泛指：The tiger is endangered（整个物种）/ A tiger is dangerous（类别中任一代表）/ Tigers are endangered（复数泛指，最中性）。
  • 主题推进：a 引入新信息，the 延续：I saw a man. The man was tall.
  • 唯一的对象：the sun, the moon, the president (of…)。
示例：
  • The computer has changed society. — 计算机（作为发明）改变了社会。
  • She plays the violin. — 她拉小提琴（乐器用 the）。
对比：科学语体偏好复数零冠词（Tigers are…）而非 the tiger。地名：the Alps, the Netherlands, the UK — 需记忆的例外。
adv_u45.exc: 专有名词通常不加冠词，但：the Netherlands, the UK, the Alps。
mistakes: I love the nature. → I love nature. — 抽象概念不加 the。
She plays the football. → She plays football. — 运动项目不加冠词。
He goes to the bed early. → He goes to bed early. — 固定短语不加冠词。

adv_u46.text: 第 46 单元 - A/an、零文章 (2)
adv_u46.exp:
Unit 46 — a/an, the 与零冠词（第 2 部分）。

现象：抽象与不可数名词中的冠词使用；更换冠词导致的语义转变。

规则：
  • 一般概念 — 零冠词：Life is short. Love conquers all.
  • 具体化概念 — the：The life of a monk is hard. The love of money is the root of all evil.
  • 类型/表现 — a/an：She has a deep love of music. — 她对音乐有深挚的爱。
不加冠词的固定表达：at war, in love, by chance, on fire, at risk, in hospital, at school, by car。

示例：
  • Happiness is hard to define. — 幸福难以定义。
  • He felt a happiness he had never known. — 他感受到了前所未有的幸福。
对比：Man is mortal（正式泛指）与 Humans are mortal（中性）。
adv_u46.exc: Man/woman 泛指人类时不加冠词：Man is mortal（正式）。
mistakes: The life is hard. → Life is hard. — 泛指不加冠词。
Dogs are the man's best friend. → Dogs are man's best friend. — 固定表达。
The happiness is important. → Happiness is important. — 抽象名词不加冠词。

adv_u47.text: 第 47 单元 - A/an、零文章 (3)
adv_u47.exp:
Unit 47 — 主题类别中的冠词使用。

现象：机构、交通、疾病、餐食、乐器的固定冠词使用。

规则：
  • 按功能表示机构 — 不加冠词：in hospital（作为病人），at school（作为学生），in prison（作为囚犯），in church（参加礼拜）。
  • 同一地点作为实体对象 — 加 the：at the hospital（前去探望），near the school。
  • by + 交通工具 — 零冠词：by train, by bus, by plane，但 on the train, in the car。
  • 疾病：通常零冠词（cancer, diabetes）；传染病/小病 — a（a cold, a headache）；美式英语中 — the flu, the measles。
  • 乐器：play the piano；运动项目：play football。
对比：英式 go to hospital 与美式 go to the hospital。Go to bed（睡觉）与 sit on the bed（坐在床上）。
adv_u47.exc: Go to hospital（英式，作为病人）与 go to the hospital（美式 / 作为探访者）。
mistakes: He's in the prison. → He's in prison. — 作为囚犯。
I go to the work by bus. → I go to work by bus. — by + 交通工具不加冠词。
She has flu.（美式）→ She has the flu. — 美式英语加 the。

adv_u48.note: 参考意义和非特定意义
adv_u48.exp:
Unit 48 — C1 级的 some 与 any。

现象：选择不仅取决于句子类型，也取决于语义（所指 vs. 非所指）。

规则：
  • Some — 已知但未具体化的数量/对象：I read it in some book. — 我在某本书里读到的。
  • 肯定句中的 any = "任何，无一例外"：Any student can apply. — 任何学生都可以申请。
  • 问句中的 some — 期待对方同意/礼貌请求：Would you like some tea?
  • 问句和否定句中的 any — 中性：Is there any milk?
惯用语：some day, somehow, somewhere（对未来的不确定性）；anyhow, anyway（无论如何）。

对比：Pick any card（任一张）与 Pick some cards（几张）。If you have any questions（任何）与 If you have some questions（某些具体的，已产生的）。
adv_u48.exc: 肯定句中 any = "任何"：Pick any card。问句中 some 期待"是"：Would you like some tea?
mistakes: I don't have some money. → I don't have any money. — 否定用 any。
Is there some milk?（中性问句） → Is there any milk?
Anybody can't do it. → Nobody can do it / Anybody can do it. — 双重否定。

adv_u49.text: 第 49 单元 – 不，没有，也没有任何
adv_u49.note: 没有解决方案/没有一个/没有任何原因
adv_u49.exp:
Unit 49 — no, none (of) 与 not any。

现象：表示缺失的三种方式，语体和句法各异。

区别：
  • No + 名词 — 断然、强调，常见于标题和正式文本：There are no seats left. — 没有剩余座位了。
  • Not any — 中性口语等价表达：There aren't any seats left.
  • None — 独立代词，不带名词：How many came? — None.
  • None of + 定指名词短语：None of the students passed.
一致：none of + 复数 — 正式用单数（None of them was ready），口语用复数（None of them were ready）。

对比：No smoking（禁止）≠ Not smoking（没有在做某动作）。不可合用：I have no any money — 双重否定错误。
adv_u49.exc: none of + 复数 后正式用单数，但复数也可：None of us is/are ready。
mistakes: I have no any money. → I have no money / don't have any money. — 不可合用。
None of them was invited（口语） → None of them were invited. — 口语中复数更常见。
No of them came. → None of them came. — of 前用 none。

adv_u50.text: 第 50 单元 – 很多（的），很多（的），很多，很多（的）
adv_u50.exp:
Unit 50 — much (of), many (of), a lot of, lots (of)。

现象：数量表达在语体上差异很大。

语体梯度：
  • A great deal of / a large amount of / much — 正式（书面语）：A great deal of effort was required.
  • A lot of / plenty of — 中性、通用。
  • Lots of / loads of / tons of — 口语：We had loads of fun.
规则：肯定句中避免 much，改用 a lot of。否定句和问句中 much/many 为中性：Do you have much time? Not much. Many of + 定指：Many of the problems remain unsolved.

一致：a lot of water is / a lot of people are — 按所指名词。

对比：much 在带修饰语的肯定句中可用：so much time, too much noise, as much as possible。
adv_u50.exc: Much 在肯定句中少用：避免 I have much money → I have a lot of money。
mistakes: I have much friends. → I have many / a lot of friends. — 可数。
A lot of water are needed. → A lot of water is needed. — 与 water 一致。
Lots of informations → Lots of information. — 不可数。

adv_u51.text: 单元 51 – 所有（的）、整体、每个、每个
adv_u51.note: 细微差别、正式语境和口语语境
adv_u51.exp:
Unit 51 — all (of), whole, every, each。

现象：四个"全体"限定词，各有不同逻辑。

区别：
  • All — 整体集合，集合性：All the students passed.
  • Whole — 完整性，无一例外，强调统一：The whole class passed. / the whole day。
  • Every — 分配式，每一个无一例外（3+ 个元素）：Every student got a certificate.
  • Each — 个体化，常用于 2+ 元素并关注差异：Each student received a different task.
后置：They all arrived / They each received a copy（位于代词之后）。

对比：all day = the whole day（24 小时），但 every day = 一连串中的每一天。Each of them knows（始终单数）。Every 不接 of — 需用 every one of。
adv_u51.exc: All / whole：all the day（少用）与 the whole day。表时间多用 the whole。
mistakes: Every students → Every student — 单数。
Each of them know. → Each of them knows. — each 单数。
All of information → All the information / all of the information.

adv_u52.text: 单元 52 - 很少，很少，更少，更少
adv_u52.note: 更少的错误与更少的金钱（以及更少的错误）
adv_u52.exp:
Unit 52 — few, little, less, fewer。

现象：考虑名词类型和语义色彩的数量对立。

规则：
  • Few — 与可数复数连用：few friends（少，消极）。A few — 积极（一些）：a few friends。
  • Little — 与不可数连用：little time（少）。A little — 积极：a little time。
  • Fewer — few 的比较级：fewer cars。
  • Less — little 的比较级：less traffic。
示例：
  • He has few real friends. — 他真正的朋友很少。
  • She has a little patience left. — 她还剩一点耐心。
口语用法：less people 代替 fewer people 广为流行，但书面规范视为错误。few/little 的正式等价：scarcely any, hardly any。对比：quite a few（出乎意料的不少，积极）与 few（少）。
adv_u52.exc: Less 用于不可数；fewer 用于可数。口语中 less cars 常见，但不合书面规范。
mistakes: Less people came. → Fewer people came. — 可数。
A few water → A little water. — 不可数。
Little friends → Few friends — 可数。

adv_u53.note: 限制性和描述性的 Who/which/that
adv_u53.exp:
Unit 53 — 关系代词。

现象：区分限定性（defining）和非限定性（non-defining）定语从句，并选择代词。

规则：
  • 限定性（无逗号，必要信息）：who/that/which/whom/whose。宾格 whom 在口语中常省略。
  • 非限定性（有逗号，附加信息）：只能用 who/whom/which/whose — 不能用 that。
  • 限定性中可省略宾格的 who/that/which：The book (which) I read.
  • Which 指代整个句子（仅非限定性）：She was late, which annoyed me.
语体：whom 正式；who 作宾语口语化。

示例：
  • My sister, who lives in Paris, is a doctor.（我只有一个姐姐，补充说明）
  • The sister who lives in Paris is a doctor.（几个姐姐中的一个，区分）
对比：逗号改变陈述语义。
adv_u53.exc: 非限定性从句中不可用 that：My sister, that lives in NY → My sister, who lives in NY。
mistakes: The book what I read → The book (that/which) I read. — what 不能作关系代词。
The man which helped me → The man who helped me. — 人用 who。
My brother, that is a doctor → My brother, who is a doctor.

adv_u54.text: 单元 54 – Whose、when、by 和其他相关词
adv_u54.exp:
Unit 54 — whose, when, whereby 等关系词。

现象：扩展的关系词组，典型用于学术和法律语体。

词汇：
  • Whose — 所属（人或物）：A country whose economy is growing.
  • Whereby = by which：A system whereby students apply online.
  • Wherein = in which（极正式）。
  • Whereupon — "于是"：He nodded, whereupon the meeting ended.
  • When — 时间：The day when we met.
  • Where — 地点：The town where I grew up.
  • Why — 原因：The reason why he left.
语体：whereby/wherein/whereupon — 仅正式书面语体。

对比：where 已含介词 — 不可说 the place where I was born in。The reason why 口语化；正式用 the reason for which。
adv_u54.exc: Whose 也可指物：a country whose population is growing。
mistakes: The reason because → The reason why / that / for which.
The place where I was born in → The place where I was born. — where 已含"在"。
The day when we met on → The day (when) we met.

adv_u55.text: 单元 55 – 关系从句中的介词
adv_u55.note: 为谁服务的公司/为谁服务的人
adv_u55.exp:
Unit 55 — 定语从句中的介词。

现象：介词位置是区分正式和口语语体的关键标志。

选项：
  • 口语：介词置于末尾，用 who/that 或省略：The person (who/that) I spoke to.
  • 正式：介词置于 whom/which 之前：The person to whom I spoke.
限制：介词后不可用 who 或 that — 只能用 whom/which。介词在前时代词不可省略。

示例：
  • The company for which I work is expanding.（正式）— 我工作的公司正在扩张。
  • The topic we were talking about was interesting.（口语）
语体：含介词的短语动词须保留介词（look after → the child I look after）。对比：学术写作偏好 of which/in which/to whom；邮件和口语则将介词置于末尾。
adv_u55.exc: 正式：the man to whom I spoke；非正式：the man (who) I spoke to。
mistakes: The company which I work → The company which I work for / for which I work.
The person to who I wrote → The person to whom I wrote. — to 后用 whom。
The issue about that we talked → The issue (which/that) we talked about.

adv_u56.text: 单元 56 – 句子中的名词短语（第 1 课）
adv_u56.exp:
Unit 56 — 名词短语（第 1 课）。

现象：名词短语是正式文本中信息密集打包的关键工具。

结构：限定词 + 前置修饰语 + 中心名词 + 后置修饰语。
  • 前置修饰语：形容词（beautiful）、名词（car park）、分词（running water）、定语连串。
  • 后置修饰语：定语从句（…that I read）、介词短语（…on the table）、不定式（the first to arrive）、分词短语（people living abroad）。
形容词顺序：意见 → 大小 → 年龄 → 形状 → 颜色 → 来源 → 材料 → 用途：a lovely little old round red Italian wooden dining table.

示例：The recently published book on climate change by the leading scientist. — 这位顶尖科学家最近出版的关于气候变化的书。

对比：学术语体偏好名词化（the publication of the book）而非动词从句（when they published the book）。
adv_u56.exc: 正式语体中，名词短语可通过后置修饰变得很长。
mistakes: The book interesting → The interesting book. — 简单形容词置于名词前。
A man tall and dark → A tall, dark man.
The girl her hair is long → The girl whose hair is long.

adv_u57.text: 单元 57 – 名词短语（第 2 课）
adv_u57.exp:
Unit 57 — 名词短语（第 2 课）：后置修饰。

现象：定语从句的缩减形式 — 经济的学术工具。

后置修饰类型：
  • 分词：the woman standing there = who is standing there；the car parked outside = which is parked outside。
  • 不定式：the first person to arrive；the only one to notice。
  • 介词短语：the man in the blue shirt。
  • 同位语：my friend Anna, a talented pianist。
示例：
  • The issues discussed at the meeting remain unresolved. — 会议上讨论的问题仍未解决。
  • Letters to be posted should be left here. — 待寄信件应放在此处。
对比：the man running（现在主动进行）与 the man who runs（习惯性）。To be-不定式后置修饰表示必要性/将来性。
adv_u57.exc: 分词和不定式后置修饰：the man standing there / the first to arrive。
mistakes: The man who is sitting there → The man sitting there. — 缩减。
The problems which are discussed → The problems discussed.
Letters to posting → Letters to post / to be posted.

adv_u58.text: 单元 58 – 状语分词从句（第 1 课）
adv_u58.note: 离开房间，她……/不知道该怎么办……
adv_u58.exp:
Unit 58 — 状语分词短语（第 1 课）。

现象：通过 -ing 和 -ed 形式压缩从句。正式书面语的重要标志。

类型：
  • 主动 -ing（同时/结果）：Seeing the danger, she acted quickly. — 看到危险，她迅速行动。
  • 被动 -ed：Exhausted by the climb, they rested. — 攀爬后筋疲力尽，他们歇息了。
  • 完成 having + V3（前时）：Having finished the report, she left. — 完成报告后，她离开了。
  • 否定：Not knowing what to do, he called for help.
关键规则：分词短语的主语 = 主句主语，否则构成悬垂分词。

含义：时间、原因、条件、让步（由语境确定）。

对比：Being tired, I went to bed（原因）与 When tired, I get irritable（条件）。正式语体偏好分词；口语偏好完整从句。
adv_u58.exc: 分词短语必须与主句有相同的主语。
mistakes: Walking home, the rain started. → While I was walking home, the rain started. — 悬垂分词。
Being tired, the bed looked inviting. → Being tired, I found the bed inviting.
Not knowing the answer, the teacher explained. → Not knowing the answer, I asked the teacher.

adv_u59.text: 单元 59 – 状语分词从句（第 2 课）
adv_u59.exp:
Unit 59 — 状语分词短语（第 2 课）。

现象：高级模式：带连接词的分词和独立（absolute）结构。

结构：
  • 分词 + 连词：While working on the project, she discovered… / When asked, he refused. / If treated early, the illness is curable.
  • 独立结构（独立主语）：The meeting (being) over, everyone left. — 会议结束后，众人散去。/ Weather permitting, we'll go hiking. — 若天气允许，我们就去徒步。
  • With 结构：With the project completed, we can relax.
语体：独立结构多见于文学和官方语体。

示例：
  • All things considered, it was a success. — 总体而言，这是一次成功。
  • The sun having set, we headed home. — 太阳落山后，我们启程回家。
对比：having done 强调主句动作之前完成，doing 表示同时。
adv_u59.exc: 完成分词 having done 强调主句动作之前已完成。
mistakes: Having finished, he is leaving. → Having finished, he left. — 时态一致。
Finished the work, he went home. → Having finished the work, he went home.
Being arrived, we had dinner. → Having arrived, we had dinner.

adv_u60.note: 她自己/他自己/他们自己：特殊情况
adv_u60.exp:
Unit 60 — C1 级的反身代词。

现象：反身代词承担多种功能，常与 each other 和人称代词混淆。

功能：
  • 真正反身（主语 = 宾语）：She hurt herself.
  • 强调（加强）：I did it myself. — 我亲自做的。
  • 介词 by 后 = "独自/自己"：She lives by herself.
  • 惯用语：help yourself, enjoy yourself, make yourself at home, behave yourself。
不使用反身代词的情形：自我照料动词（wash, shave, dress, hide）通常省略反身代词：She dressed quickly.

对比：They love each other（相互，A→B 和 B→A）与 They love themselves（各自爱自己）。with 后：I took my keys with me，不用 with myself（除非强调）。Between you and me，不用 myself — 典型的过度修正。
adv_u60.exc: wash, dress, shave, hide 后反身代词通常省略：I washed，而非 I washed myself。
mistakes: She washed herself and dressed herself. → She washed and dressed.
I and my friend saw us in the mirror. → My friend and I saw ourselves in the mirror.
They talked themselves. → They talked to each other.

adv_u61.note: 一个作为代词/一般代词/另一个
adv_u61.exp:
Unit 61 — one/ones 作为替代词和泛指代词。

现象：one 有两种角色：替代可数名词和泛指"人们"。

功能：
  • 替代名词：I prefer the red one. / The ones on the left are mine.
  • 泛指（正式）：One should always be polite. — 人应当始终有礼。口语等价为 you。
  • One another = 相互（比 each other 更正式）：They support one another.
限制：one/ones 不用于不可数名词（this music，不用 this one）以及所有格之后（my book，不用 my one — 英式口语除外）。

示例：
  • Which jacket? — The blue one.
  • One never knows what may happen. — 谁也说不准会发生什么。
对比：学术语体偏好 one 泛指；口语用 you。
adv_u61.exc: One 作泛指代词属正式语体：One should be careful。
mistakes: I need a red one pen. → I need a red one / a red pen. — one 替代名词。
Give me the blue ones book. → Give me the blue one.
They helped themselves one to another. → They helped one another / each other.

adv_u62.note: 我想是这样/我希望如此/他们这么说
adv_u62.exp:
Unit 62 — so 作为从句替代。

现象：so 替代整个 that-从句，避免重复。

动词：think, hope, believe, suppose, expect, imagine, guess, be afraid, seem, appear, say, tell。

示例：
  • Will he come? — I think so. — 我想是的。
  • Is it raining? — I'm afraid so. — 恐怕是的。
  • So I've heard. — 我也是这么听说的。
否定 — 两种模式：
  • I don't think so（常用于 think, believe, suppose, expect, imagine）。
  • I think not / I hope not / I'm afraid not（hope, be afraid, guess, say — 只能用 not 否定）。
对比：Say so（确认）与 say that（引入新信息）。If so — "若是这样"；if not — "若不是"。不可用 I think it 替代从句。
adv_u62.exc: I'm afraid so / not；但不可 I'm afraid it。
mistakes: I think it. → I think so. — so 替代 that-从句。
I hope no. → I hope not.
I don't think so that he will come. → I don't think he will come. — 冗余。

adv_u63.text: 第 63 单元 - 这样做；这样的
adv_u63.note: 她立即这样做了。这就是他的愤怒。
adv_u63.exp:
Unit 63 — do so 与 such。

现象：do so — 动词短语的正式替代；such — 强调手段，尤其在倒装中。

Do so / do it / do that：
  • Do so — 正式，精确替代带宾语的动词：She was asked to sign the document, which she did so willingly.
  • Do it — 中性：Did you tell him? — Yes, I did it yesterday.
  • Do that — 带动作强调。
Such：
  • Such + a/an + adj + noun：It was such a beautiful day.
  • 倒装（极正式）：Such was his anger that he slammed the door. — 他的怒气如此之大，以致摔门而去。
  • Such as = 例如：fruits such as apples。
语体：do so 和倒装的 such — 学术和文学语体。对比：so + adj（so beautiful）与 such + a + adj + noun（such a beautiful day）。
mistakes: Such a was the noise → Such was the noise — 倒装中不加冠词。
He asked me to leave and I did it so. → …and I did so.
It was such nice day. → It was such a nice day. — such a + 可数单数。

adv_u64.note: 她会游泳，他也可以/省略号
adv_u64.exp:
Unit 64 — 助动词的省略。

现象：省略动词短语但保留助动词 — 文本衔接和口语自然度的关键。

模式：
  • 赞同 so + 助动词 + 主语：She likes jazz, and so does he.
  • 否定赞同 neither/nor + 助动词 + 主语：I don't smoke, and neither does she.
  • 对比：He works hard, but she doesn't.（省略 work hard）
  • 简短回答：Will you come? — I might.
倒装：so/neither/nor 后必须倒装：So do I，不用 So I do。So I do = 确认（"确实如此！"）。

示例：
  • I've never been to Japan. — Neither have I.
  • Tom passed, but Sam didn't.
对比：Me too（口语）= So do I（较正式）。be 之后 — So am I。
adv_u64.exc: So/neither/nor + 助动词 + 主语：So do I，不用 So I do。
mistakes: I can swim and he can too swim. → …and so can he.
She doesn't smoke and neither I do. → …and neither do I. — 倒装。
I'm tired. Me too am. → I'm tired. So am I / Me too.

adv_u65.text: 单元 65 – 避免 to 不定式
adv_u65.note: 如果可以的话我愿意来。
adv_u65.exp:
Unit 65 — to-不定式的省略。

现象：省略不定式短语但保留 to — 自然口语的关键。

结构：
  • 保留 to：I'd love to come but I'm not able to. — 我很想来，但做不到。
  • 条件和礼貌回避：I would help if I wanted to.
  • 回答：Are you going? — I'd like to.
常接 to-省略的动词：want, plan, hope, need, try, mean, intend, would like, used to。

规则：
  • 不可重复第二个 to：I'd like to, but I can't to → I can't.
  • can/must/will 等情态动词后 — 省略时不带 to：I can if you want me to.
对比：I don't want to（省略动作对象）与 I don't want（只是"不想"，无指代）。连贯对话中省略是必要的 — 否则答复显得刻板。
adv_u65.exc: want, would like, try 后可保留 to：I'd love to，而非 I'd love to come。
mistakes: I'd like to, but I can't to. → I'd like to, but I can't. — 第二个 to 多余。
He told me do it. → He told me to do it. — tell + to-inf。
Would you like? — Yes, I'd like. → Yes, I'd like to. — 保留 to。

adv_u66.exp:
Unit 66 — 形容词的位置。

现象：定语（名词前）和表语（系动词后）用法的限制。某些形容词因位置不同而含义不同。

分组：
  • 仅限定语：main, chief, former, mere, outright, sheer, nuclear, atomic：the main reason，不能说 the reason is main。
  • 仅限表语：afraid, alive, alone, asleep, awake, aware, content, glad, ill, well, unable：The child is asleep，但 a sleeping child（非 asleep child）。
  • 含义变化：the present members（现任）与 the members present（在场的）；a certain person（某人）与 I'm certain（确信）；my old friend（老朋友）与 my friend is old（年老）。
示例：
  • The late Mr Smith — 已故的（定语）；
  • He's late — 迟到（表语）。
对比：位置不是文体，而是语义。后置位置常见于固定表达（time immemorial, body politic）。
adv_u66.exc: Alive, alone, afraid, asleep, awake — 仅表语：an alive fish → a living fish。
mistakes: The afraid child → The frightened child.
The present members（意为"在场的"）→ The members present — 后置位置改变含义。
An asleep baby → A sleeping baby.

adv_u67.text: 单元 67 – 可分级和不可分级的形容词（第 1 课）
adv_u67.exp:
Unit 67 — 可分级与不可分级形容词（第 1 课）。

现象：形容词分为相对的（允许程度）和绝对的（已表达极端状态）。类型决定强化词的选择。

可分级：cold, hot, big, tired, interesting, important。强化词：very, quite, rather, fairly, a bit, slightly, extremely, incredibly。

不可分级：
  • 绝对：perfect, unique, impossible, essential, dead, alive, correct, wrong。
  • 极端：freezing, boiling, exhausted, starving, furious, terrified, delicious, brilliant。
  • 分类：nuclear, digital, annual, wooden。
不可分级的强化词：absolutely, completely, totally, utterly, quite（意为"完全"）。

示例：
  • It was absolutely freezing. — 冷得要命。
  • The film was very interesting.
对比：very cold（可分级）与 absolutely freezing（已是极限）。Quite 与可分级搭配意为"相当"，与不可分级搭配意为"完全"。
adv_u67.exc: Very 不与不可分级搭配：very perfect → absolutely perfect。
mistakes: Very delicious → Absolutely delicious. — 不可分级。
Absolutely good → Very good. — 可分级。
Very impossible → Completely impossible.

adv_u68.text: 单元 68 – 可分级和不可分级的形容词（第 2 课）
adv_u68.exp:
Unit 68 — 可分级与不可分级形容词（第 2 课）。

现象：强化词选择的语用学和 C1 级的典型固定搭配。

固定搭配：
  • Bitterly cold/disappointed — 极冷/极失望。
  • Highly successful/unlikely/controversial — 极为成功/不可能/有争议。
  • Utterly ridiculous/exhausted — 完全荒谬/精疲力竭。
  • Deeply concerned/sorry — 深切关注。
  • Perfectly reasonable/fine — 完全合理。
减弱词：a bit, slightly, somewhat, rather, fairly — 仅与可分级搭配。

语体：
  • 正式：highly, extremely, considerably。
  • 口语：really, pretty, dead（英）, well（英口语）。
示例：
  • The results were highly encouraging. — 结果令人鼓舞。
  • She was bitterly disappointed by the outcome.
对比：very unique — 书面规范中的禁忌，但口语中可见。Quite unique — 意为"相当独特"时可接受。
adv_u68.exc: 固定搭配严格：utterly ridiculous, bitterly cold, highly successful。
mistakes: Highly cold → Bitterly cold. — 固定搭配。
Absolutely tired → Completely exhausted. — 与不可分级搭配。
Very unique → Quite unique / unique. — unique 不可分级。

adv_u69.text: 单元 69 – 分词形容词和复合形容词
adv_u69.note: 无聊/无聊/众所周知/最先进
adv_u69.exp:
Unit 69 — 分词形容词和复合形容词。

现象：由动词和短语创造形容词修饰语的高产方式。

分词形容词：
  • -ing — 主动特征，引起情绪：a boring lecture（无聊的讲座 — 令人感到无聊）。
  • -ed — 被动/结果状态：a bored audience（感到无聊的听众）。
复合形容词：
  • 名词前加连字符：a well-known writer, a ten-year-old girl, state-of-the-art technology。
  • 系动词后不加连字符：The writer is well known.
  • 高产模式：N + -ed（big-eyed, left-handed）；adj + N + -ed（good-natured）；adv + V3（air-conditioned, purpose-built）；adj + V-ing（good-looking, slow-moving）。
示例：
  • A thought-provoking book. — 发人深省的书。
  • A soft-spoken diplomat.
对比：-ing 与 -ed 常被混淆：I'm interested（我感兴趣）与 it's interesting（这有趣）。
adv_u69.exc: -ing 描述情绪的原因；-ed 描述所经历的状态：boring film / bored viewer。
mistakes: I am boring with this lesson. → I am bored with this lesson. — 状态用 -ed。
The film was bored. → The film was boring.
A well-know artist → A well-known artist. — 复合词用 -ed。

adv_u70.text: 第 70 单元 – 形容词 + to-不定式、-ing、that 从句
adv_u70.note: 渴望学习/有能力/有把握
adv_u70.exp:
Unit 70 — 形容词 + to-inf、-ing 和 that-从句。

现象：形容词要求特定的补语；混淆会导致错误。

模式：
  • Adj + to-inf：able, eager, reluctant, willing, likely, unlikely, due, liable, keen, happy, ready：She was eager to learn.
  • Adj + 介词 + -ing：capable of doing, fond of reading, interested in learning, afraid of failing, tired of waiting, good at singing。
  • Adj + that-从句：sure that, certain that, aware that, convinced that, surprised that, sorry that, glad that。
  • 形式主语 it 的评价：It is kind of you to help. It is difficult for us to decide.
示例：
  • I'm afraid of flying. — 我害怕坐飞机。
  • He's keen to start / keen on starting. — keen 两种模式。
对比：sorry to + inf（关于未来的动作）与 sorry for -ing（关于过去的动作）。用 good at，不用 good in。形容词搭配的介词是 C1 级词汇要求。
adv_u70.exc: Keen on + ing，但 keen to + inf：keen on swimming / keen to learn。
mistakes: Capable to swim → Capable of swimming. — capable + of + -ing。
Interested to music → Interested in music.
Afraid to spiders → Afraid of spiders.

adv_u71.text: 单元 71 – 形容词和副词：细微差别
adv_u71.note: 他大声说话。他是一个大声说话的人。
adv_u71.exp:
Unit 71 — 形容词和副词的细微差别。

现象：许多词有两种形式 — 带 -ly 和不带 — 含义不同，这在 C1 级至关重要。

配对：
  • Hard（努力地）/ hardly（几乎不）：He works hard / He hardly works.
  • Late（晚）/ lately（最近）：She arrived late / I've been busy lately.
  • Near（靠近）/ nearly（几乎）：Come near / I nearly fell.
  • High（高，物理）/ highly（极为）：Birds fly high / highly respected.
  • Deep（深，物理）/ deeply（深切，情感）：dig deep / deeply concerned。
  • Wide（宽广）/ widely（广泛）。
系动词后用形容词：feel, look, sound, taste, smell, seem, appear, become, get — 用形容词，不用副词：She looks happy，不用 happily。

对比：good/well — 经典：He speaks good English（形容词修饰 English）与 He speaks English well（副词修饰 speaks）。
adv_u71.exc: Hard/fast/late — 形容词和副词同形；hardly, lately — 含义不同。
mistakes: He works hardly. → He works hard. — hardly = "几乎不"。
She arrived lately. → She arrived late. — lately = "最近"。
He speaks good English well. → He speaks English well.

adv_u72.text: 单元 72 - 比较级和最高级
adv_u72.note: C1 的比较：椭圆和特殊结构
adv_u72.exp:
Unit 72 — C1 级的比较级和最高级。

现象：复杂的比较模式：平行结构、强调强化词、省略。

结构：
  • 递进重复：more and more expensive, colder and colder。
  • 平行 the + 比较级…, the + 比较级…：The more you practise, the better you get. — 练得越多越进步。
  • 最高级 + 现在完成时：the best film I've ever seen。
  • 比较级的强化词：much, far, a lot, considerably, significantly, slightly, a bit, rather + 比较级：far better, significantly higher。
不规则：good/well → better → best；bad/badly → worse → worst；far → further/farther → furthest/farthest；little → less → least。

示例：
  • This is by far the most interesting book. — 这本书无疑是最有趣的。
对比：further（抽象 — further details）与 farther（距离，主要美式）。双重比较（more better）禁用。
adv_u72.exc: 以 -y/-ow/-er 结尾的双音节常用 -er/-est：narrower, cleverer。
mistakes: More taller → Taller. — 双重比较级。
The most best → The best. — 双重最高级。
More good → Better.

adv_u73.note: 超出预期/按计划/尽可能好
adv_u73.exp:
Unit 73 — 比较短语和从句。

现象：简化和省略的比较结构，用于经济表达。

结构：
  • 不完整：than expected, than planned, than usual, than before：The results were better than expected.
  • As…as：as soon as possible, as good as new, as often as not。
  • The same as / different from / similar to。
  • No sooner…than：No sooner had we arrived than it started raining. — 我们刚到就下雨了。
  • 惯用语：more or less, sooner or later, as good as it gets。
  • Rather than — 连词：Rather than complain, she took action.
示例：
  • She's not as clever as she thinks. — 她并不像自己认为的那样聪明。
  • It's more difficult than it looks.
语体：正式 — 完整 than I am；口语 — than me。对比：different from（标准）/ different to（英式）/ different than（美式）。
adv_u73.exc: The more… the more：The more he studies, the better he becomes。
mistakes: He is taller than me tall. → He is taller than I am / than me.
As soon as possible as → As soon as possible.
More expensive as → More expensive than.

adv_u74.text: 74 单元 – 副词的位置（第 1 课）
adv_u74.note: 副词在句子中的位置：开头、中间、结尾
adv_u74.exp:
Unit 74 — 副词的位置（第 1 课）。

现象：英语对副词位置有严格规则，并有细微的语义效果。

三个位置：
  • 句首：强调、评论：Suddenly, the lights went out.
  • 句中 — 实义动词之前，be 之后，第一个助动词之后：She always arrives early. / She is always on time. / She has always lived here.
  • 句末 — 方式、地点、时间副词的中性位置。
句中位置的规则：频率（often, always, usually, rarely, never），肯定（probably, definitely, certainly），焦点（just, only, even），完成（already, still, yet）。

示例：
  • He has probably forgotten. — 他大概忘了。
  • I only saw him yesterday.
对比：She's never late / She is never late — be 之后。简短回答中重音可改变规则：Yes, she always has.
adv_u74.exc: 频率副词 — 通常在实义动词之前，但在 be 之后：She is often late。
mistakes: I always am tired. → I am always tired. — be 之后。
She speaks always loudly. → She always speaks loudly.
He goes usually to work by bus. → He usually goes to work by bus.

adv_u75.text: 单元 75 – 副词的位置（第 2 课）
adv_u75.exp:
Unit 75 — 副词的位置（第 2 课）：多个状语。

现象：当句末有多个状语时，存在明确的顺序。

顺序：方式（怎样）→ 地点（哪里）→ 时间（何时）。

示例：
  • She sang beautifully in the theatre last night. — 她昨晚在剧院唱得很美。
  • He works hard at the office every day.
例外和细节：
  • 时间状语可置于句首：Last night, she sang beautifully in the theatre.
  • 位移动词：地点紧跟动词：She went home quickly，通常不用 quickly home。
  • 同类的多个副词 — 从一般到具体：every Tuesday at 3 p.m.
规则：直接宾语不被副词与动词分隔：She speaks English fluently，不用 She speaks fluently English。

对比：I always play tennis in the park on Sundays — 句中频率副词 + MPT。
adv_u75.exc: 顺序：方式 → 地点 → 时间。She sang beautifully in the theatre last night.
mistakes: I go every day to work. → I go to work every day. — 时间在句末。
She plays in the park tennis. → She plays tennis in the park. — 宾语先于状语。
He drives quickly always. → He always drives quickly.

adv_u76.text: 76单元 – 地点、方向、规律性和时间副词
adv_u76.note: 这里/那里/远处；经常/经常；最近/很快
adv_u76.exp:
Unit 76 — 地点、方向、频率和时间副词。

现象：按语义类别划分副词并说明各组特征。

分组：
  • 地点：here, there, everywhere, nowhere, somewhere, indoors, outdoors, abroad, upstairs。
  • 方向：home, away, forward, backward, up, down。
  • 时间：now, then, soon, recently, yesterday, today, tomorrow, already, still, yet。
  • 频率：always, usually, often, frequently, sometimes, occasionally, rarely, seldom, hardly ever, never。
here/there 后的倒装：带名词 — 倒装：Here comes the bus!；带代词 — 正常语序：Here she comes.

示例：
  • I've been abroad recently. — 我最近去过国外。
  • She rarely complains.
语体：hardly ever（口语），seldom（正式）。对比：home 和 abroad — 本身是副词，不需介词：go home，不用 go to home。
adv_u76.exc: Here/there 触发倒装：Here comes the bus（代词则不倒装：Here she comes）。
mistakes: Here comes he. → Here he comes. — 代词不倒装。
Recently I have see him. → Recently I have seen him / I have seen him recently.
I go there to home. → I go home. — home 已是副词。

adv_u77.text: 单元 77 – 程度副词和焦点副词
adv_u77.note: 非常/极其/甚至/只是/仅仅/几乎
adv_u77.exp:
Unit 77 — 程度副词和焦点副词。

现象：两个重要类别 — 一类修饰强度，另一类缩小指涉范围，且位置至关重要。

程度副词：very, extremely, incredibly, really, quite, rather, fairly, pretty, somewhat, a bit, slightly。位于形容词/副词之前：very quickly, extremely happy。

焦点副词：only, just, even, also, too, mainly, especially, particularly, merely, simply。其位置决定突出的内容。

位置变化的示例：
  • Only she told him the truth. — 只有她说了真相（别人没说）。
  • She only told him the truth. — 她只是告诉他真相（没做别的）。
  • She told only him the truth. — 只告诉他（不告诉别人）。
  • She told him only the truth. — 只说真相（不说别的）。
语体：rather 带负面搭配（rather disappointing）；quite 歧义（英式"相当"，美式"完全"）。

对比：fairly good（还行）< quite good（不错）< very good。
adv_u77.exc: Only/even/just — 位置改变含义：I only saw Tom 与 I saw only Tom。
mistakes: I almost never meet him.（更常见）→ I hardly ever meet him.
<s>Only he wants to help.</s>（意指"只有他"时）可能有歧义；需明确位置。
He is very asleep. → He is fast / sound asleep.

adv_u78.note: 显然/个人/坦白/不幸的是
adv_u78.exp:
Unit 78 — 评论副词和视角副词。

现象：这些副词修饰整个句子，表达说话者态度或限定适用范围。

评论副词（对情境的评价）：fortunately, unfortunately, surprisingly, obviously, clearly, naturally, admittedly, undoubtedly, hopefully, presumably, apparently。

视角副词（视角/领域）：personally, frankly, honestly, technically, scientifically, politically, financially, theoretically。

位置：通常位于句首，用逗号隔开；较少用于句中。

示例：
  • Frankly, I disagree with his approach. — 坦率地说，我不同意他的方法。
  • Technically, that's not allowed. — 严格来说，那是不允许的。
  • Unfortunately, the meeting was cancelled.
语体：对学术和商务写作中的含糊表达和作者立场定位至关重要。

对比：She spoke frankly（方式副词 — 怎么说的）与 Frankly, she's wrong（评论副词 — 作者态度）。
adv_u78.exc: 评论副词通常句首 + 逗号：Frankly, I disagree.
mistakes: Personal, I think so. → Personally, I think so.
Hopefully he comes. → Hopefully, he will come. — 用将来时。
Obvious, he is right. → Obviously, he is right.

adv_u79.text: 单元 79 – 时间状语从句
adv_u79.exp:
Unit 79 — 时间状语从句。

现象：带有细微差别的时间连词和铁律 — 其后不用将来时。

连词与细微差别：
  • When — 一般时间关系。
  • As soon as / the moment (that) / immediately — 紧接的先后。
  • Once — 完成后（= 在……之后）：Once you finish, we can go.
  • Until / till — 直到某时。
  • By the time — 到某时，常与完成时连用：By the time he arrived, we had eaten.
  • While / as — 同时。
  • Before / after — 先后。
铁律：用现在时代替将来时，用过去完成时代替 would-have：I'll call when I arrive，不用 when I will arrive。

示例：
  • By the time you read this, I will have left. — 你读到这个时，我已经走了。
  • As soon as she heard the news, she called him.
对比：疑问句中的 when — 可用将来时（When will you come?）。
adv_u79.exc: when, as soon as, once, until, by the time 后用现在时代替将来时。
mistakes: I'll call you when I will arrive. → …when I arrive.
Until you won't finish → Until you finish.
By the time he will come → By the time he comes.

adv_u80.text: Unit 80 - 给出理由：as，因为；为了和与
adv_u80.exp:
Unit 80 — 表示原因：as, because, since, for, with。

现象：五种手段，在语体和位置上各异。

区别：
  • Because — 中性，新信息，回答"为什么？"：We stayed in because it was raining.
  • As / since — 已知/显然的原因，常置于句首：Since it's late, let's stop.
  • For — 正式文学，新信息，只能置于主句之后：He was anxious, for she was late. — 他很焦虑，因她迟到。
  • With + 名词 / -ing — 伴随原因：With everyone watching, I got nervous.
  • Because of / due to / owing to / on account of + 名词/-ing：介词。
语体：表原因的 for — 书面语，近于古语；due to — 正式；because — 通用。

对比：because + 从句，because of + 名词。Due to 传统上作定语（delays due to fog），但现代语体中也可作状语。
adv_u80.exc: For 表原因为正式/文学用法：He was worried, for she was late.
mistakes: Because of he was late → Because he was late / Because of his lateness.
As/since 在句中有时听起来不自然；because 较中性。
With her smart, she succeeded. → With her being smart / Being smart, she succeeded.

adv_u81.text: 单元 81 – 目的和结果：为了，以便
adv_u81.note: 她离开是为了避免争论。
adv_u81.exp:
Unit 81 — 目的和结果。

现象：区分目的（意图）和结果（后果）的结构。

目的（purpose）：
  • To-inf（同一主语）：She left early to catch the train.
  • In order to / so as to — 较正式：He worked hard in order to succeed.
  • So that / in order that + 从句（不同主语，常用 can/could/would）：He whispered so that nobody could hear.
  • 否定目的：so as not to / in order not to：He left early so as not to be late.
结果（result）：
  • So + adj/adv + that：It was so cold that we stayed in.
  • Such + (a/an) + adj + noun + that：It was such a shock that he fainted.
  • So much/many/little/few + noun + that。
对比：so that = 目的或结果取决于语境：He spoke clearly so that everyone could understand（目的）与 He spoke so clearly that everyone understood（结果）。不可用 for + to-inf：I came for to see you → to see you。
adv_u81.exc: 否定目的用 so as not to / in order not to：He left early so as not to miss the train.
mistakes: I came for to see you. → I came to see you. — 不可 for + inf。
So that he can't hear（若为目的）→ so that he couldn't hear / so as not to let him hear.
For not being late → In order not to be late / So as not to be late.

adv_u82.text: 单元 82 – 对比子句：though, while, while, while
adv_u82.exp:
Unit 82 — 对比：although, while, whilst, whereas, despite。

现象：两类：让步（尽管）和对照（相反地）。

让步连词：
  • Although / though / even though + 从句：Although it was cold, we went out. Even though — 加强。
  • Despite / in spite of + 名词/-ing/the fact that：Despite the cold, we went out / Despite being cold…
对照：
  • While / whilst / whereas — 对比两种观点：While he prefers tea, she likes coffee. Whilst — 英式较正式。
连接副词：however, nevertheless, nonetheless, yet, still, on the other hand — 连接两句：It was cold; however, we went out.

语体：though 置于句末 — 口语：It's cold. I'll go, though.

对比：Although 和 but 不能合用：不用 Although it rained, but we went。Despite 要求名词短语，不接从句。
adv_u82.exc: Despite / in spite of + 名词/-ing，不接从句：despite he was tired → despite being tired / although he was tired。
mistakes: Despite of the rain → Despite the rain / In spite of the rain.
Although tired, but he went. → Although tired, he went. — 不用 but。
Whereas he is tall, his brother short. → Whereas he is tall, his brother is short.

adv_u83.text: 单元 83 – If 在复杂句子中（第 1 课）
adv_u83.exp:
Unit 83 — 条件句（第 1 课）：类型 0、1、2。

现象：三种基本的真实和假设条件。

类型：
  • 零条件（永恒规律）：If + 现在时，现在时：If you heat water to 100°C, it boils.
  • 第一类（真实将来）：If + 现在时，will/can/may…：If it rains, we'll stay in.
  • 第二类（非真实现在/不太可能的将来）：If + 过去时，would/could/might…：If I had more time, I'd travel more.
主句变化：will, can, may, might, should, must, be going to — 表达不同情态。If you see him, tell him（祈使）。

规则：if 后标准条件中绝不用 will。

示例：
  • If I were you, I would apologize. — 如果我是你，我会道歉。
  • Should you need help, call me.（第一类的正式倒装。）
对比：If you will 可用于礼貌请求（If you will excuse me） — 另一种含义。
adv_u83.exc: 零/第一类条件中 if 后不用 will：if you will come → if you come。
mistakes: If I will have time, I'll call. → If I have time, I'll call.
If he comes, I tell him. → …I'll tell him.
If I would be rich → If I were rich. — 第二类。

adv_u84.text: 单元 84 – If 在复杂句子中（第 2 课）
adv_u84.exp:
Unit 84 — 条件句（第 2 课）：第三类和混合类型。

现象：完全指向过去的条件，以及时间指涉不同的组合。

第三类条件：If + 过去完成时，would have + V3。未实现的过去：If I had studied harder, I would have passed. — 如果我当时更努力，就能通过了。

混合条件：
  • 过去原因 → 现在结果：If I had taken the job, I would be rich now. — 如果当时接了那工作，现在就富了。
  • 现在特性 → 过去结果：If she weren't so careless, she wouldn't have broken it. — 要不是她这么粗心，她就不会弄坏它。
语体：倒装的正式形式：Had I known, I would have helped.

示例：
  • If they hadn't warned us, we might be dead.（混合）
  • We would have arrived earlier if the train hadn't been delayed.
对比：第三类 — 对过去的完全遗憾；混合 — 区分原因和结果的时间。
adv_u84.exc: 混合类型：If I had studied, I would be a doctor now.
mistakes: If I would have known → If I had known.
If she didn't miss the bus, she would be here.（若原因在过去）→ If she hadn't missed the bus, she would be here now.
I would passed if I had studied. → I would have passed if I had studied.

adv_u85.text: 第85单元-如果我是你；想象他是...
adv_u85.note: 虚拟条件 was 和正式条件
adv_u85.exp:
Unit 85 — 虚拟式 were 和条件句的倒装。

现象：普通条件结构的正式替代，典型用于商务、法律和文学语体。

虚拟式 were：非真实条件中所有人称都用：If I were you… If she were here… If he were to… 口语中 was 也可，但固定表达 If I were you 除外。

省略 if 的倒装：
  • 第二类：Were + 主语 + to-inf：Were she to call, I would answer.
  • 第三类：Had + 主语 + V3：Had I known, I would have helped.
  • 礼貌条件：Should + 主语 + inf：Should you require assistance, please contact us.
固定表达：I wish I were… Imagine he were… As it were（可以说）。

示例：
  • Should the weather deteriorate, the event will be postponed.（商务语体）
  • Were it not for your help, I would have failed. — 若非你的帮助……
对比：倒装形式在正式交流中简洁而高贵。
adv_u85.exc: If I were you — 正式；if I was you 口语可。
mistakes: If I was the boss, I would change things.（正式）→ If I were the boss…
I wish I was taller.（正式）→ I wish I were taller.
Imagine he was here. → Imagine he were here. — 虚拟式。

adv_u86.text: Unit 86 – If not and unless;如果以及是否
adv_u86.note: Unless it rains / I don't know whether
adv_u86.exp:
Unit 86 — if not, unless; if 与 whether。

现象：常被混淆的连词，语义限制不同。

Unless 与 if not：
  • Unless = except if，提出唯一可能的例外：I won't go unless you come. — 除非你去，否则我不去。
  • 虚拟语境中如果指"若不发生 X"，不能用 unless：I'd be upset if you didn't come，不用 unless you came。
If 与 whether：
  • 间接疑问句中 whether（if 的替代，较正式）：I don't know whether/if he'll come.
  • 介词后 — 只用 whether：It depends on whether…
  • to-inf 前 — 只用 whether：I'm not sure whether to go.
  • 紧接 or not — 只用 whether…or not：whether or not she comes。
  • 作主语 — 只用 whether：Whether he comes is unclear.
示例：
  • Unless it rains, we'll have a picnic.
  • The question is whether they will agree.
对比：unless 加双重否定错误：unless you don't come。
adv_u86.exc: Unless = except if；不用于不太可能/非真实条件：Unless I were you → If I weren't you。
mistakes: Unless you don't come → Unless you come. — unless 已含否定。
I don't know if or not he will come. → I don't know whether or not he will come.
Whether you like, tell me. → Whether you like it or not, tell me.

adv_u87.text: Unit 87 - 如何组合复杂句子的各个部分
adv_u87.note: 然而/因此/此外/相比之下
adv_u87.exp:
Unit 87 — 连接词和文本组织。

现象：连接词保证文本的逻辑衔接 — 学术和商务语体的关键。

功能分组：
  • 添加：moreover, furthermore, in addition, besides, what's more。
  • 对比：however, nevertheless, nonetheless, on the other hand, in contrast, conversely, on the contrary（用于反驳，不等于"另一方面"）。
  • 结果：therefore, consequently, as a result, thus, hence, accordingly。
  • 举例：for instance, for example, such as, namely, in particular。
  • 总结：in short, in brief, to sum up, overall, in conclusion。
  • 时间：meanwhile, subsequently, previously, simultaneously。
标点：连接词之后通常需要逗号，之前用分号/句号：It rained; however, we went out.

对比：on the contrary（反驳）≠ on the other hand（另一观点）。Besides 比 moreover 口语化。Thus 和 hence — 正式，典型用于科学文本。
adv_u87.exc: However / therefore / moreover 通过分号或句号连接句子：It rained; however, we went out.
mistakes: It rained, however we went out. → It rained; however, we went out.
Therefore of the rain → Because of the rain / Therefore, we stayed in.
In contrary → On the contrary / In contrast.
