el_u68.exp:
Unit 68 — some 与不可数名词和复数的用法。

some 意为"一些、若干"，对应汉语"一点、几个"或零对应。

基本规则：

  • 肯定句中：I need some water. There are some apples on the table. — 我需要一些水。桌上有几个苹果。
  • 礼貌请求（期待"是"的回答）：Would you like some coffee? Can I have some sugar? — 要咖啡吗？能给我点糖吗？
  • 中性疑问句和否定句中用 any：Is there any milk? I don't have any money.

与单数可数名词不用 some — 那时用 a/an。在"某个"含义中，some 可放在单数前：Some man called you. — 某个男人打电话给你。此义中发强音 /sʌm/，而"一些"义中发弱音 /səm/。
el_u68.exc: 疑问句中的 some — 期待"是"的答复或提议/请求时：Can I have some water? "真正"询问存在的疑问句用 any：Is there any water?
mistakes: I don't have some money → I don't have any money — 否定句用 any。
Would you like any tea? → Would you like some tea? — 提议用 some。
some informations → some information — 不可数不加 -s。
Is there some milk? → Is there any milk? — 中性疑问句。

el_u69.note: 我看到一只狗。那只狗是棕色的。
el_u69.exp:
Unit 69 — a/an 与 the 的根本区别。

a/an — 不定指：听者不知道具体指哪个对象；众多中的一个、初次提及、类别中的任何代表。

the — 定指：对象从上下文、情境、共同知识或前文提及中对双方都明确；独一无二的对象。

  • I bought a book yesterday. The book is really interesting. — 我昨天买了一本书。那本书很有意思。（再次提及 → the）
  • Close the door, please. — 请关门。（听者知道的具体的门）
  • The sun is shining. — 太阳在照耀。（独一无二的物体）
  • She is a nurse. — 她是护士。（职业 — 总用 a/an）

the 的信号词：same, only, first, last, next, right, wrong，最高级，限定性定语从句（the man who called），复数地名（the USA, the Alps）。
el_u69.exc: 独一无二物体前总用 the：the sun, the moon, the world。即使是对说话者独特的事物，首次提及时也可用 a：I had a strange dream。
mistakes: I saw the dog in the street for the first time → I saw a dog — 首次提及。
Sun is shining → The sun is shining — 独一无二。
Close door, please → Close the door, please — 听者知道的具体的门。
She is the teacher（职业时）→ She is a teacher — 职业用 a/an。

el_u70.exp:
Unit 70 — 定冠词 the 使用场合的系统化。

当对象独一无二、由上下文限定或属于固定类别时使用 the。

  • 独特物体：the sun, the moon, the Earth, the sky, the world, the universe。
  • 由上下文限定：the kitchen（我们家的），the manager（这家店的）。
  • 位于 same, only, first, second, next, last 后：the same day, the first time, the last chance。
  • 与最高级：the tallest building, the best student。
  • 乐器：play the piano / the guitar / the violin。
  • 形容词作群体名词：the rich, the poor, the elderly, the British。
  • 某些地理名称：海洋、河流、山脉、复数国名（the Pacific, the Nile, the Alps, the Netherlands）。

例：The moon goes round the Earth. She plays the piano. He is the only friend I have. — 月亮绕地球转。她弹钢琴。他是我唯一的朋友。
el_u70.exc: 语言名称前不加 the：I speak English（但 the English language）。Next/last week — 相对于说话时刻不加 the；the next day — 在过去叙事中使用。
mistakes: play piano → play the piano — 乐器加 the。
She is best student → She is the best student — 最高级加 the。
on first day → on the first day — first/next/last/same 后加 the。
Moon is bright → The moon is bright — 独特物体。

el_u71.text: 71单元-：当你需要它的时候，当你不需要它的时候
el_u71.exp:
Unit 71 — 熟悉名词前省略 the 的情况。

当地点/行为采用其典型功能而非具体对象时，使用零冠词。

  • 按用途的场所：go to school / work / church / university / bed / hospital / prison。Tom goes to school every day.（他是学生。）比较 His mother went to the school to see him.（作为访客 — the）
  • 用餐：have breakfast / lunch / dinner — 不加冠词。但 the dinner (that) we had yesterday — 加定语后需加冠词。
  • 语言：speak English / French / Russian。
  • 体育项目：play football / chess / tennis。
  • 泛指的季节和月份：in summer, in July（或 in the summer）。

对比对：go to bed（去睡觉）vs sit on the bed（坐在具体的床上）；at home（在家）vs in the house（在房子里）；at school（在上学）vs at the school（在学校建筑那里）。
el_u71.exc: 短语 in bed 意为"睡觉/躺着"，而 in the bed — 字面意思"在作为家具的床上"。类似 at school（上学）vs at the school（作为访客在学校建筑）。
mistakes: go to the school every day（关于学生）→ go to school — 按用途使用。
have the breakfast → have breakfast — 用餐不加冠词。
play the football → play football — 体育项目不加冠词。
learn the English → learn English — 语言不加冠词。

el_u72.note: 广播/电视/电话/公共汽车
el_u72.exp:
Unit 72 — 日常用语中 the 的固定搭配。

作为固定短语记忆 — 一般规则不足以涵盖。

  • 带 the：the cinema, the theatre, the radio, the phone, the internet, the bank, the doctor, the dentist, the post office, the station。Listen to the radio. I saw her at the cinema.
  • 不带 the：television / TV（看电视），bed（睡觉），home, work，by 后的交通工具 — by car, by bus, by train, by plane, on foot。
  • 固定：on holiday, on business, on the phone, at home, at work。

例：I watch TV in the evening, but I also listen to the radio. — 我晚上看电视，但也听广播。They travel by car, not by train. — 他们开车出行，不坐火车。She is still on the phone. — 她还在通电话。

比较：in bed（躺在床上，睡觉）vs on the bed（坐在床上）；go home（不加介词和冠词）— home 用作副词。
el_u72.exc: Go to work（不加 the），但 go to the cinema/theatre（加 the）。At home — 不加冠词；但 in the house。这些表达需要作为固定短语记忆。
mistakes: watch the television → watch television / watch TV — 不加冠词。
listen to radio → listen to the radio — 加 the。
go to the bed → go to bed — 固定用法不加 the。
by the bus → by bus — by 后交通工具不加冠词。

el_u73.note: 英国、泰晤士河、法国、伦敦
el_u73.exp:
Unit 73 — the 与地理名称的使用规则。

不加冠词：

  • 大洲：Europe, Asia, Africa。
  • 大多数国家：France, Germany, Russia, Japan。
  • 州、郡、区：Texas, Yorkshire。
  • 城市：London, Paris, Moscow。
  • 单独的山和湖：Mount Everest, Lake Baikal。
  • 街道、广场、公园：Oxford Street, Trafalgar Square, Central Park。

加 the：

  • 通名/复数国名：the UK, the USA, the Netherlands, the Philippines, the Czech Republic。
  • 海洋、海、河流、运河：the Atlantic, the Mediterranean, the Thames, the Suez Canal。
  • 山脉和群岛：the Alps, the Himalayas, the Bahamas。
  • 沙漠：the Sahara, the Gobi。
  • 酒店、博物馆、剧院、电影院：the Hilton, the British Museum, the Odeon。

例：We flew from London to the USA across the Atlantic. We climbed Mount Fuji but not the Alps.
el_u73.exc: 不加 the：单独的湖（Lake Baikal），单独的山（Mount Everest），街道（Oxford Street）。加 the：沙漠（the Sahara），群岛（the Bahamas），酒店（the Hilton）。
mistakes: the France → France — 单数国名不加 the。
Alps → the Alps — 山脉加 the。
United States → the United States — 通名国家。
the London → London — 城市不加冠词。

el_u74.text: 第74单元-这个，这些，那个，那些
el_u74.note: 这本书/这些书/那辆车/那些车
el_u74.exp:
Unit 74 — 指示代词 this, these, that, those。

根据距离（物理或情感）和数量指示物体。

  • this（单）/ these（复）— 靠近说话者，"这个/这些"：This book is mine. These shoes are new.
  • that（单）/ those（复）— 远处，"那个/那些"：That car over there is fast. Those days were happy.

功能：

  • 作名词前定语：this pen, those people。
  • 独立使用：What is this? Who are those?
  • 打电话（英式）：Hello, this is Anna. Is that Tom? — 我是安娜。是汤姆吗？（美式：Is this Tom?）
  • 指代已说内容：That's a good idea. — 这是个好主意。

情感色彩：that 可表达疏远或不赞成 — I don't like that music. 搭配 this and that = "这个那个"。
el_u74.exc: 电话对话中：英式 Is that Tom? — This is Anna. 美式多用 Is this Tom? That 可表达情感疏远：I don't like that idea.
mistakes: this books → these books — this 只与单数连用。
that childs → those children — 复数形式。
This is my friends → These are my friends — 数的一致。
I like these music → I like this music — music 是不可数。

el_u75.exp:
Unit 75 — 替代词 one / ones 用于可数名词。

替代前文提及的名词，避免重复。

  • one — 单数：I need a pen. Have you got one? — 我需要一支笔。你有吗？
  • ones — 复数：These apples are bad. The red ones are better. — 这些苹果不好。红色的更好。

典型搭配：

  • 形容词后：the blue one, a big one, a new one。
  • this/that/which 后：Which one do you want? I'll take this one.
  • the 加定语：the one on the table, the ones I bought yesterday。

例：My car is old. I want a new one. — 我的车旧了。想要一辆新的。These cups are dirty; give me clean ones. — 这些杯子脏了；给我干净的。

不与不可数名词连用：不能说 I need water. Give me one. → Give me some. my/his/her/your 后不加形容词时不加 one：That's mine，而非 my one。
el_u75.exc: One/ones 不与不可数名词连用：不能 I need some water. Give me one. → Give me some. my, his, her 后不加名词时不加 one：That's mine，不用 my one（但口语中偶见）。
mistakes: I want a red one shoes → I want red ones — 复数形式 ones。
This bread is better than that one → than that — 不可数，不用 one。
the blue ones shirt → the blue one — one 替代名词。
Which ones you want? → Which ones do you want? — 需要助动词。

el_u76.exp:
Unit 76 — some 和 any 在句中的分布。

规则：some — 肯定句和礼貌请求；any — 否定句、疑问句和 if 后。

  • 肯定：There is some milk in the fridge.
  • 否定：There isn't any milk.
  • 疑问：Is there any milk?
  • 提议/请求（期待"是"）：Would you like some tea? Can I have some water?

派生词遵循同一原则：

  • someone / somebody, something, somewhere — 肯定句。
  • anyone / anybody, anything, anywhere — 否定和疑问。

特殊情况：肯定句中 any = "任何"（不受限选择）：

  • You can take any book you like. — 你可以拿任何你喜欢的书。
  • Anyone can do this. — 任何人都能做到。

if 后通常用 any：If you have any questions, ask. — 如有问题，请问。
el_u76.exc: 肯定句中 any = "任何"：Take any book you like. if 条件句中通常用 any：If you have any questions, ask.
mistakes: I have any money → I have some money — 肯定句用 some。
There isn't some bread → There isn't any bread — 否定句用 any。
somewhere to go? No, nowhere → 疑问句用 anywhere：Anywhere to go?
I don't know something → I don't know anything — 否定后用 any-。

el_u77.text: 77 单元 - 不，没有，没有任何
el_u77.note: 没有牛奶。他们都没有来。
el_u77.exp:
Unit 77 — 否定量词 no, none 和结构 not … any。

表达"没有/一个也没有"的三种方式：

  • no + 名词（强调）：There is no time. I have no idea.
  • not … any + 名词（中性）：There isn't any time. I don't have any idea.
  • none — 不带名词，作替代：How much money do you have? — None. — 你有多少钱？— 一点没有。

关键规则：英语中一句话只能有一个否定。如果动词已是否定，后面只用 any，不用 no/none。

  • I don't have any friends here. = I have no friends here.
  • There weren't any people in the shop. = There were no people in the shop.

None of + 限定词 + 名词：None of my friends came. None of the milk was fresh. — 我的朋友一个也没来。所有牛奶都不新鲜。none of + 复数名词后，动词可用单数（正式）或复数（口语）。
el_u77.exc: None of + 复数名词后，动词既可用单数（正式）也可用复数（口语）：None of us knows / know.
mistakes: I don't know nothing → I don't know anything / I know nothing — 一个否定。
There is none milk → There is no milk — 名词前用 no。
No of them came → None of them came — of 前用 none。
He hasn't no money → He has no money / He hasn't any money。

el_u78.note: 没有人来。我没有看到任何人。
el_u78.exp:
Unit 78 — 否定代词 nobody / no one, nothing, nowhere 及其与 not anybody 等的关系。

形式等价：

  • nobody / no one = not anybody / not anyone
  • nothing = not anything
  • nowhere = not anywhere

单一否定规则：nobody/nothing/nowhere 后动词为肯定。

  • Nobody knows the answer. — 没人知道答案。
  • Nothing happened yesterday. — 昨天什么也没发生。
  • There is nowhere to go. — 无处可去。
  • 比较：I didn't see anybody. = I saw nobody.

nobody/no one/nothing 后动词用单数，但指代代词通常用 they/their/them（性别中立）：Nobody brought their umbrella. Did anyone forget their ticket?

反意疑问句中，否定义后用肯定尾句：Nobody came, did they? — 没人来，是吧？
el_u78.exc: Nobody/no one/nothing 与单数动词一致，但指代代词常用 they/their：Nobody brought their umbrella.
mistakes: Nobody don't like it → Nobody likes it — 不双重否定。
I didn't see nothing → I didn't see anything / I saw nothing。
Anyone is here（作否定）→ No one is here — 肯定"没人"用 nobody/no one。
Nothing happened, didn't it? → Nothing happened, did it? — 反意疑问用肯定。

el_u79.note: 有人在吗？任何人都可以做到这一点。
el_u79.exp:
Unit 79 — 带后缀 -body / -one / -thing / -where 的不定代词完整体系。

形式矩阵：

  • 人：somebody / someone, anybody / anyone, nobody / no one, everybody / everyone。
  • 物：something, anything, nothing, everything。
  • 地点：somewhere, anywhere, nowhere, everywhere。

-body 和 -one 可互换，意义相同。some-/any-/no- 的选择遵循一般规则：肯定 — some-，否定/疑问 — any-，不带 not 的否定 — no-。

例：

  • Someone is knocking at the door. — 有人敲门。
  • Is there anything to eat? — 有吃的吗？
  • There's nothing in the box. — 盒子里什么也没有。
  • Everyone was happy at the party. — 聚会上每个人都很开心。

这些词后动词用单数：Everybody is here. 肯定句中 any- 表"任何"：Anyone can learn English. — 任何人都能学英语。
el_u79.exc: somebody/anybody/nobody 后动词用单数，但指代代词常用 they：Someone left their bag. 美式：somebody 和 someone 可互换。
mistakes: Somebody are waiting → Somebody is waiting — 单数。
I need nothing anywhere → I don't need anything anywhere — 不混用系统。
Is there somebody?（真实疑问）→ Is there anybody? — 疑问句标准。
Nobody didn't come → Nobody came — 一个否定。

el_u80.exp:
Unit 80 — every 和 all 的区别。

Every 表"每个"，强调个体单位，与单数名词和单数动词连用：

  • Every student has a book. — 每个学生都有一本书。
  • Every day I drink coffee. — 我每天喝咖啡。
  • Every + 派生词：everyone / everybody, everything, everywhere — 也用单数动词：Everyone is ready.

All 表"所有/全部"作为整体，与复数或不可数连用：

  • All students must come. — 所有学生都必须来。
  • I worked all day. — 我工作了一整天。
  • All the water was dirty. — 所有水都脏了。

对比：Every day（每天，365 次）≠ all day（一整天，从早到晚）。时间表达：every year / month / Monday 和 all year / month / Monday。every- 派生词总是连写，作单数代词。
el_u80.exc: All day/all night/all week — 不加冠词（= "一整天"）。Every day ≠ everyday（形容词"日常的"）：an everyday routine。
mistakes: every students → every student — every 后单数。
Everyone are happy → Everyone is happy — 动词用单数。
all the day I worked → all day I worked — 固定不加 the。
I go there every days → every day — 单数。

el_u81.text: 81单元-所有、大多数、一些、任何、没有
el_u81.note: 所有猫/大多数人/一些水/不知道
el_u81.exp:
Unit 81 — 量词 all, most, some, any, no 与名词及 of 搭配。

两种用法：

  • 不加 of — 关于某类的概括陈述：All birds can fly. Most children like sweets. Some people are lucky. I have no money.
  • 加 of — 代词前或带限定词（the, my, this）的名词前：All of us agree. Most of the students passed. Some of my friends live abroad. None of them came.

no 不能加 of — 用 none 代替：none of the books, none of us。

All 可在 the/my/this 前省略 of：all (of) my friends, all (of) the time。人称代词前 of 必需：all of us，不用 all we。

例：Some people like tea; some of my friends drink it every day. — 有些人喜欢茶；我的一些朋友每天都喝。Most animals sleep at night, but most of my cats sleep all day.
el_u81.exc: All 可在 the/my/this 前不带 of：all (of) my friends。代词前 of 必需：all of us，不用 all we。
mistakes: All of people → All people / All of the people — of 需要限定词。
Most of students → Most students / Most of the students。
All we → All of us — 代词前 of 必需。
No of them → None of them — of 前用 none。

el_u82.text: 第82单元 - 两者，要么，都不是
el_u82.note: 两姐妹/任一日/都没有选择
el_u82.exp:
Unit 82 — 两个对象的配对量词 both, either, neither。

含义：

  • both — 两者都/这个和那个（肯定）。
  • either — 两者中任一/这个或那个。
  • neither — 两者都不（无 not 的否定）。

形式：

  • 名词前：both sisters, either day, neither answer。
  • of + 限定词：both of the girls, either of us, neither of them。
  • 配对连词：both … and，either … or，neither … nor。

动词一致：

  • both 后 — 总用复数：Both are correct. — 两个都对。
  • either/neither 后 — 通常单数（正式）：Neither of the answers is right. 口语中允许复数。

例：Both of my parents are teachers. — 我父母都是老师。You can come on Monday or Tuesday — either day is fine. — 周一或周二都可以 — 任何一天都行。Neither book was interesting. — 两本书都没意思。She speaks neither French nor German.
el_u82.exc: Neither + 单数动词（正式）：Neither of them is right. 口语中允许复数。both 后动词只用复数：Both are correct.
mistakes: Both of sisters → Both sisters / Both of the sisters。
Neither answers is right → Neither answer is right — neither 后单数。
I don't like neither → I don't like either — 不双重否定。
Both my parents is → Both my parents are — 复数。

el_u83.note: 很多水/很多朋友/很多工作
el_u83.exp:
Unit 83 — 数量词 much, many, a lot of / lots of。

选择取决于名词可数性和句型。

  • many + 可数复数：many books, many people, many problems。
  • much + 不可数：much time, much money, much information。
  • a lot of / lots of + 两种均可：a lot of friends, a lot of water。

文体规则：

  • 肯定句中倾向 a lot of：I have a lot of work.（much work — 正式）
  • 否定和疑问中 — much/many：Do you have many friends? I don't have much money.

how, so, too, as 后 — 总用 much/many，不用 a lot of：How much is it? There are too many cars. I have so much work!

不带名词：He works a lot. Thanks a lot. 例：How many languages do you speak? — I speak three. How much time do you need? — Not much.
el_u83.exc: 肯定句中 much/many 听起来正式：He has a lot of money — 中性，He has much money — 不寻常。但 too much/so much/as much — 肯定句中正常。
mistakes: much books → many books — 可数。
many water → much water / a lot of water。
I have much friends → I have a lot of friends / many friends — 肯定句。
How many money? → How much money? — 不可数。

el_u84.note: 几个朋友/很少的时间/一些事情
el_u84.exp:
Unit 84 — (a) few 和 (a) little 的量词对比。

形式取决于可数性，意义取决于是否带冠词 a。

  • few + 可数复数 = "少，几乎没有"（否定评价）：He has few friends and feels lonely.
  • a few + 可数复数 = "几个，一些"（肯定）：He has a few good friends.
  • little + 不可数 = "少，不够"：We have little time — hurry!
  • a little + 不可数 = "一些，足够"：We have a little time — we can rest.

标志搭配：

  • very few / very little 加强"少"：Very few people came.
  • quite a few / quite a lot of = "相当多"（可数）。
  • only a few / only a little = "只有几个/一点"。

例：Can you lend me a little money? — 能借我一点钱吗？I understood little of what she said. — 我几乎没听懂她说什么。
el_u84.exc: Quite a few / quite a little = "相当多"。Only a few / only a little = "只有几个"。不能说 a few water — 不可数只用 a little。
mistakes: a few water → a little water — 不可数。
little friends（意为"小的"）→ small friends — 此处 little = "少"。
I have few money → I have little money。
There are a little chairs → a few chairs — 可数。

el_u85.note: 美好的一天/天空是蓝色的
el_u85.exp:
Unit 85 — 形容词（adjectives）：描述名词。

形容词指示特征，不随性、数、格变化：a big house, big houses, big cars。

两种位置：

  • 定语位 — 名词前：a beautiful garden, an interesting book。
  • 表语位 — 在系动词 be, seem, look, feel, taste, smell, become, get 之后：The garden is beautiful. She looks tired. This tastes good.

多个形容词的顺序：Opinion → Size → Age → Shape → Colour → Origin → Material → Purpose：a lovely small old round wooden dining table。

-ed / -ing 对：

  • -ed — 感受：I am bored / tired / interested / excited。
  • -ing — 感受来源：The film is boring. The news is exciting.

例：I'm interested in this interesting topic. — 我对这个有趣的话题感兴趣。She was surprised by the surprising result.
el_u85.exc: -ed 形容词描述感受（I'm bored），-ing 描述来源（The book is boring）。顺序：Opinion-Size-Age-Shape-Colour-Origin-Material（a lovely small old round wooden table）。
mistakes: a house beautiful → a beautiful house — 形容词在名词前。
I'm boring of this film → I'm bored with this film — 感受用 -ed。
happys children → happy children — 形容词不变化。
a red big car → a big red car — 先大小，后颜色。

el_u86.exp:
Unit 86 — 方式副词（adverbs）：回答"怎么样？"的问题。

构成：形容词 + -ly。

  • slow → slowly, quick → quickly, careful → carefully, beautiful → beautifully。
  • 以 -y 结尾：y → ily — happy → happily, easy → easily。
  • 以 -le 结尾：le → ly — simple → simply。

特殊形式（形容词 = 副词）：

  • fast, hard, late, early, high, low, near, far, daily, weekly。
  • He runs fast. She works hard. We arrived late.

例外：good → well。She plays the piano well.

配对意义改变：

  • hard（努力）vs hardly（几乎不）：He works hard ≠ He hardly works。
  • late（晚）vs lately（最近）。
  • near（近）vs nearly（几乎）。

副词描述动词（drive carefully）、形容词（incredibly beautiful）或其他副词（very quickly）。
el_u86.exc: Hardly ≠ hard：He works hard（努力）vs He hardly works（几乎不工作）。Late/lately：arrive late（晚）vs lately（最近）。Friendly, lovely, silly — 形容词，非副词。
mistakes: She speaks very good → She speaks very well — 动词后用副词。
He drives careful → He drives carefully。
She sings beautiful → She sings beautifully。
He runs quick → He runs quickly（或 fast）。

el_u87.text: 单元 87 - 比较级（旧 → 旧）
el_u87.note: 旧的、更贵的、更好的、更差的
el_u87.exp:
Unit 87 — 形容词比较级。

构成取决于音节数。

  • 1 音节：+ -er — old → older, cheap → cheaper, fast → faster。
  • 1 音节+辅+元+辅：双写 — big → bigger, hot → hotter, thin → thinner。
  • 以 -y 结尾（2 音节）：y → ier — happy → happier, busy → busier, easy → easier。
  • 2+ 音节：more + 形容词 — more expensive, more interesting, more beautiful。

不规则形式：

  • good → better, bad → worse, far → farther / further, little → less, much/many → more。

某些双音节允许两种形式：clever → cleverer / more clever, quiet → quieter / more quiet, simple → simpler / more simple。

例：My new car is faster than my old one. This book is more interesting than that one. Today is hotter than yesterday. — 我的新车比旧车快。这本书比那本更有趣。今天比昨天热。
el_u87.exc: -y 结尾双音节用 -ier：happy→happier, busy→busier。某些双音节允许两种形式：clever→cleverer / more clever; quiet→quieter / more quiet。
mistakes: more big → bigger — 单音节形容词。
more better → better — 不双重比较。
more happy → happier — -y 双音节。
expensiver → more expensive — 长形容词。

el_u88.note: 她比我高。比我想象的要好。
el_u88.exp:
Unit 88 — 用连词 than 作比较。

结构：A + 比较级 + than + B。

  • Tom is taller than Jim. — 汤姆比吉姆高。
  • This book is more interesting than that one.

than 后的代词形式：

  • 口语规范 — 宾格：taller than me / him / her / them。
  • 正式规范 — 主格加动词：taller than I am / than he is。

比较级前的加强词：

  • "明显地"：much, far, a lot, way — much taller, far more expensive, a lot better。
  • "稍微"：a bit, a little, slightly — a bit older, slightly cheaper。
  • 不能 very + 比较级（very taller 不可）。

数字比较：more than 100 people, less than an hour。平行递进：The older he gets, the wiser he becomes. — 他越老越聪明。It's getting colder and colder. — 越来越冷。
el_u88.exc: 加强比较中：much/a lot/far + 比较级（much taller）；a bit/slightly + 比较级（a bit older）。不能：very taller。
mistakes: taller that me → taller than me — 用 than，非 that。
very taller → much taller — 不用 very 加强。
more better than → better than — 不加 more。
She is tall than him → She is taller than him — 需要比较级。

el_u89.exp:
Unit 89 — 表达相等比较的结构 (not) as … as。

相等 — as + 形容词/副词 + as：

  • Tom is as tall as Jim. — 汤姆和吉姆一样高。
  • She runs as fast as her brother. — 她跑得和她哥哥一样快。

不相等 — not as … as（= 比 than 柔和）：

  • This hotel isn't as expensive as the other one. = The other hotel is more expensive.
  • Today isn't as cold as yesterday.

数量结构：

  • as much + 不可数 + as：I have as much money as you.
  • as many + 可数复数 + as：She has as many books as me.

相关表达：

  • the same as（与……相同）：My bag is the same as yours.（非 the same like）。
  • different from（与……不同）：Her answer is different from mine.
  • not as … as 的口语等价 — not so … as。
el_u89.exc: The same as（非 the same like）：My bag is the same as yours. 可以 as much/many as：I have as many books as you.
mistakes: as tall than → as tall as — 结构 as…as。
the same like → the same as。
She is so tall as me → She is as tall as me — 肯定用 as…as。
not as taller as → not as tall as — 原级形容词。

el_u90.text: 90 单元 - 最高级（最古老的）
el_u90.exp:
Unit 90 — 最高级（Superlative）— 特征的极限程度。

形式与比较级平行：

  • 1 音节：the + 形容词 + -est — the oldest, the cheapest, the biggest。
  • 以 -y 结尾：the + 形容词 + -iest — the happiest, the easiest。
  • 2+ 音节：the most + 形容词 — the most expensive, the most beautiful。

不规则：good → the best, bad → the worst, far → the farthest / furthest, little → the least, much/many → the most。

冠词 the 必需（有时用物主代词：my best friend）。

最高级后的介词：

  • in 与地点/群体连用：the tallest building in the world, the best student in the class。
  • of 在列举前：the youngest of the three brothers。

常跟现在完成时：It's the best film I've ever seen. — 这是我看过的最好的电影。

例：Mount Everest is the highest mountain on Earth. She's the most intelligent person I know.
el_u90.exc: 最高级后通常用 in（地点/群体）：the best in the world，但人/物前用 of：the youngest of the three。后接现在完成时：the best film I've ever seen。
mistakes: the most tall → the tallest — 单音节用 -est。
best student → the best student — 需要冠词。
the most best → the best — 不加 most。
the interestingest → the most interesting — 长词用 most。

el_u91.note: 足够好/足够有钱/足够老可以开车
el_u91.exp:
Unit 91 — 词 enough（足够）。

位置取决于词性：

  • 形容词/副词之后：fast enough, old enough, warm enough, well enough。
  • 名词之前：enough money, enough time, enough people。

结构 enough + to-不定式（足够……以便）：

  • She is old enough to drive. — 她够大了，可以开车。
  • Is it warm enough to swim? — 够暖可以游泳吗？
  • I don't have enough money to buy it. — 我钱不够买它。

备选结构加 for + 名词：This box is big enough for all my books. 带不定式主语：The coffee is hot enough for me to drink.

否定：not … enough = "不够"：He isn't tall enough to reach the shelf. — 他不够高够不到架子。

独立使用：Have you got enough? — Yes, thanks, that's enough.
el_u91.exc: Enough 在名词之前，但在形容词/副词之后：enough money, warm enough。偶尔 enough 放在名词后（罕见、过时）：time enough。
mistakes: enough old → old enough — 形容词后。
money enough → enough money — 名词前。
old enough for drive → old enough to drive — to-不定式。
She isn't tall enough for reach it → tall enough to reach it。

el_u92.exp:
Unit 92 — 词 too（太，过于）。

总带否定含义 — 特征"妨碍"做某事。

结构 too + 形/副 (+ to-不定式)：

  • It's too cold to swim. — 太冷不能游泳。
  • She's too young to drive. — 她太年轻不能开车。
  • He speaks too quickly for me to understand.

与名词 — too much / too many：

  • too much + 不可数：too much sugar, too much noise, too much work。
  • too many + 可数复数：too many people, too many mistakes。

对比 too vs very：

  • very — 中性：The coffee is very hot（但我在喝）。
  • too — 过分：The coffee is too hot (to drink) — 不能喝。

勿与同形 too = "也"（句末）混淆：I'm tired too. — 我也累了。

加 for + 人：These shoes are too small for me. — 这双鞋对我太小。
el_u92.exc: Too ≠ very：very cold（中性）vs too cold（过分，不能做某事）。Too"也"义放句末：I'm tired too.
mistakes: too much books → too many books — 可数。
too many water → too much water。
She is too beautiful（作赞美）→ She is very beautiful — too 带负面义。
too tired for work → too tired to work — to-不定式。

el_u93.text: 单元 93 - 句子中的词序
el_u93.note: 她总是在办公室努力工作。
el_u93.exp:
Unit 93 — 英语句子的基本语序。

标准模式：Subject → Verb → Object → Manner → Place → Time（SVOMPT）。

  • She (S) reads (V) books (O) carefully (M) in the library (P) every evening (T).

关键规则：绝不把状语放在动词和直接宾语之间。不能 She reads carefully books — 正确 She reads books carefully。

状语顺序：通常先地点后时间：I met him at the station yesterday. 但短时间状语可置句首：Yesterday I met him at the station.

例：

  • I drink coffee every morning at home. — 我每天早上在家喝咖啡。
  • He speaks English very well. — 他英语说得很好。
  • We went to the cinema last Saturday.

长宾语可将状语"推"向动词旁：She opened carefully the box she had been given.
el_u93.exc: 方式副词通常在句末：She speaks English well. 若宾语很长，副词可在其前：He opened carefully the box he had found.
mistakes: I like very much chocolate → I like chocolate very much — 副词在宾语后。
She reads every day books → She reads books every day。
He speaks very well English → He speaks English very well。
I saw yesterday him → I saw him yesterday。

el_u94.text: 单元 94 – 频率副词的位置
el_u94.note: 总是、通常、经常、有时、从不
el_u94.exp:
Unit 94 — 频度副词在句中的位置。

按频度降序：always (100%) → usually → normally → often → sometimes → occasionally → seldom → rarely → hardly ever → never (0%)。

位置规则：

  • 实义动词之前：I always get up at 7. She often visits her parents.
  • 动词 be 之后：He is always late. They are usually busy.
  • 第一个助动词之后、实义动词之前：I have never been to Paris. She doesn't usually drink coffee.

仅一个否定：He never smokes，而非 He doesn't never smoke。

位置灵活的副词 — sometimes, usually, often, occasionally — 可置句首或句末以强调：Sometimes I work late. / I work late sometimes.

Never, rarely, seldom, hardly ever — 只在句中；置句首需要倒装（正式）：Never have I seen such a thing.
el_u94.exc: Sometimes, usually, often 可置句首或句末：Sometimes I work late. Never, rarely, seldom — 只在句中（或句首倒装，较正式）。
mistakes: I go always to school → I always go to school — 实义动词前。
She always is late → She is always late — be 之后。
He doesn't never smoke → He never smokes — 一个否定。
Always I wake up early → I always wake up early。

el_u95.note: 我还在等。她已经离开了。
el_u95.exp:
Unit 95 — 副词 still, yet, already 及其位置。

still（仍然，依然）— 情况持续超出预期：

  • 位置：实义动词前、be/助动词后：I still live here. She is still waiting. He still hasn't called.

yet（已经？/ 还没）— 用于疑问和否定，句末：

  • Have you finished yet? I haven't finished yet. — 已经做完了吗？还没做完。

already（已经）— 用于肯定，早于预期；句中：

  • She has already left. — 她已经离开了。
  • I've already seen that film.

对比色彩：

  • still + 否定表烦躁：He still hasn't answered. — 他还没回答（本应回答了）。
  • not yet — 中性"还没"：Not yet, but soon.
  • 疑问中 already = 惊讶：Have you finished already? — 已经做完了？！

与时态：yet / already 多与现在完成时连用（英式）；美式允许过去简单时。
el_u95.exc: Still 在否定中表延迟不满：She still hasn't called（本应打电话）。Not yet — 中性"还没"。疑问中 already = 惊讶：Have you finished already?
mistakes: I haven't finished already → I haven't finished yet — 否定用 yet。
She already is here → She is already here — be 后。
Did you see him yet?（英式）→ Have you seen him yet? — yet 与完成时。
He still not came → He still hasn't come / He hasn't come yet。

el_u96.text: 单元 96 – 给予、借出、传递、发送、展示 + 两个添加
el_u96.note: 把书给我。 / 把书给我。
el_u96.exp:
Unit 96 — 给予和传递动词后的双宾语。

动词 give, send, lend, pass, show, tell, teach, offer, bring, buy, make, write 带直接宾语（什么？）和间接宾语（给谁？）。

两种可能的语序：

  • 不带介词：动词 + 间接（给谁）+ 直接（什么）。Give me the book. She told us the story.
  • 带介词 to / for：动词 + 直接 + to/for + 间接。Give the book to me. She told the story to us.

选择取决于焦点：

  • 代词作直接宾语时通常只用带介词形式：Give it to me（而非 Give me it）。
  • 长或新宾语置句末：She gave her friend a beautiful handmade scarf.

介词 for 与 buy, make, cook, get, find 连用：I bought a present for my mother. / I bought my mother a present.

例外：explain, describe, suggest, mention, introduce 只用带介词结构：Explain the rule to me（非 Explain me the rule）。
el_u96.exc: 动词 explain 和 describe 不能用不带 to 的双宾语：explain something to somebody（非 explain me something）。
mistakes: Give me it → Give it to me — 代词作直接宾语时介词必需。
Explain me the rule → Explain the rule to me。
She showed to me the photo → She showed me the photo / showed the photo to me。
He said me the news → He told me the news — say 不带双宾语。

el_u97.text: Unit 97 - 而且、但是、或者、所以、因为
el_u97.note: 我累了，所以我就去睡觉了。
el_u97.exp:
Unit 97 — 主要并列和从属连词：and, but, or, so, because。

  • and — 添加：I got up and had breakfast.
  • but — 对比：I'm tired but happy.
  • or — 选择：Tea or coffee?
  • so — 结果：It was late, so I went home. — 很晚了，所以我回家了。
  • because — 原因：I went home because it was late. — 我回家了，因为很晚了。

so / because 逻辑相反 — 原因和结果顺序相反：

  • 原因 + so + 结果。
  • 结果 + because + 原因。

标点：and, but, or, so 在并列复句中通常前加逗号。because 在句中时不加逗号；从句在句首则加：Because it was raining, we stayed in.

相关连词：although / though（让步）：Although it rained, we had fun.
el_u97.exc: So 和 because 相反：It rained, so I stayed home = I stayed home because it rained. Although/though — 让步：Although it rained, we went out.
mistakes: Because it was late, so I left → 任选其一：Because it was late, I left / It was late, so I left。
I was tired, because I slept → I was tired, so I slept — 是结果不是原因。
But however → 任选其一：but 或 however。
I like tea and I don't like coffee（对比）→ I like tea but I don't like coffee。

el_u98.text: 单元 98 - When 和 null 条件类型 (if)
el_u98.note: 下雨的时候，我呆在家里。如果你加热冰，它就会融化。
el_u98.exp:
Unit 98 — 连词 when 和零条件句（普遍真理）。

关键规则：时间和条件从句中不用将来时，即使意义是将来 — 用现在简单时代替 will。

  • When I arrive, I'll call you. — 我到达时会给你打电话。（非 when I will arrive）
  • If it rains, we'll stay home.

零条件句 — 始终成立的情况（自然规律、习惯、普遍事实）：

结构：If / When + 现在简单时, 现在简单时。

  • If you heat water to 100°C, it boils. — 把水加热到 100°C，它就沸腾。
  • When the sun sets, it gets dark.
  • If I'm tired, I go to bed early.

零条件句中 if 和 when 几乎可互换：两者都表"每当"。

与第一条件句的区别：零 — 普遍真理（If I eat fish, I feel sick），第一 — 具体未来事件（If I eat fish tonight, I'll feel sick）。
el_u98.exc: When 表将来 = 现在时：When I see him, I'll tell him. 零条件中 if 和 when 表普遍真理时几乎可互换。
mistakes: When I will arrive, I'll call → When I arrive, I'll call — when 后不加 will。
If water will boil → If water boils — 零条件两处都用现在时。
When I'll be ready → When I'm ready。
If it rains I will staying home → I'll stay home — will 后用不定式。

el_u99.text: 单元 99 - 如果：条件类型 1（第一个条件）
el_u99.note: 如果下雨，我就呆在家里。
el_u99.exp:
Unit 99 — 第一条件句：将来的真实条件。

用于可能或有可能发生的将来事件："如果 X 发生，Y 会发生"。

结构：

  • If + 现在简单时, will + 不带 to 的不定式。
  • 两部分可调换；从句在前时加逗号。

例：

  • If it rains tomorrow, I'll stay at home. — 如果明天下雨，我就待在家里。
  • She'll be angry if you're late. — 你迟到她会生气。
  • If you study hard, you'll pass the exam.

主句中 will 可替换为：

  • 情态动词：If you're tired, you can rest.
  • 祈使句：If you see John, tell him to call me.
  • be going to：If he doesn't hurry, he's going to miss the train.

条件部分 if 后从不用 will（礼貌请求除外：If you will wait a moment…）。if 的同义词：unless（除非），provided that, as long as。
el_u99.exc: 主句中 will 可换为祈使句或情态动词：If you see him, tell him / you should tell him. if 后不能用 will，请求除外：If you will wait a moment…
mistakes: If it will rain, I stay → If it rains, I'll stay — will 只在主句。
If I will have time → If I have time。
If he comes, I will to help → I will help — will 后不加 to。
I'll go if it won't rain → if it doesn't rain — if 后用现在时。

int_u01.text: 第 1 单元 - 现在进行时
int_u01.note: 我正在做/她现在正在工作
int_u01.exp:
Unit 1 — Present Continuous 描述此刻正在发生的动作、临时情境或已安排好的将来计划。

公式：(+) S + am/is/are + V-ing；(−) am/is/are + not；(?) Am/Is/Are + S + V-ing?

例句：
  • I'm writing an email now. — 我正在写邮件。
  • She is staying with friends this week. — 她这周暂住在朋友家（临时）。
  • We're flying to Rome on Friday. — 我们周五飞罗马（计划）。
  • The climate is changing rapidly. — 气候正快速变化（过程）。
信号词：now, at the moment, currently, today, this week, Look!, Listen!
对比：与 Present Simple —— 长期 vs. 临时（He works in a bank vs. He's working from home this month）。
int_u01.exc:
状态动词（know, want, believe, need, like, love, hate）不用于进行时。
例外：have 表示「吃/度过」（I'm having lunch），see = 「会面」（I'm seeing her tonight）。
mistakes: I am knowing the answer. → I know the answer. —— 状态动词不用进行时。
She works on a project now. → She is working on a project now. —— 此刻/当前需要进行时。
I'm wanting some coffee. → I want some coffee. —— want 是状态动词。

int_u02.text: 第 2 单元 - 现在简单
int_u02.note: 我工作/她工作/一般真理
int_u02.exp:
Unit 2 — Present Simple 表达恒常真理、定期动作、时刻表和习惯。

公式：(+) S + V(第三人称单数 -s/-es)；(−) do/does + not + V；(?) Do/Does + S + V?

例句：
  • Water boils at 100°C. — 水在100°C沸腾（事实）。
  • She teaches English at a university. — 她教英语（长期）。
  • I usually have coffee for breakfast. — 我早餐通常喝咖啡（习惯）。
  • The museum opens at 10. — 博物馆10点开门（时刻表）。
信号词：always, usually, often, sometimes, rarely, never, every day, twice a week。
拼写：第三人称单数 —— -o, -s, -x, -ch, -sh 后加 -es；-y → -ies（study → studies）。
int_u02.exc:
时刻表和节目表 —— 用一般现在时，即使谈及未来：The train leaves at 8.
第三人称单数必须加 -s/-es：she works, he goes, it does。
mistakes: She work in a bank. → She works in a bank. —— 第三人称单数漏 -s。
Do she like tea? → Does she like tea? —— 第三人称用 does，动词不加 -s。
The Earth is revolving around the Sun. → The Earth revolves around the Sun. —— 恒常真理用一般现在时。

int_u03.text: 单元 3 – 现在简单或现在进行时
int_u03.note: 我在这里工作。 / 这周我在家工作。
int_u03.exp:
Unit 3 — 一般现在时和现在进行时的选择：长期 vs. 临时。

规则：Present Simple —— 用于总体/长期状态（工作、住所、习惯）。Present Continuous —— 用于此刻或这段时期正在发生的事（临时变化、进行中的过程）。

例句：
  • Tom lives in London, but he's living in Berlin at the moment. — Tom 住在伦敦，但目前暂住柏林。
  • Prices are rising very fast. — 物价正快速上涨（当下过程）。
  • Water freezes at 0°C. — 水在0°C结冰（自然规律）。
特殊情形 —— 情绪：always/constantly + 进行时 表达恼怒：He's always losing his keys! —— 他老是丢钥匙！
关键问题：「这是普遍真理还是正好现在/临时发生？」
int_u03.exc:
状态动词永远用一般时，即便是「现在」：I know it now.
Always + 进行时 = 恼怒：He's always complaining!
mistakes: She lives with her parents this month. → She is living with her parents this month. —— 临时情境。
I'm usually going to work by bus. → I usually go to work by bus. —— 定期动作用一般时。
What you are doing? → What are you doing? —— 疑问句倒装 are you。

int_u04.text: 第 4 单元 - 静态动词：非连续动词
int_u04.note: 我知道/我明白/我想要（不是：我知道）
int_u04.exp:
Unit 4 — 状态动词（stative verbs）通常不用于进行时。

状态动词分组：
  • 思维/意见：know, believe, think (=认为), understand, remember, suppose, mean
  • 情感：like, love, hate, prefer, want, need, wish
  • 感知：see, hear, smell, taste, seem, appear
  • 拥有/关系：have (=owe), own, belong, contain, consist, depend
例句：
  • I know what you mean. — 我懂你的意思。
  • This soup tastes great. — 这汤味道很好。
双重含义：think —— 意见（一般时）vs. 思考过程（进行时）；have —— 拥有（一般时）vs. 「度过/吃」（having lunch/a shower —— 进行时）；see —— 看见（一般时）vs. 约会（I'm seeing John tonight）。
int_u04.exc:
Think、have、see 含义会变：I'm thinking（思考）vs. I think（认为）。I'm having a bath（正在洗）vs. I have a car（拥有）。
Feel 和 look 描述感受/外表 —— 两种形式皆可：I feel / I'm feeling tired.
mistakes: I am understanding now. → I understand now. —— understand 是状态动词。
Are you believing me? → Do you believe me? —— believe 不用进行时。
This dress is belonging to her. → This dress belongs to her. —— belong 是状态。

int_u05.note: 我工作了/你去了吗？ /我没看到
int_u05.exp:
Unit 5 — Past Simple 表示过去特定时刻完成的动作。

公式：(+) 规则动词：V+ed，不规则动词：V2；(−) did not (didn't) + V1；(?) Did + S + V1?

例句：
  • We met in 2015. — 我们在2015年相识。
  • She didn't call yesterday. — 她昨天没打电话。
  • Did you enjoy the film? — 你喜欢这部电影吗？
  • Columbus discovered America in 1492. — 哥伦布于1492年发现美洲。
信号词：yesterday, last week/year, in 2010, ago, when I was young, then。
-ed 拼写：studied, stopped, liked（双写/软化）。重要：did 后动词始终为 V1，不加 -ed。
int_u05.exc:
不规则动词（go/went, see/saw, take/took）须背熟。
在 did 的疑问/否定中，主动词用 V1：Did you go?（不是 Did you went?）。
mistakes: I goed home. → I went home. —— go 是不规则动词。
Did you saw the film? → Did you see the film? —— did 后用不带 to 的不定式。
She didn't knew. → She didn't know. —— 否定用 didn't + V1。

int_u06.text: 第六单元 - 过去进行时
int_u06.note: 你打电话的时候我正在读书。
int_u06.exp:
Unit 6 — Past Continuous 描述过去某一特定时刻正在持续的动作，或被打断的背景动作。

公式：was/were + V-ing。

用法：
  • 某时刻持续：At 8 p.m. I was having dinner. — 晚8点我在吃晚饭。
  • 被打断的事件：I was reading when the lights went out. — 我正在读书时灯灭了。
  • 并行过程：While he was cooking, she was setting the table. — 他做饭时她在摆餐桌。
  • 叙述背景：The sun was shining and birds were singing.
与 Past Simple 对比：Past Simple —— 短暂完成的事件；Past Continuous —— 持续的背景（when/while + 背景，短暂事件用一般时）。
int_u06.exc:
状态动词不用过去进行时：I knew（不是 I was knowing）。
背景与打断事件：Past Continuous + Past Simple。
mistakes: I was knowing him well. → I knew him well. —— know 是状态。
When she called, I cooked. → When she called, I was cooking. —— 持续背景用进行时。
I was watching TV while I did homework. → I was watching TV while I was doing homework. —— 两个并行过程。

int_u07.text: 第七单元 – 现在完成时（我已经完成了）
int_u07.note: 我已经完成了。她已经离开了。
int_u07.exp:
Unit 7 — Present Perfect 将过去动作与当下时刻相连：重要的是结果，而非何时发生。

公式：have/has + V3 (过去分词)。

三种含义：
  • 当下结果：I've lost my keys（现在找不到）。
  • 人生经历：Have you ever been to Japan? — 你去过日本吗？
  • 新闻/近期事件：The President has resigned. — 总统辞职了。
例句：
  • She has written three books. — 到目前她写了三本书。
  • The price has gone up. — 价格涨了。
重要：不与具体过去时间连用（yesterday, ago, in 1990）。对比：I've seen this film（经历）vs. I saw it last night（事实）。
int_u07.exc:
与 yesterday, ago, last week, in 2010 连用 —— 只用 Past Simple。
与 today, this week, this morning（若时段未结束）—— 用 Present Perfect。
mistakes: I have seen him yesterday. → I saw him yesterday. —— 具体过去用 Past Simple。
She has went home. → She has gone home. —— 需要 V3（gone）。
I finished my homework (结果重要). → I have finished my homework. —— 现在的结果。

int_u08.text: 第 8 单元 - 现在完成时（第 2 部分）：更多示例
int_u08.note: 只是、已经、然而、曾经、从未
int_u08.exp:
Unit 8 — Present Perfect 与典型副词：just, already, yet, ever, never, recently, lately。

含义及位置：
  • just（刚刚）—— 位于 have 与 V3 之间：She has just left. — 她刚走。
  • already（已经，比预期早）—— 位于 have 与 V3 之间：I've already eaten. — 我已经吃过了。
  • yet（还/已经）—— 位于否定/疑问句末：Has he arrived yet?
  • ever/never（曾经/从未）：Have you ever tried sushi? / I've never smoked.
  • recently/lately：I haven't seen him lately.
英式 vs. 美式：英式 —— Present Perfect；美式中 just/already/yet 常用 Past Simple：Did you eat yet?
int_u08.exc:
Yet —— 疑问/否定句末。Already —— have 与 V3 之间（或句末表惊讶）。Just —— have 与 V3 之间。
美式英语中 just/already/yet 常用 Past Simple：Did you eat yet?
mistakes: I have yet finished. → I haven't finished yet. —— yet 用于否定/疑问。
Have you ever been to Paris? Yes, I have been there last year. → Yes, I went there last year. —— 具体时间 → Past Simple。
She has already went. → She has already gone. —— 需要 V3。

int_u09.text: 单元 9 - 现在完成进行时
int_u09.note: 我已经工作了一整天了。
int_u09.exp:
Unit 9 — Present Perfect Continuous 强调从过去开始持续至今（或刚结束而有明显结果）的动作长度。

公式：have/has + been + V-ing。

含义：
  • 过程延续至今：She has been studying for three hours. — 她已学习三小时了。
  • 明显结果：You look tired — have you been running? — 你看起来累了 —— 你刚跑步了吗？
  • 近期结束：It's been raining（地面湿）。
信号词：for, since, all day/morning, how long, recently, lately。
重要：状态动词（know, love, have=拥有）不用进行时 —— 用 Present Perfect：I've known him for years.
int_u09.exc: 状态动词不用进行时 —— 用 Present Perfect：I've known her for years（不是 I've been knowing）。
mistakes: I am working here for 5 years. → I have been working here for 5 years. —— 与 for/since 连用需完成时。
I've been knowing him since 2010. → I've known him since 2010. —— know 是状态。
How long you have been waiting? → How long have you been waiting? —— 需倒装。

int_u10.text: 单元 10 – 现在完成时与现在完成进行时
int_u10.note: 我已经写过/我一直在写
int_u10.exp:
Unit 10 — Present Perfect 与 Present Perfect Continuous 的选择：结果 vs. 过程。

Present Perfect（have+V3）—— 做了多少/什么结果，动作完成：I've read 50 pages. — 我读了50页。
Present Perfect Continuous（have been+V-ing）—— 过程持续多久，强调时长：I've been reading all morning. — 我读了一上午。

对比例句：
  • She's painted the kitchen（完成）vs. She's been painting the kitchen（过程，可能未完成）。
  • I've written three emails（数量）vs. I've been writing emails（过程投入）。
与 live, work, teach, study 连用：两种形式常可互换，意义无别：I've lived / I've been living here for 5 years.
与数量（how many, three times）连用 —— 只用 Perfect。
int_u10.exc: 与 live, work, study, teach 连用时，Perfect 和 Perfect Continuous 常等同：I've lived / I've been living here for 5 years.
mistakes: I've been reading three books this month. → I've read three books this month. —— 可数结果用 Perfect。
She's painted the wall all morning. → She's been painting the wall all morning. —— 未完成的过程。
How many times have you been visiting Rome? → How many times have you visited Rome? —— 次数用 Perfect。

int_u100.text: 第 100 单元 - 以 -ly 结尾的副词
int_u100.exp:
Unit 100 — -ly 副词的构成和使用。

基本规则：形容词 + -ly → 副词：quick→quickly, careful→carefully。拼写变化：y→ily (happy→happily), -le→-ly (simple→simply)。
例外（同形）：fast, hard, early, late, high, low, straight — 形容词 = 副词。
  • She drives fast. It's a fast car.
  • He works hard. It's a hard job.
假朋友：
  • hardly = 几乎不（不是「艰难」）；
  • lately = 最近（不是「晚」）；
  • highly = 非常（不是「高」）；
  • nearly = 几乎。
Good → well：She sings well，不用 good。但：I feel good/well — 两者都可。
以 -ly 结尾的形容词（非副词！）：friendly, lovely, lonely, silly, ugly。副词表达需描述性：in a friendly way。

方式副词位置 — 通常在动词/宾语之后。
int_u100.exc: 以 -ly 结尾的形容词：friendly, lovely, lonely, silly — 非副词！副词描述性构成：in a friendly way。
mistakes: She sings good. → She sings well. — well 是 good 的副词。
He works hardly. → He works hard. — hardly = 几乎不。
She spoke friendly. → spoke in a friendly way — friendly 是形容词，非副词。

int_u101.text: 单元 101 – 形容词和副词：well、fast、late、hard
int_u101.note: 这是一辆快车。她开车开得很快。
int_u101.exp:
Unit 101 — 形容词和副词同形与异形对。

同形（形 = 副）：fast, hard, late, early, high, low, near, deep, straight。
  • It's a fast train. The train goes fast.
  • She arrived late. It was a late arrival.
不同含义的对：
  • hard（努力，艰难）vs. hardly（几乎不）：I work hard. I can hardly hear you.
  • late（晚）vs. lately（最近）：She arrived late. I haven't seen him lately.
  • high（高）vs. highly（非常）：The bird flew high. She is highly qualified.
  • near（近）vs. nearly（几乎）：Come near. I nearly fell.
  • free（免费）vs. freely（自由地）：Children travel free. Speak freely.

语境决定含义：hardly 有负面语气，接近 almost not。
int_u101.exc: Lately = 最近，近来（不是「晚」）。Late = 晚。
mistakes: I haven't seen him late. → I haven't seen him lately. — 「最近」。
I can hard hear you. → I can hardly hear you. — 「几乎不」用 hardly。
He arrived lately.（= 晚）→ He arrived late. — 「晚」用 late。

int_u102.note: 这么冷/天气这么冷/这么多
int_u102.exp:
Unit 102 — 加强语气的 so 和 such。

So — 位于形容词或副词前（无名词）：
  • It's so cold. — 太冷了。
  • She speaks so quickly.
  • so much / so many：I have so much work.
Such — 位于（冠词和）名词前，可带形容词：
  • such a + adj + 可数单数：such a cold day, such a nice person；
  • such + adj + 复数/不可数：such nice people, such good weather。
结果从句：
  • so + adj/adv + that：It was so cold that we left.
  • such a + adj + noun + that：It was such a cold day that we left.
比较：It was so cold = It was such cold weather。

记住：名词前 — such；「光」形容词前 — so。
int_u102.exc: Such a — 与可数单数：such a cold day。不带 a 与复数和不可数：such good ideas, such good food。
mistakes: It was so a cold day. → It was such a cold day. — 有名词用 such。
Such cold（无名词）→ so cold — 光形容词前只用 so。
Such a good weather → such good weather — weather 不可数，不用 a。

int_u103.exp:
Unit 103 — enough（足够）和 too（太）。

Enough：
  • 形容词/副词后：warm enough, fast enough；
  • 名词前：enough money, enough time；
  • 结构：adj/noun + enough + to-inf：She's old enough to drive. We have enough money to buy it.
Too：
  • 形容词/副词前：too tired, too quickly；
  • 结构：too + adj/adv + to-inf：He's too young to vote. It's too late to call.；
  • too + adj + for somebody：It's too difficult for me.
含义对比：
  • enough — 正面的足够：一切正常；
  • too — 过量，妨碍做事：负面语气。
Very vs. too：very cold — 非常冷（事实）；too cold — 太冷了（妨碍行动）。

勿混：too ≠ very；enough money，但 old enough（位置不同）。
int_u103.exc: Enough 在形容词之后，但在名词之前：warm enough，enough money。
mistakes: She is enough old to drive. → old enough — enough 在形容词后。
I'm too much tired. → too tired — 形容词前 too 不带 much。
He is too young for vote. → too young to vote — 结构 too + adj + to-inf。

int_u104.text: Unit 104 – 相当，相当，漂亮，相当
int_u104.note: 相当好/相当困难/相当容易
int_u104.exp:
Unit 104 — 适度程度副词：quite, rather, pretty, fairly。

Quite（相当）— 中性偏正面：quite good, quite nice。与某些形容词 = completely（完全）：quite right, quite wrong, quite sure, quite impossible。
Rather — 比 quite 强，常带负面语气：rather difficult, rather cold。与正面形容词意为「愉快地惊讶」：It was rather good!
Pretty — 口语 = quite：pretty good, pretty difficult。
Fairly — 比 quite 弱，中性：fairly easy, fairly good。
名词前的位置：
  • quite/rather 位于冠词之前：quite a good film, rather a nice day；
  • fairly/pretty — 之后：a fairly good film, a pretty nice day。
强度等级：fairly < quite/pretty < rather < very。

Rather 常暗示与预期的对比。
int_u104.exc: Quite + 某些绝对形容词 = 「完全」：quite right, quite impossible, quite sure。
mistakes: A quite good film → quite a good film — quite 位于冠词前。
Rather a good result 或 a rather good result — 两种都有，但 a rather good result 更佳。
He's quite right? — 此意下 quite = 「完全」不能与「相当」细节搭配。

int_u105.text: 单元 105 - 比较程度（第 1 课）
int_u105.exp:
Unit 105 — 形容词和副词的比较级。

单音节：+er：cheap→cheaper, old→older, fast→faster。
以 -y 结尾双音节：y→ier：happy→happier, easy→easier, busy→busier。
长（2+音节）：more +：more expensive, more difficult, more interesting。
例外（不规则）：
  • good → better；
  • bad → worse；
  • far → farther/further；
  • little → less；
  • much/many → more；
  • old → older / elder（仅亲属）。
辅音重写（一个音节，辅+元+辅）：big→bigger, hot→hotter, thin→thinner。
Older vs. elder：my elder brother（家中），但比较中只用 older：He is older than me.

不能组合：more better → better。
int_u105.exc: Elder/eldest — 仅用于家庭成员（my elder brother）。其他用 older。
mistakes: More big → bigger — 短形容词用 -er。
More better → better — better 已是比较级。
He is elder than me. → older than me — 比较用 older。

int_u106.text: 单元 106 - 比较方法（第 2 课）
int_u106.note: 好多了/暖和了/有什么好转了吗？
int_u106.exp:
Unit 106 — 比较级的修饰。

加强词（显著更/更少）：
  • much, far, a lot, considerably + 比较级：much better, far more expensive, a lot cheaper。
减弱词（稍微）：
  • a bit, a little, slightly：a bit warmer, slightly more difficult。
Any / no 用于疑问和否定：
  • Is it any better? — 稍微好些吗？
  • It's no better. — 一点也不好。
递进：comparative and comparative = 越来越：getting better and better, more and more expensive。
The … the …：The more you practise, the better you get. The sooner, the better.
禁止 very：very 不与比较级搭配：不是 very better → much better。

一次一形：more cheaper → cheaper。
int_u106.exc: 不能 very + 比较级。使用 much, far, a lot, a bit, slightly。
mistakes: Very better → much better / far better — 比较级用 much 加强。
It's a bit more cheaper. → a bit cheaper — 只用一种比较级形式。
Is it more better? → Is it any better? — 疑问用 any。

int_u107.exp:
Unit 107 — 所有比较结构。

相等：as + adj/adv + as：
  • She's as tall as her brother. — 和哥哥一样高。
  • He runs as fast as me.
不等（比比较级更温和）：not as … as / not so … as：
  • It's not as cold as yesterday. — 不像昨天那么冷。
比较级 + than：
  • She runs faster than me/I.
  • This book is more interesting than that one.
than/as 后：口语 — 宾格（than me, as her）；正式 — 主格（than I am, as she does）。
Twice/three times as：
  • It's twice as expensive as before. — 比以前贵两倍。
The same as：My car is the same colour as yours.

不能混用：不是 as tall than，不是 more cheap than。
int_u107.exc: than/as 后正式文体用主格：taller than I (am)。口语用 taller than me。
mistakes: She's taller as me. → taller than me — 比较级后用 than。
He's as tall than her. → as tall as her — 相等用 as…as。
It's more cheap than the other. → cheaper than — 短形容词用 -er。

int_u108.text: 单元 108 – 最高级（最好的）
int_u108.exp:
Unit 108 — 最高级。

形式：
  • 短的：+est：oldest, biggest, happiest；
  • 长的：the most +：the most beautiful；
  • 不规则：best, worst, furthest, least, most, eldest。
始终带 the：the oldest, the most interesting — 定冠词必用。
最高级后的介词：
  • in + 地点/群体：the tallest in the class, the best player in the team；
  • of + 时间/整体：the hottest day of the year, the best of all。
与 Present Perfect — 特色组合：
  • It's the best film I've ever seen. — 我看过最好的电影。
  • She's the nicest person I've ever met.
替代：by far the best, easily the best — 更强。

不能 the most best — 只用 the best。
int_u108.exc: 最高级后 — in + 地点/群体，非 of：the tallest in the class，不是 of the class。
mistakes: She is most beautiful. → the most beautiful — 最高级必须 the。
The best player of the team → the best player in the team — 群体用 in。
It's the most best film. → the best film — 一种最高级形式。

int_u109.text: 单元 109 - 词序：地点和时间（第 1 课）
int_u109.exp:
Unit 109 — 句末状语顺序。

一般规则：动词 → 宾语 → 方式 → 地点 → 时间。
  • She sang the song beautifully at the concert last night.
  • He drove carefully to the airport yesterday.
地点先于时间：
  • 正确：I went to the cinema last night.
  • 错误：I went last night to the cinema.
多个地点状语 — 从小到大：
  • I live at 12 Oxford Street in London in England.
多个时间状语 — 从具体到一般：
  • She called at 5pm yesterday.
  • We'll meet at 7 on Monday next week.
前置强调（文体）：
  • Yesterday, I went to the cinema. — 强调时间。

不能把副词放在动词和宾语之间：She sang beautifully the song。
int_u109.exc: 时间副词可前置强调：Yesterday she went to the cinema。
mistakes: She went last night to the cinema. → She went to the cinema last night. — 地点在时间之前。
I live in London at 12 Oxford Street. → at 12 Oxford Street in London — 从小到大。
He works every day in the office. → He works in the office every day. — 时间在句末。

int_u11.text: 第 11 单元 – 你...多久了？
int_u11.exp:
Unit 11 — 问题 How long…? 询问从过去开始延续至今的情境时长。

结构：
  • How long have you + V3/been V-ing?
  • 回答：for + 时段 或 since + 起点。
例句：
  • How long have you lived in Paris? — For ten years. — 你在巴黎住多久了？—— 十年。
  • How long has she been waiting? — Since 8 o'clock. — 她等多久了？—— 从8点。
  • How long have you known him?（状态 —— 用 Perfect，非进行）。
重要：俄/中文现在时（「你住多久了？」）→ 英语用完成时。How long are you…? —— 错误。状态动词 —— 只用 Perfect，不用 Perfect Continuous。For = 时段，since = 起点。
int_u11.exc: 状态动词用 Present Perfect：How long have you known her?（不是 been knowing）。
mistakes: How long do you live here? → How long have you lived here? —— 过去到现在的情境。
I'm here since Monday. → I've been here since Monday. —— 需要完成时。
How long are you waiting? → How long have you been waiting? —— 到现在的时长。

int_u110.text: 单元 110 - 词序：动词副词（第 2 课）
int_u110.note: 总是，经常，通常 - 放在一个句子中
int_u110.exp:
Unit 110 — 动词旁副词的位置。

频率副词（always, usually, often, sometimes, rarely, never, hardly ever）：
  • 实义动词之前：I always drink coffee in the morning.
  • be 之后：She is always late.
  • 助动词之后：I have never been to Paris. You can always ask me.
确信/评论副词（probably, definitely, certainly, obviously, clearly）— 位置相同：
  • She will probably come.
  • He is definitely right.
方式副词（well, quickly, carefully）— 通常句末：
  • She speaks English well.
强调时放句首：Sometimes I feel sad. Often, she arrives early.
不能置于动词和直接宾语之间：She speaks well English.

信号：与 be — 之后；与其他动词 — 之前；与 Perfect/Modal — 助动词和实义动词之间。
int_u110.exc: 与 be 频率副词在之后：She is always late。与其他动词 — 之前：She always arrives late。
mistakes: I always am happy. → I am always happy. — to be 之后。
She eats always breakfast. → She always eats breakfast. — 实义动词之前。
I have been never there. → I have never been there. — 助动词之后。

int_u111.text: 111单元——仍然，然而，已经；不再/不再
int_u111.note: 我还在等。他不再在这里工作了。
int_u111.exp:
Unit 111 — 持续/完成情境副词。

Still — 情境出乎预期地持续：
  • I'm still waiting for him.
  • She still lives with her parents.
  • 位置：实义动词之前，be 之后：He is still here.
Yet — 「尚未/已经」用于疑问和否定，常在句末：
  • Have you finished yet? — 完成了吗？
  • I haven't seen him yet. — 还没看到。
Already — 「已经」用于肯定，通常位于动词之间或句末：
  • She has already left.
Any more / any longer / no longer — 停止：
  • He doesn't work here any more / any longer.
  • He no longer works here.（更正式，no longer 位于实义动词前）

对比：still — 持续；any more/no longer — 停止；yet — 尚未发生；already — 早于预期。
int_u111.exc: Still — 在肯定句中位于实义动词之前。Yet — 在疑问/否定句末。
mistakes: I don't know still. → I still don't know. — still 位于动词前（即使否定）。
Have you already finished?（中性疑问）→ Have you finished yet? — 疑问常用 yet。
He doesn't work here no longer. → He no longer works here. / doesn't work here any more。

int_u112.note: 连我都知道。她甚至不能走路。
int_u112.exp:
Unit 112 — 聚焦副词 even 强调意外。

基本含义：「甚至」— 强调某事超出预期。
位置：位于要强调的词/短语之前（或实义动词前）：
  • Even a child could solve it. — 连孩子都能解决。
  • She can't even walk. — 她甚至不能走路。
  • He works even on Sundays. — 他甚至周日也工作。
与比较级：even = 更：
  • It's even better than I expected.
  • She's even more talented than her sister.
否定强调：not even：
  • He didn't even say sorry. — 甚至没说对不起。
Even though / even if — 让步连词（引导从句）：
  • Even though it was cold, we went out.
  • I'll come even if it rains.

不能：Even it rained → Even though it rained。
int_u112.exc: Even 不可置于句首而不改变语序。比较级加强：even better, even more。
mistakes: Even it was cold, we went out. → Even though it was cold… — 从句前用 even though。
It's more better. → It's even better. — 比较级加强用 even。
He not even said sorry. → He didn't even say sorry. — even 在助动词之后。

int_u113.text: Unit 113 – 尽管，尽管，尽管，尽管，尽管
int_u113.note: 虽然很累，但她还是工作了。
int_u113.exp:
Unit 113 — 表达让步的方式（对比，「尽管」）。

连词（引导从句）：
  • although, though, even though — 同义；even though 更强；
  • Although it was cold, she went out.
  • Even though he was tired, he kept working.
  • though 常在句末作副词：It was cold. I went out, though.
介词（在名词、代词或 -ing 前）：
  • in spite of, despite — 意思相同；
  • Despite the rain, we continued.
  • In spite of being tired, she worked.
  • Despite the fact that it was cold, we went out.（the fact that 增加从句）
关键禁忌：
  • despite of — 不带 of；
  • although 后只能跟句子，不跟名词。

对比：although + clause，despite/in spite of + noun/-ing。
int_u113.exc: Although/though — 跟从句；despite/in spite of — 跟名词或 -ing。不能 despite of。
mistakes: Despite of the rain → Despite the rain / In spite of the rain — despite 不带 of。
Although the rain, we went out. → Although it was raining… — although 后跟从句。
In spite she was tired, she worked. → In spite of being tired — in spite of + noun/-ing。

int_u114.exp:
Unit 114 — in case（以防）— 提前采取的措施。

含义：in case 意为提前采取行动，为可能情况做准备。
  • Take an umbrella in case it rains. — 带伞（提前），以防下雨时做好准备。
  • I'll write down the address in case I forget. — 记下地址以防忘记。
与 if 的区别：
  • I'll call you if I'm late. — 只有迟到才打电话（条件）。
  • I'll call you in case I'm late. — 提前打电话（以防万一）。
语法：in case 后用 Present Simple/Past Simple，不用 will/would，即使讲将来：
  • Take water in case you get thirsty.
  • I took a map in case we got lost.
Just in case — 口语说法「以防万一」：Take your phone just in case.

In case of + noun（正式、标识）— 「万一某事」：In case of fire, break glass.
int_u114.exc: in case 后用 Present Simple，即使讲将来：I'll take an umbrella in case it rains，不用 will rain。
mistakes: Take money in case you will need it. → in case you need it — in case 后用 Present Simple。
Call me if I'm late vs. Call me in case I'm late：混淆含义 — if = 条件，in case = 提前。
In case of he comes → In case he comes — 从句前不带 of。

int_u115.text: 单元 115 — 除非、只要、提供、提供
int_u115.note: 除非你快点，否则你就会迟到。
int_u115.exp:
Unit 115 — if 的条件连词替代。

Unless = if not：
  • Unless you hurry, you'll be late. = If you don't hurry…
  • I won't go unless you come with me. = if you don't come。
禁止双重否定：不能 unless … not。
As long as / so long as — 「只要/条件是」：
  • You can borrow my car as long as you're careful.
  • I'll help so long as you tell me the truth.
Provided (that) / providing (that) — 更正式，「条件是」：
  • You can stay provided that you pay rent.
  • Providing he apologises, I'll forgive him.
语法：这些连词后用 Present Simple（如 if 从句），不用 will：
  • Unless it rains, we'll go out.
  • As long as you are ready, we can leave.

这些连词都引导条件，但语气不同：unless — 否定条件，provided — 限制性要求。
int_u115.exc: Unless = if not，所以不能 unless … not：Unless you hurry，不是 unless you don't hurry。
mistakes: Unless you don't hurry → Unless you hurry — 避免双重否定。
As long as you will be careful → as long as you are careful — 连词后用 Present Simple。
Provided that he will come → provided that he comes — 现在时。

int_u116.text: 单元 116 - 作为：原因和时间
int_u116.exp:
Unit 116 — 多义连词 as。

含义1：「因为」（= because, since）：
  • As it was late, I went home. — 因为晚了。
  • As she knew French, she translated.
含义2：「当/在…期间」 — 同时进行的持续动作：
  • As I was walking home, I saw an accident.
  • She sang as she cooked.
Just as — 「正当…时」：
  • Just as I was leaving, the phone rang.
as / when / while 的选择：
  • as — 同时进行的持续动作或渐进发展；
  • when — 短暂瞬间事件：When I opened the door, I saw him.；
  • while — 强调持续：While I was cooking, he called.

as 的其他含义：角色（work as a teacher），比较（as tall as）— 在其他单元讨论。
int_u116.exc: As 表「在…期间」用于持续动作（常 Past Continuous）。短暂用 when。
mistakes: As I opened the door, I dropped the keys.（瞬间）→ When I opened the door… — 短暂用 when。
As that it was late, I went home. → As it was late… — 不带 that。
Because as it rained → As it rained / Because it rained — 不重复原因。

int_u117.text: 单元 117 – 喜欢和作为：例如
int_u117.note: 她看起来像她的母亲。她是一名教师。
int_u117.exp:
Unit 117 — like vs. as 在比较和指示角色中。

Like（介词）+ 名词/代词/-ing — 「像、如」：
  • She sings like a bird. — 像鸟一样唱（像）。
  • He's like his father. — 像他父亲。
  • It tastes like chicken.
As（连词）+ 句子 — 「如，正如」：
  • Do as I say, not as I do.
  • She works hard, as her mother did.
As（介词）+ 名词 — 真实角色或功能：
  • She works as a teacher. — 她是老师（她的工作）。
  • He used the book as a pillow. — 把书当枕头。
关键对比：
  • As a teacher, I understand students. — 我是老师。
  • Like a teacher, he spoke clearly. — 像老师一样，但不是老师。

口语：like 有时用作连词（Do like I told you），但非正式；书面语用 as。
int_u117.exc: As 表真实角色：She works as a teacher（她是老师）。Like a teacher = 像老师，但不是。
mistakes: She works like a nurse.（她就是护士）→ works as a nurse — 真实角色用 as。
Do like I say. → Do as I say. — 从句前用 as。
He's like his father's. → He's like his father. — like 后用普通格名词。

int_u118.text: 单元 118 – 就像/好像/好像：好像
int_u118.note: 他看上去就像见了鬼一样。
int_u118.exp:
Unit 118 — 印象和比较：like, as if, as though。

As if / as though — 连词「好像」（意义相同，可互换）：
  • He looks as if he's seen a ghost. — 看起来像见过鬼。
  • She acts as though she owns the place.
Like — 口语版，常见于美式英语：It looks like it's going to rain.。正式语用 as if。
真实情境（可能真实）— 现在时/Perfect：
  • It looks as if it's raining. — 好像在下雨。
  • She looks as if she has seen something scary.
不真实/想象的 — 过去时（如虚拟语气）：
  • He talks as if he knew everything. — 但他不知道。
  • She walks as if she were a queen.（正式 were，口语 was）

区别：as if he is rich（可能真）vs. as if he were rich（不真实，实际不富）。
int_u118.exc: as if/as though 后不真实情境用过去时（如虚拟语气）：He acts as if he knew — 但他不知道。
mistakes: He looks as if he was a ghost.（真实外观）→ as if he has seen a ghost — 真实情境用现在时/Perfect。
She speaks as if she know everything. → as if she knew everything — 不真实用过去时。
Like he's tired（正式）→ as if he's tired — 书面语用 as if/as though。

int_u119.text: 单元 119 - For、期间、同时：期间
int_u119.note: 三天/电影拍摄期间/我在那里的时候
int_u119.exp:
Unit 119 — for, during, while 表时间。

For + 时间段 — 动作持续多久：
  • I waited for two hours. — 等了两小时。
  • She's lived here for five years.
During + 名词 — 动作在哪个时期发生：
  • I fell asleep during the film. — 在电影期间的某时刻睡着。
  • We met during the conference.
While + 句子 — 连词「当…时」：
  • I fell asleep while I was watching the film.
  • She called while I was cooking.
关键区别：
  • for 回答「多久？」：for 2 hours；
  • during 回答「何时？」+ 名词：during lunch；
  • while — 与 during 含义相同，但在从句前。

不能：during three days（持续时间）→ for three days；while the film（名词前）→ during the film。
int_u119.exc: During 表「何时」（在哪个时期），for — 「多久」。容易混淆：for 2 hours vs. during the meeting。
mistakes: I was there during three days. → for three days — 持续时间用 for。
During I was watching TV → While I was watching TV — 从句前用 while。
While the film → during the film — 名词前用 during。

int_u12.text: 单元 12 – 现在完成时与since和for
int_u12.note: 我认识她很多年了。从周一开始。
int_u12.exp:
Unit 12 — 介词 for 与 since 和 Present Perfect / Perfect Continuous 连用。

for + 时段：for two hours, for a week, for ages, for a long time。
since + 起始时刻：since Monday, since 2010, since breakfast, since we met。since 后可接整个从句：since I was a child。

例句：
  • We've been friends for 20 years. — 我们做朋友已20年。
  • I haven't seen her since June. — 从六月起没见过她。
  • He has worked here since he graduated. — 他从毕业起就在这里工作。
ago vs. since：ago —— 只用 Past Simple，从现在往前数：two years ago。不能 since two years —— 应为 for two years。
口语中 for 可省略：I've been here (for) ten years.
int_u12.exc:
Ago 仅用于 Past Simple：two years ago（不是 since two years ago）。
For 可省略：I've lived here (for) ten years.
mistakes: I've known her since 10 years. → I've known her for 10 years. —— 时段用 for。
I've worked here for 2010. → I've worked here since 2010. —— 起点用 since。
She lives here since March. → She has lived here since March. —— 与 since 连用 —— 完成时。

int_u120.note: 我将在周五之前完成。当她到达时……
int_u120.exp:
Unit 120 — by, until, by the time 表终点。

Until (till) — 连续动作直到某刻：
  • I waited until 9 pm. — 连续等到9点。
  • She slept until noon.
  • We can't leave until he arrives.
By — 「不迟于」；动作在此期限前完成（不一定持续）：
  • I'll finish the report by Friday. — 不迟于周五。
  • Be home by 10. — 10点前到家。
By the time + 句子 — 「到那时」：
  • By the time she arrived, I had already left. — 主句用 Past Perfect。
  • By the time you read this, I'll be in Paris. — 主句用 Future。
until vs. by 对比：
  • I'll wait until 9.（连续等待）
  • I'll call you by 9.（此时前打电话）

不能：I'll finish until Friday → by Friday；I'll wait by 9 → until 9。
int_u120.exc: By the time + 从句 Past Simple，主句 Past Perfect：By the time she arrived, I had left。
mistakes: I'll wait by 9pm.（连续等待）→ I'll wait until 9pm. — 连续用 until。
I'll finish until Friday. → I'll finish by Friday. — 期限用 by。
By the time she arrived, I left. → I had already left — 需要 Past Perfect。

int_u121.exp:
Unit 121 — 时间介词 at / on / in 的系统概览。

At — 精确时间点：小时(at 7.30)、用餐时间(at lunch)、节日期间(at Easter)、at night, at the weekend(英式)。On — 日期与星期：on Friday, on 3 March, on Christmas Day, on my birthday。On Friday morning — 带有日期限定时用 on。In — 较长的时间段：月份、年份、世纪、季节(in May, in 1999, in summer, in the 20th century)，以及一天中的时段：in the morning/afternoon/evening。

例：I'll see you on Monday evening — 星期一晚上见。She was born in 1990 — 她生于 1990 年。The lesson starts at 9 — 课程 9 点开始。

不用介词 在 this, last, next, every, tomorrow, yesterday 之前：See you next week。对比：in time(提前)↔ on time(准时)— 见 Unit 122。
int_u121.exc:
不用介词：this/last/next/every — see you next Monday，I saw her last week。

at night，但 in the night(指某一具体夜晚)。

at Christmas(期间)，on Christmas Day(当天)。
mistakes: in Monday → on Monday — 星期几用 on。
on the morning → in the morning — 一天中的时段用 in。
at next week → next week — next/last/this 不加介词。
in 9 o'clock → at 9 o'clock — 精确时间用 at。

int_u122.text: 单元122——准时、准时；在最后和最后
int_u122.exp:
Unit 122 — 两组常被混淆的固定搭配。

On time — 准时、按时刻表：The train left on time — 火车准时发车。In time (for sth / to do sth) — 及时(赶得上)：We got to the airport just in time to catch our flight — 我们刚好及时赶到机场搭机。

At the end (of sth) — 某事或某段时间的末尾：at the end of the film, at the end of May。In the end = finally, eventually — 最终：We couldn't decide where to go. In the end we stayed home — 我们无法决定去哪，最后留在了家里。

记忆法：on time = 精准(时刻表上的点)；in time = 留有余量(在时间窗口之内)。At the end = 时间轴上的一个点；in the end = 结局、最终结果。
int_u122.exc:
in the end = finally(最终)；at the end = 某事的末尾。

on time 没有比较级 — 不能说 more on time。
mistakes: in time for the meeting (= punctual) → on time — 「准时」= on time。
at the end, I agreed → in the end, I agreed — 「最终」= in the end。
in the end of the film → at the end of the film — 具体事物的末尾用 at。

int_u123.text: Unit 123 – Prepositions of place: in, at, on (lesson 1)
int_u123.note: 在房间里/在公交车站/在桌子上
int_u123.exp:
Unit 123 — in / at / on 作地点介词的基本含义。

In — 三维/封闭空间内部：in the kitchen, in a car, in Paris, in the water, in the sky。At — 某一点、作为节点的地方：at the door, at the bus stop, at the traffic lights, at the top/bottom。On — 表面或线上：on the wall, on the floor, on the ceiling, on the coast, on page 10。

例：There's somebody at the door — 门口有人。The picture is on the wall — 画挂在墙上。She lives in a small village — 她住在一个小村庄。

微妙区别： in the corner of the room(房间内的内角)↔ at the corner of the street(街道外的路角)。in the front row(前排内)↔ at the front of the building(大楼正面)。介词的选择决定对空间的感知方式 — 体积、点位或平面。
int_u123.exc:
in bed / in hospital / in prison — 强调功能时不加冠词。

at the door(在门边)，但 on the door(门上的标牌)。
mistakes: on the car → in the car — 小汽车内部 — in。
in the bus stop → at the bus stop — 会合点 — at。
at the picture → in the picture — 图片内 — in。

int_u124.text: 单元 124 – 地点介词：in、at、on（第 2 课）
int_u124.note: 在医院/在家/在电视上/在公共汽车上
int_u124.exp:
Unit 124 — 带地点介词的固定表达，第二轮。

In + 较大范围及「无冠词机构」：in bed, in hospital (英式), in prison, in the country, in the mountains, in the world, in the middle。At + 典型地点/活动：at home, at work, at school, at university, at the station/airport, at a concert, at a party, at Sue's (house)。On：on the ground floor, on the second floor, on the way, on a farm, on an island, on the left/right, on the coast, on the river。

例：He's been in hospital since Monday — 他从周一开始住院。I met him at a party — 我在派对上遇到他。We live on the fifth floor — 我们住在五楼。

英式 vs 美式： in hospital(英式，指病人)↔ in the hospital(美式)。媒体：on TV, on the radio，但 in the newspaper。这些搭配最好整块记忆。
int_u124.exc:
英式：in hospital；美式：in the hospital。

on TV / on the radio，但 in the newspaper。

at home / at work / at school 不加冠词。
mistakes: in home → at home — 固定表达。
in TV → on TV — 节目在 on TV 上播。
on the bus as passenger 可以，但 in the bus 较少 — 公交工具用 on the bus。

int_u125.text: 单元 125 – 地点介词：in、at、on（第 3 课）
int_u125.exp:
Unit 125 — 第三轮：in / at / on 与 front, back, top, bottom, side, end, middle, beginning 搭配。

含义由「地点 + 介词」组合决定：in the front/back — 物体内部(in the front of the car — 车厢前部)；at the front/back — 外部正面/背面(at the back of the queue — 排在队尾)；on the front/back — 表面上(on the back of the envelope — 信封背面)。

例：I sat at the back of the class — 我坐在教室后排。Write your name on the back of the photo — 请把名字写在照片背面。The driver sits in the front — 司机坐在前面(车内)。

At the top/bottom of — 顶部/底部(at the top of the hill)。On top of — 在…之上(on top of the box)。At the end of — 在…末端(空间/时间)。这些搭配需要理解物理几何。
int_u125.exc: in the front/back(of a car/room)— 内部；at the front/back(of a building/queue)— 正面/背面；on the front/back(of a page)— 表面。
mistakes: on the front of the class → at the front of the class — 前部 — at。
at the front of the envelope → on the front of the envelope — 表面 — on。
at the back of the car → in the back of the car — 车内 — in。

int_u126.text: 单元 126 – 方向介词：to, at, in, into
int_u126.exp:
Unit 126 — 方向和移动介词：to, into, at, in, on, onto。

To 表示移动目标：go to work, drive to London, return to the office。Into — 向封闭空间内部移动：walk into the room, get into the car, pour milk into the cup。At 用于带方向动作：look at, point at, throw at, shout at, laugh at, aim at — 目标如靶子。

in vs into 的差别：She was swimming in the lake(在哪里，过程)↔ She jumped into the lake(去哪里，进入动作)。

例：I'm going to bed — 我去睡觉。He ran into the house — 他跑进屋子。Don't throw stones at the dog — 别向狗扔石头。

不用介词：go/come/get home。Arrive：arrive at + 建筑/事件，arrive in + 城市/国家。Enter — 及物，无 into：enter the building。
int_u126.exc:
不用介词：go home，get home。

arrive at(地点)，但 arrive in(城市/国家)。
mistakes: go to home → go home — home 不加 to。
arrive to London → arrive in London — arrive in + 城市。
enter into the room → enter the room — enter 不加介词。

int_u127.text: 单元 127 – 不同情况下的 On、in、at
int_u127.note: 在公共汽车上/在出租车上/在聚会上
int_u127.exp:
Unit 127 — 规则交叉时 in, on, at 的选用。

交通工具： on a bus/train/plane/ship/bike(大型、可「上」的工具)，但 in a car/taxi(坐在车厢内)。活动： at a party/meeting/concert/wedding/match/conference — 人群聚集点。岛屿/海岸： in Sicily(大岛、领地)，on a small island(地图上一点)。地址： at 25 Baker Street(门牌)，on/in Baker Street(街)，in London(城市)。

例：I saw her on the train — 我在火车上见到她。There were 200 people at the concert — 演唱会有 200 人。He grew up on a farm — 他在农场长大。

规则：中型/公共交通 ⇒ on；私密封闭 ⇒ in。活动 ⇒ at。这些选择依类比习得，而非靠逻辑推导。
int_u127.exc:
on a bus/train/plane/ship/bike，但 in a car/taxi。

at a party / at a concert / at a meeting。
mistakes: in the bus → on the bus — 公共交通 — on。
on a taxi → in a taxi — 出租车 — in。
in the party → at the party — 活动 — at。

int_u128.exp:
Unit 128 — 多功能介词 by。

用法：(1)交通方式，不带冠词：by car, by bus, by train, by plane, by sea, by air；但 on foot。(2)被动句的施事者：Hamlet was written by Shakespeare — 莎士比亚所写。(3)方法/工具：pay by credit card, contact me by email, by mistake, by chance, by accident。(4)位置紧邻：Sit by me — 坐我旁边。(5)最后期限：I'll be back by 10 — 我 10 点前回来。(6)独自：by myself/yourself = alone。

例：I'll send the file by email — 我会用邮件发文件。She lives by the sea — 她住在海边。Finish the project by Friday — 周五前完成项目。

By vs until：by = 到某时完成(一次性)，until = 直到(持续)：Wait until 6 ↔ Finish by 6。With vs by：工具(cut with a knife)vs 方式/施事(killed by a soldier)。
int_u128.exc:
不加冠词：by car/bus/train/plane，但 on foot。

by myself = alone；by himself(与 by 搭配的反身)。
mistakes: by a car → by car — 交通方式不加冠词。
by foot → on foot — 步行 = on foot。
until the time → by the time — 「到…时」= by the time。

int_u129.exp:
Unit 129 — 名词 + 介词：固定搭配。

For：a reason for, a need for, a demand for, a request for。Of：a cause of, a photo of, an example of, an advantage of, a lack of。In：an increase/rise/fall/drop/decrease in, a change in, difficulty in, interest in, belief in。To：damage to, an answer to, a solution to, a reply to, an attitude to/towards, invitation to。With：a relationship/connection with, contact with。Between：difference between。

例：There's no reason for worry — 没有担心的理由。The cause of the fire is unknown — 火灾原因未知。There's been a rise in prices — 物价上涨。What's the solution to this problem? — 这个问题的解决方案是什么？

易混对： cause of ↔ reason for(都表示原因)。increase in(非 of)用于描述上升的参数。介词后接动名词：a reason for leaving early。
int_u129.exc:
cause of，但 reason for。

an increase/rise/fall in(非 of)。

damage to，solution to。
mistakes: reason of → reason for — 某事的原因 — for。
cause for → cause of — 原因 — of。
solution of → solution to — 解决方案 — to。
increase of prices → increase in prices — 上涨 — in。

int_u13.text: 第 13 单元 – 现在完成时与一般过去时（第 1 部分）
int_u13.note: 你见过吗？ / 你昨天看到了吗？
int_u13.exp:
Unit 13 — Present Perfect vs. Past Simple 的核心对比（第1部分）。

Present Perfect —— 与现在有联系，无具体时间：I've finished the report（它现在已完成）。
Past Simple —— 过去特定时刻完成的动作：I finished it at 3 p.m.

决定性标志：
  • Perfect：ever, never, just, already, yet, so far, recently, this week（当前时段）。
  • Past Simple：yesterday, ago, last week/month, in 2010, when。
例句：
  • Have you seen the news? — 你看新闻了吗？vs. Did you see the news this morning? —— 时段结束。
  • I've been to Rome（经历）vs. I went to Rome in May（事实）。
When-疑问句 —— 永远用 Past Simple：When did you arrive?
int_u13.exc:
美式英语中 Past Simple 常替代 Present Perfect：Did you eat? = Have you eaten?
When-疑问句 —— 只用 Past Simple：When did you arrive?
mistakes: When have you arrived? → When did you arrive? —— 过去的 when —— Past Simple。
I have lost my keys yesterday. → I lost my keys yesterday. —— yesterday → Past Simple。
Did you ever visit Japan? → Have you ever visited Japan? —— 人生经历 —— Perfect。

int_u130.text: 单元 130 – 形容词 + 介词（第 1 课）
int_u130.exp:
Unit 130 — 形容词 + 介词，第一部分。

Of(情感与特征)：afraid of, frightened of, scared of, terrified of; ashamed of, aware of, conscious of; capable of, fond of, full of, jealous of, proud of, short of, tired of, typical of。At(能力/反应)：good/bad/brilliant/hopeless/clever/terrible at；surprised/amazed/shocked at/by。From：different from, absent from, safe from。For：famous for, responsible for, ready for, suitable for, sorry for。

例：She's afraid of spiders — 她怕蜘蛛。He's very good at chess — 他国际象棋很强。This region is famous for its wines — 该地区以葡萄酒闻名。

微妙点： sorry for sb(同情)↔ sorry about sth(为某事道歉)。good at + 名词/-ing，但 good to sb = 对某人友善，good for sb/sth = 有益的。different from — 中性；to/than — 口语化。
int_u130.exc:
different from(英式偏好)，different to/than 也可见。

good at + 名词/-ing，但 good to sb(对…友善)。
mistakes: afraid from → afraid of — of。
good in math → good at math — at。
different of → different from — from。
famous of → famous for — for。

int_u131.text: 单元 131 – 形容词 + 介词（第 2 课）
int_u131.exp:
Unit 131 — 形容词 + 介词，第二部分。

With：angry/annoyed/furious/pleased/happy/satisfied/bored/delighted/disappointed with。About：angry/annoyed/worried/upset/excited/happy/nervous about。In：interested in, involved in, successful in, experienced in, rich in, weak in。On：keen on, dependent on, based on。To：married to, engaged to, similar to, close to, related to, kind/nice/rude/polite to, opposed to, addicted to。By：surprised/shocked/impressed/amazed/astonished by/at。

例：I'm not interested in politics — 我对政治不感兴趣。She's married to a doctor — 她嫁给了一位医生。He was very kind to me — 他对我非常友好。

对比： angry with sb(对人)↔ angry about sth(对事)。tired of = 厌倦 ↔ tired from = 因…而累。interested in(持久兴趣)↔ interesting to sb(对某人有趣)。
int_u131.exc:
interested in(持续兴趣)，但 interesting to sb。

tired of(厌倦)vs. tired from(累于)。
mistakes: interested on music → interested in music — in。
satisfied of → satisfied with — with。
keen in → keen on — on。
proud for → proud of — of。

int_u132.text: 单元 132 – 动词后面和后面的介词
int_u132.exp:
Unit 132 — 动词 + at vs + to：定向动作 vs 交流。

At — 视线/攻击/目标的方向：look at, stare at, glance at, glare at, point at, laugh at, smile at, wave at, shout at, throw at, aim at, shoot at, fire at。To — 信息传递/称呼：speak to, talk to, listen to, say sth to, explain to, describe to, mention to, suggest to, apologise to, write to, reply to。

例：Don't shout at me! — 别冲我吼(攻击性)。She shouted to me across the street — 她从街对面喊我(让我听到)。He threw the ball to me(传给我接)↔ threw a stone at the window(扔向窗户，想打中)。

意义转换的对比： shout at = 斥责，shout to = 喊叫。throw at(想击中)vs throw to(传递)。laugh at(嘲笑)— 始终用 at。smile at — 微笑的对象。listen 后必须用 to：listen to music。
int_u132.exc:
shout at(攻击)vs. shout to(让对方听到)。

throw at(想击中)vs. throw to(传递)。
mistakes: look to the picture → look at the picture — at。
point to me angrily → point at me — 指向某人 — at。
laugh of him → laugh at him — at。

int_u133.exp:
Unit 133 — 带 about, for, of, after 的动词。

About(想/谈话题)：talk/speak/read/write/hear/know/think/dream/worry/complain/argue about。For(目的/期待)：ask for, apply for, wait for, search for, look for, care for, pay for, hope for, long for, vote for。Of：consist of, smell of, taste of, think of, hear of, dream of, die of。After：look after(照顾)、take after(像亲属)、ask after(问候健康)、run after(追赶)。

例：I'm looking for my keys — 我在找钥匙。Who looks after the children? — 谁照顾孩子？She takes after her mother — 她像母亲。The soup smells of garlic — 汤有蒜味。

三个 look： look at(看)、look for(找)、look after(照顾)。这三个是常见错误点。wait for — 介词必备，即使加代词：wait for me。
int_u133.exc:
wait for sb，但 wait on = 服侍。

look after(照顾)≠ look for(找)≠ look at(看)。
mistakes: worry for → worry about — about。
wait sb → wait for sb — for 必备。
dream for → dream of/about — of/about。
look for the kids (= take care) → look after the kids — 照顾 — after。

int_u134.text: 单元 134 – 介词 about 和 of：hear、think、dream
int_u134.exp:
Unit 134 — 随 about vs of 改变意义的动词。

Think about(思考、集中思考)↔ think of(看法；想起；打算)：I'm thinking about what to do — 我在考虑怎么做；What do you think of my idea? — 你觉得我的主意如何？I can't think of the name — 想不起名字。Hear about(听说消息)↔ hear of(听过…存在)：Have you heard of Murphy's book? — 听说过 Murphy 的书吗？Dream about(做梦梦见)↔ dream of(梦想)。

Remind：两种结构。Remind sb of sb/sth = 让想起(相似)：She reminds me of my sister. Remind sb about/to do sth = 提醒(去做)：Remind me to call him.

Complain：complain to sb about sth — 向谁抱怨什么。Warn：warn sb about/of sth — 两种都可用。介词选择常区分「话题」(about)和「内容/本质」(of)。
int_u134.exc:
think of(意见/想起)vs. think about(思考)。

remind of(让想起某人)vs. remind about(提醒某事)。
mistakes: hear from news → hear about the news — about。
dream for you → dream of/about you — of/about。
remind me to her → remind me of her — 相似 — of。

int_u135.text: 单元 135 – 介词 of, for, from, on
int_u135.exp:
Unit 135 — 动词 + of, for, from, on。

Of：accuse sb of, approve of, consist of, dispose of, remind sb of, suspect sb of。For：apologise for, blame sb for, forgive sb for, pay for, punish sb for, thank sb for, charge sb for, pray for, long for。From：borrow from, suffer from, recover from, protect/save/prevent/stop sb from, differ from, escape from, translate from。On：depend on, rely on, count on, concentrate on, insist on, spend (money) on, live on, congratulate sb on。

例：I depend on you — 我依靠你。She apologised for being late — 她为迟到道歉。He suffers from asthma — 他患哮喘。They accused him of stealing — 他们指控他偷窃。

「阻止」结构： prevent/stop/protect/save sb from doing sth。Spend on + 物/事，spend (time) -ing 无介词。It depends 单独作答时无 on，但 it depends on you 带介词。
int_u135.exc:
depend on，但简短回答 it depends(无 on)。

suffer from(疾病)vs. suffer (because) of(原因)。
mistakes: approve for → approve of — of。
suffer of flu → suffer from flu — from。
depend of → depend on — on。
consist from → consist of — of。

int_u136.text: 单元 136 – 介词 in, into, with, to, on
int_u136.exp:
Unit 136 — 本系列收官：动词 + in, into, with, to。

In：believe in, specialise in, succeed in, result in, invest in。Into(移动/转化)：crash/bump/run into, divide into, translate into, break into (a house)。With：agree/disagree with, argue with, discuss sth with sb, collide with, deal with, provide sb with, share sth with sb, compare X with Y。To：belong to, happen to, listen to, refer to, react to, object to, prefer X to Y。

例：Do you believe in ghosts? — 你相信有鬼吗？The car crashed into a tree — 车撞上了树。I prefer tea to coffee — 相比咖啡我更爱茶。This book belongs to Tom — 这本书是汤姆的。

勿混淆： believe sb(相信某人所说)≠ believe in sb/sth(相信存在/价值)。happen to sb — 发生在某人身上，不用 with。discuss 后不加 about：discuss the plan，但 talk about the plan。
int_u136.exc:
succeed in + -ing，但 success in(名词)也用 in。

happen to sb(发生在某人身上)，happen with 较少见。
mistakes: believe on God → believe in God — in。
crash in the tree → crash into the tree — into。
succeed on → succeed in — in。

int_u137.text: 单元 137 - 短语动词：一般概念
int_u137.note: 起床/打开/照顾 - 原则
int_u137.exp:
Unit 137 — 短语动词(phrasal verbs)：总体结构。

形式：动词 + 小品词(up, down, in, out, on, off, away, back)。三类：
  • 不及物 — 无宾语：My car broke down. She grew up in Leeds.
  • 及物可分 — 名词宾语位于小品词前后均可，代词宾语只能在中间：Turn on the radio = Turn the radio on; Turn it on(不能 turn on it)。
  • 及物不可分(介词动词) — 宾语总在小品词后：look after the children = look after them(不能 look them after)。
意义往往具有习语性：give up = 放弃，而非「给向上」。

例：Please put your phone away — 把手机收起来。I ran into an old friend — 我偶遇老朋友。

指南：带方向小品词的多可分(put on, take off)，带「完整介词」的多不可分(look after, get over, run into)。字典标注(sep/insep)可靠。
int_u137.exc:
部分短语动词可分：turn it on(代词时不能 turn on it)。

不可分：look after him(不能 look him after)。
mistakes: turn on it → turn it on — 代词在动词和小品词之间。
look him after → look after him — look after 不可分。
get on it (the bus) → get on it 可以，但 get it on 意思不同。

int_u138.text: 单元 138 – 带有 in 和 out 的动词短语
int_u138.exp:
Unit 138 — 带 in 和 out 的短语动词。

In：get in(进入/到达)、come in(进来)、drop in(顺便造访)、join in(加入活动)、fill in(填表)、break in(闯入)、check in(办理入住/登机)。Out：find out(发现)、work out(解决/奏效/锻炼)、figure out(弄清)、sort out(整理)、point out(指出)、try out(试用)、run out (of)(用完)、eat out(外出就餐)、go out(出去/熄灭)。

例：I've just found out she's moving — 我刚得知她要搬家。Please fill in this form = fill this form in = fill it in — 请填这张表。We've run out of milk — 我们的牛奶用完了。

语义逻辑： out 常表示向外 + 完成/耗尽(sell out, tire out, burn out)。In = 向内/填充(fill in, hand in)。break in(闯入)≠ break into + 宾语(闯入建筑)。
int_u138.exc:
find out(发现)代词宾语时 find it out 较少 — 通常 find out the truth。

break in = 闯入；break into + 宾语。
mistakes: fill the form in (OK) / fill in it → fill it in — 代词在中间。
break in the house → break into the house — 带宾语用 into。
find out it → find it out。

int_u139.text: 单元 139 – 不带 out 的动词短语（续）
int_u139.exp:
Unit 139 — 续：带 out 的短语动词。

「彻底/完成」义： carry out(执行)、sort out(解决)、work out(计算/成功)、cross out(划掉)、leave out(略去)、cut out(剪掉/停止)、check out(退房)。「显现」义： turn out(原来是)、come out(书/太阳出来)、break out(战争/火灾爆发)、stand out(突出)、make out(辨认微弱信号)。

例：The plan didn't work out — 计划没成。It turned out (that) he was right — 结果他是对的。His new novel came out last week — 他的新小说上周出版。We must carry out the order — 我们必须执行命令。

结构： turn out to be + adj/noun 或 turn out that + clause。run out 不及物(time ran out)，带宾语需用介词：run out of time。check out(退房)≠ check in(入住)。
int_u139.exc:
turn out(原来)后接 to be / that：It turned out to be wrong。

run out of(不是单 run out，若带宾语)：We ran out of milk。
mistakes: run out milk → run out of milk — of。
carry on the plan out → carry out the plan — 分离错误。
turn out that to be → turn out to be。

int_u14.text: 第 14 单元 – 现在完成时与一般过去时（第 2 部分）
int_u14.exp:
Unit 14 — Present Perfect vs. Past Simple 的细微情形（第2部分）。

必用 Present Perfect：
  • 在 It's the first/second/best/worst time… 之后：It's the first time I've eaten sushi. — 我第一次吃寿司。
  • 在 This is the best… I've ever… 之后：This is the best book I've ever read.
  • 时段未结束时：I've drunk three coffees today（一天未结束）。
时段开放 vs. 封闭：
  • I haven't seen her this morning（上午仍在）vs. I didn't see her this morning（上午已过）。
新闻 vs. 细节：标题 —— Perfect，细节 —— Past Simple：A plane has crashed. It took off at 6 a.m. and…
关键：情境当下是否仍有意义，还是过去的封闭事实？
int_u14.exc: 在 It's the first/second/best time… 之后用 Present Perfect：It's the best book I've ever read.
mistakes: It's the first time I see this. → It's the first time I've seen this. —— 在 It's the first time 后用 Perfect。
This is the best coffee I ever drank. → This is the best coffee I've ever drunk. —— 需要 Perfect。
Have you eaten? — Yes, I have eaten an hour ago. → Yes, I ate an hour ago. —— ago 用 Past Simple。

int_u140.text: 单元 140 – 带有 on 和 off 的动词短语（第 1 课）
int_u140.exp:
Unit 140 — 带 on 和 off 的短语动词，第一轮。

On(开启/继续/穿上)：turn/switch on(打开)、put on(穿上/增重)、go on(继续/发生)、carry on(继续)、hold on(等一下)、get on (with)(继续/相处)。Off(关闭/脱离/出发)：turn/switch off、take off(脱下；起飞)、set off(出发)、put off(推迟；让人反感)、call off(取消)、see sb off(送行)。

例：The meeting has been called off — 会议被取消了。What's going on here? — 这里怎么回事？We set off at dawn — 我们黎明出发。Put off the decision until Monday — 把决定推到周一。

小品词语义： on = 接触/持续，off = 断开/移走。put off 有两义 — 推迟和使反感。take off：脱衣服和飞机起飞 — 同一「分离」隐喻。
int_u140.exc:
put on clothes = 穿；take off clothes = 脱。

call off = 取消(= cancel)；set off = 出发。
mistakes: put on it → put it on — 代词在中间。
cancel off → call off / cancel — 不搭配。
set off to the trip → set off on the trip — on。

int_u141.text: 单元 141 – 带有 on 和 off 的动词短语（第 2 课）
int_u141.exp:
Unit 141 — on/off，第二轮。

Get on — 上公交/火车/飞机；get off — 下车。汽车/出租车用：get in/into, get out of。Get on with sb — 相处；get on with sth — 继续做某事。Fall off — 从…掉下；销售下滑：Sales have fallen off。Go off 有多义：响起(The alarm went off)、变质(The milk has gone off)、离开(She went off to Paris)、失去兴趣(I've gone off coffee)。Show off — 炫耀。Pay off — 有回报/还清。Doze off, nod off — 打盹。

例：My hard work paid off — 我的努力有了回报。He's always showing off his new car — 他老是炫耀新车。I get on well with my neighbours — 我和邻居相处融洽。

要点： switch off ≈ turn off 对电器可互换。get on 与 get off 与公共交通搭配 — 好记忆配对。
int_u141.exc:
get on(公交/火车)，但 get in(汽车/出租车)。

switch off / turn off — 对电器同义。
mistakes: get in the bus → get on the bus — on。
get on a taxi → get in a taxi — in。
switch off it → switch it off。

int_u142.text: 单元 142 – 带有 up 和 down 的动词短语
int_u142.exp:
Unit 142 — 带 up 和 down 的短语动词：高度隐喻。

Up 通常 = 增长/加强/完成：grow up(人长大)、speed up(加速)、save up(存钱)、clean up(彻底打扫)、wash up(洗碗，英式)、set up(建立/搭建)、cheer up(振作)、eat up(吃光)、use up(用尽)。Down = 降低/停止：break down(故障/情绪崩溃)、slow down(减速)、calm down(冷静)、turn down(调小/拒绝)、cut down (on)(减少摄入)、let sb down(让人失望)、write down(记下)。

例：He's trying to cut down on sugar — 他在减少糖分。Don't let me down — 别让我失望。Our car broke down on the motorway — 我们的车在高速上抛锚。
