对比： break down(机器/人崩溃)≠ break up(关系破裂；学校放假)。turn up(调大/出现)↔ turn down(调小/拒绝)。
int_u142.exc:
break down(车坏)≠ break up(分手)。

grow up(人长大)≠ grow(植物生长)。
mistakes: I grow up in Moscow (now) → I grew up in Moscow — 通常一般过去时。
slow → slow down — 劝减速 — down。
set up on business → set up a business — 无 on。

int_u143.text: 单元 143 – 带有 up 的动词短语（第 1 课）
int_u143.exp:
Unit 143 — 带 up 的短语动词，续：核心层。

Pick up — 拾起/去接人/学会/染上(病/语言)/(风)变强。Give up — 戒除(+ -ing/名词)：give up smoking。Make up — 编造；和好(make (it) up with sb)；组成；化妆。Keep up (with) — 跟上。End up — 最终处于(+ -ing/形容词/地点)：We ended up staying home. Put up (with) — 忍受。Take up — 开始爱好；占空间/时间。Use up — 用光。

例：I can't put up with this noise — 我受不了这噪音。She took up painting last year — 她去年开始学画。Can you pick me up at the station? — 能来车站接我吗？

搭配： give up 要求动名词，非不定式。put up with — 三词组(不可分)：I can't put up with it，不能 put up it with。make up 多义 — 依语境。
int_u143.exc:
make up 多义：编造(a story)、和好(with sb)、组成。

give up + -ing/名词，不用 to-不定式。
mistakes: give up to smoke → give up smoking — -ing 形式。
pick up it → pick it up。
keep up from me → keep up with me — with。

int_u144.text: 单元 144 – 带 up 的动词短语（第 2 课）
int_u144.exp:
Unit 144 — up 更多用法：出现、揭示、创建。

Turn up / show up — 露面(常意外或迟到)。Come up — 出现(话题、问题)；走近。Come up with — 想出(主意、方案)。Look up — 查字典/参考资料(可分：look it up)。Set up — 建立、组织。Bring up — 抚养；提出话题。Split up — 分手。Wake up — 醒来/叫醒。Blow up — 爆炸；充气。Do up — 系扣/整修。

例：She didn't turn up to the party — 她没来派对。I came up with a new idea — 我想出一个新主意。Can you look up the word in the dictionary? — 能在词典里查这个词吗？

区分： come up(自行出现)≠ come up with(主动创造)。bring up vs raise 作「抚养」近义；bring up 也指「提出话题」。set up a business = start a business。
int_u144.exc:
come up with = 想出；come up(无 with)= 出现。

look up(查词)可分：look it up。
mistakes: come up an idea → come up with an idea — with。
look up it → look it up。
show on / turn on (arrive) → show up / turn up — 「出现」= show/turn up。

int_u145.text: 单元 145 – 短语动词与away和back
int_u145.exp:
Unit 145 — 收官：away 和 back。

Away(远离、处理、持续)：go away(走开)、run away(逃跑)、get away(逃脱；去度假)、throw away(扔掉；错失)、give away(免费赠送；泄密)、put away(收起)、keep away (from)(远离)、pass away(婉：去世)。Back(返回、回应)：come back, go back, get back(返回)；give back, pay back, send back(归还)；call/ring/phone back(回电)；look back(回顾)。

例：Don't throw those papers away — 别扔那些纸。He gave away all his money to charity — 他把钱都捐给慈善。I'll call you back later — 我稍后回电。

近义/细节： throw away ≈ throw out(扔掉)。get back(回来)≠ get sth back(要回)。come back home — 无 to。put away(收到架上)≠ put off(时间推迟)。
int_u145.exc:
get back = 返回；get sth back = 要回。

throw away / throw out — 同义(扔掉)。
mistakes: come back to home → come back home — home 无 to。
go away from here (imperative tone) 可以，但 go out away — go away 无多余小品词。
throw away it → throw it away。

int_u15.text: 第 15 单元 – 过去完成时（我已经完成了）
int_u15.note: 我到达时她已经离开了。
int_u15.exp:
Unit 15 — Past Perfect（「过去的过去」）表示某动作发生在过去某动作或时刻之前。

公式：had + V3（所有人称相同）。缩写：'d。

用法：
  • 顺序：When I arrived, the film had already started. — 我到时电影已开始。
  • 原因/状态：She was tired because she hadn't slept. — 她累因为没睡。
  • 间接引语：He said he had seen it.
信号词：already, just, never, by the time, before, after, when。
与 Past Simple 对比：若顺序已明（I woke up and had breakfast），无需过去完成时。当事件顺序重要且不明显时才需 Past Perfect。
int_u15.exc:
若顺序已明（尤其与 before, after 连用），无需 Past Perfect：After I ate, I left.
状态动词不用 Perfect Continuous —— 只用 Past Perfect：I had known.
mistakes: When I came, she already left. → When I came, she had already left. —— 更早的过去。
He said he saw the film before. → He said he had seen the film before. —— 间接引语。
I had ate before he arrived. → I had eaten before he arrived. —— 需要 V3（eaten）。

int_u16.text: 第 16 单元 - 过去完成进行时
int_u16.note: 她来之前我已经等了一个小时了。
int_u16.exp:
Unit 16 — Past Perfect Continuous 表示过去某时刻之前持续进行的过程（有时带当时的结果）。

公式：had been + V-ing。

含义：
  • 过去时刻之前的时长：By 2020, she had been teaching for 15 years. — 到2020年她已教书15年。
  • 过去状态的原因：His eyes were red because he had been crying. — 他眼睛红因为哭过。
  • 被打断的过程：I had been waiting for an hour when the bus finally came.
信号词：for, since, how long, all day, before。
状态动词不用进行时 —— 用 Past Perfect：I had known him for years before he told me.
对比：Past Perfect —— 事实/结果；Past Perfect Continuous —— 过程/时长。
int_u16.exc: 状态动词不用 —— 改用 Past Perfect：I had known him for years.
mistakes: She was tired because she worked all day. → She was tired because she had been working all day. —— 持续到过去某点的动作。
He had been knowing her for years. → He had known her for years. —— know 是状态。
I had waiting for an hour. → I had been waiting for an hour. —— 需要 had been + -ing。

int_u17.text: 第 17 单元 - 拥有与拥有
int_u17.note: 你有车吗？ / 你有车吗？
int_u17.exp:
Unit 17 — have got（英式）与 have（美式/通用）表示拥有。

含义相同：I've got a brother = I have a brother.

形式：
  • have got：(+) I've got / She's got；(−) I haven't got / She hasn't got；(?) Have you got…? Has she got…?
  • have：(+) I have / She has；(−) I don't have / She doesn't have；(?) Do you have…? Does she have…?
重要限制：have got —— 仅用于现在时。过去时 —— 只用 had（无 got）：I had a bike when I was 10. 疑问：Did you have…?

动作（非拥有）—— 只用 have（无 got）：have breakfast/a shower/a party/a good time。此处 have = 「进行/度过」。
int_u17.exc:
Have got 仅用于现在时。过去用 had（无 got）：I had a car then.
动作（非拥有）用 have：have lunch, have a shower（不是 have got）。
mistakes: I've got a shower every morning. → I have a shower every morning. —— 动作，非拥有。
She had got a car last year. → She had a car last year. —— have got 仅用于现在时。
Have you a pen? → Have you got a pen? / Do you have a pen? —— 需正确形式。

int_u18.note: 我以前也抽烟，但后来戒了。
int_u18.exp:
Unit 18 — used to + 不定式 描述过去常有而现已不存在的习惯或状态。

公式：(+) used to + V1；(−) didn't use to + V1；(?) Did + S + use to + V1?

例句：
  • I used to play the guitar. — 我以前弹吉他（现在不弹）。
  • She used to have long hair. — 她以前留长发（状态）。
  • Didn't you use to smoke? — 你不是以前抽烟吗？
  • We didn't use to have internet. — 我们以前没有网络。
重要：
  • 没有现在时形式：当前习惯用 Present Simple（I play tennis）。
  • did 后 —— use to（无 -d）。
切勿混淆！used to do（过去习惯）vs. be used to doing（对某事习以为常，见 Unit 61）。
int_u18.exc:
没有现在形式：used to 仅指过去。
切勿混淆：be/get used to + -ing = 习惯于（I'm used to getting up early）。
mistakes: I use to play tennis when I was young. → I used to play tennis… —— 形式为 used to。
I am used to smoke. → I used to smoke. —— 表过去习惯。
Did you used to live there? → Did you use to live there? —— did 后用 use to。

int_u19.text: 第 19 单元 – 未来的现在、连续和简单
int_u19.note: 明天我要去见她。火车八点出发。
int_u19.exp:
Unit 19 — 用现在时表达将来。

Present Continuous —— 个人计划和约定：已安排好/与人约定的事。
  • I'm meeting Sarah at 6. — 6点与 Sarah 见面（已约）。
  • We're flying to Madrid on Monday. — 周一飞马德里（票已订）。
Present Simple —— 时刻表、节目表、日程（不依赖说话人）：火车、电影、课程。
  • The train leaves at 7:45. — 火车7:45开。
  • The film starts at 8. — 电影8点开始。
  • Term begins on September 1st. — 学期9月1日开始。
与 going to 对比：Present Continuous 强调约定，going to 强调意图。常可互换。状态动词（be, know, need）—— 用一般时。
int_u19.exc: 状态动词 → Present Simple：I need help tomorrow（不是 I'm needing）。
mistakes: I meet her tomorrow. → I'm meeting her tomorrow. —— 个人计划 → 进行时。
The train is leaving at 8 every day. → The train leaves at 8 every day. —— 时刻表 → 一般时。
What you doing tonight? → What are you doing tonight? —— 需要 are。

int_u20.note: 我要更加努力地学习。天要下雪了。
int_u20.exp:
Unit 20 — be going to 表达意图和有明显迹象的预测。

公式：am/is/are + going to + V1。

两种含义：
  • 意图（已决定）：I'm going to quit my job next month. — 我打算辞职。
  • 基于明显证据的预测：Look at those clouds — it's going to rain! — 看那些云 —— 要下雨了！
过去 —— was/were going to（曾计划但未做）：I was going to call you, but I forgot. — 我本想打电话，但忘了。

例句：
  • She's going to have a baby. — 她要生孩子了（显然/已计划）。
  • We're going to visit Spain this summer.
风格：代替 be going to go/come，常用 Present Continuous：I'm going to the cinema。
int_u20.exc: 常用 Present Continuous 代替 be going to go/come：I'm going to the party（不是 I'm going to go）。
mistakes: Look — it going to rain! → Look — it's going to rain! —— 需要 is。
I going to study tonight. → I'm going to study tonight. —— 助动词 am。
She's going quit her job. → She's going to quit her job. —— 不定式前需 to。

int_u21.note: 我会帮助你。明天会很冷。
int_u21.exp:
Unit 21 — will/shall 表达临时决定、承诺、请求和无明显迹象的预测。

公式：will/'ll + V1；否定 won't；疑问 Will + S + V1?

含义：
  • 临时决定：The phone's ringing — I'll get it! — 我来接！
  • 承诺：I'll call you tomorrow. I promise.
  • 预测（意见）：I think Brazil will win.
  • 提议/拒绝：I'll help you / The door won't open.
重要规则：在 if, when, as soon as, before, after, until 后 —— 绝不用 will，改用现在时：When he arrives, I'll tell him.
Shall（英式，美式罕用）—— 与 I/we 连用表提议：Shall I open the window? Shall we dance?
对比：will = 当下决定；going to = 早先决定。
int_u21.exc: 在 if, when, as soon as, before, after 后 —— 绝不用 will：When he comes, I'll tell him（不是 when he will come）。
mistakes: I'll call you when I will arrive. → …when I arrive. —— 时间从句禁用 will。
I think it'll rain probably. → I think it will probably rain. —— 副词位于 will 与动词之间。
He'll to help us. → He'll help us. —— will 后用不带 to 的不定式。

int_u22.text: 第 22 单元 – 意愿/应：特征（第 2 课）
int_u22.note: 我要打开窗户吗？你会在那里吗？
int_u22.exp:
Unit 22 — will/shall 的功能：提议帮助、请求、同意、拒绝。

Shall I/we…? —— 提议帮助或共同建议：
  • Shall I carry your bag? — 要我提包吗？
  • Shall we go to the cinema? — 我们去看电影吧？
Will you…? —— 请求或邀请：
  • Will you close the door, please? — 请关门好吗？
  • Will you have some tea? — 喝茶吗？
物品拒绝（won't）：The car won't start. — 车发动不起来。

坚定承诺/威胁：I'll be there, don't worry. / You'll regret this.

重要：shall 与 you/he/she/they 连用显得古旧；请求用 will/would。美式中 shall 几乎不用。这些形式后 —— 用不带 to 的不定式。
int_u22.exc: Shall 与 I/we 连用表提议和疑问；美式英语几乎不用。
mistakes: Shall you open the window? → Will you open the window? / Shall I open it? —— shall 只与 I/we 连用。
I'll to help you. → I'll help you. —— will + V，无 to。
Will I carry your bag? → Shall I carry your bag? —— 提议帮助 —— shall I。

int_u23.text: 第 23 单元 – 意志与将要
int_u23.exp:
Unit 23 — will vs. going to 的关键区别。

will —— 说话当下的决定：
  • A: We need milk. B: OK, I'll buy some. — 刚刚决定。
going to —— 说话前的决定：
  • A: Why are you going to the shop? B: I'm going to buy milk. — 早已计划。
预测：
  • will —— 意见/感觉，无证据：I think she'll like the gift.
  • going to —— 有明显依据：She's going to have a baby（能看出）/ The sky is dark — it's going to rain.
测试问题：「我是刚刚才决定的吗？」—— will。「我之前就想过吗？」—— going to。
天气预报主播：will（正式预报）或 going to（明显迹象）。
int_u23.exc: 基于意见（无证据）的预测 —— 用 will：I think she'll win. 基于明显证据的预测 —— 用 going to：Look at the clouds — it's going to rain.
mistakes: The phone's ringing. I'm going to answer it. → I'll answer it. —— 临时决定 → will。
I've decided: I will buy a new car. → I'm going to buy a new car. —— 已决定 → going to。
Look at the sky! It will rain. → It's going to rain. —— 有证据 → going to。

int_u24.text: 第 24 单元 - 将来进行时和将来完成时
int_u24.note: 我八点开始工作，那时我就完成了。
int_u24.exp:
Unit 24 — Future Continuous 和 Future Perfect。

Future Continuous（will be + V-ing）—— 将来某时刻持续的动作，或计划/礼貌询问。
  • This time tomorrow I'll be flying to Rome. — 明天这时我将在飞往罗马。
  • Will you be using your car tonight? — 你今晚会用车吗？（礼貌询问）。
Future Perfect（will have + V3）—— 到将来某时刻已完成的动作。
  • By 2030, we will have moved. — 到2030年我们已搬家。
  • She'll have finished the report by Friday.
信号词：by + 时间, by the time… → Perfect；at + 时间, this time next… → Continuous。
Future Perfect Continuous（will have been V-ing）—— 强调时长的少见形式：By June I'll have been working here for 10 years.
int_u24.exc: Future Perfect Continuous（will have been doing）少见 —— 用以强调时长：By next month I'll have been working here for 10 years.
mistakes: At 10 tomorrow I will sleep. → At 10 tomorrow I'll be sleeping. —— 具体时刻 —— 进行时。
By Friday I'll finish the report. → By Friday I'll have finished the report. —— 截至某期限 —— Perfect。
I will been working. → I will have been working. —— 需 will have been。

int_u25.text: 单元 25 - 条件类型 1（第一个条件）
int_u25.exp:
Unit 25 — First Conditional：真实/可能的将来条件。

公式：If + Present Simple, will/can/may/might + V1。主从句可调换（仅当 if 从句在前时用逗号）。

例句：
  • If it rains, we'll stay home. — 若下雨，我们就待家里。
  • You'll pass the exam if you study. — 若你学习就能过考试。
  • If you need help, call me.（主句用祈使句）。
铁律：在 if, when, as soon as, unless, until, before, after 后 —— 绝不用 will。

连词替代：
  • unless = if…not：Unless you hurry, you'll be late.
  • as long as / provided (that) —— 只要：I'll lend it as long as you return it.
  • in case —— 以防：Take an umbrella in case it rains.
int_u25.exc:
在 if 后 —— 绝不用 will。
Unless = if…not：Unless you hurry, you'll be late.
mistakes: If it will rain, we'll stay home. → If it rains, we'll stay home. —— if 后无 will。
If you will call me, I answer. → If you call me, I'll answer. —— will 只用于主句。
Unless you don't hurry, you'll be late. → Unless you hurry… —— unless 已含否定。

int_u26.text: 第 26 单元 - 可以、可以、能够
int_u26.note: 我会游泳。我得以逃脱。我一定能来。
int_u26.exp:
Unit 26 — 表达能力：can, could, be able to。

can —— 现在的能力/可能：I can speak French. 形式 can't/cannot。
could —— 过去的一般能力（常能做到）：I could swim when I was 5.
was/were able to —— 过去特定情境中的一次成功：The door was locked but I was able to open it. — 门锁着但我成功打开了。

be able to —— 以下情形的唯一形式：
  • 将来：I'll be able to help tomorrow.
  • 完成时：I haven't been able to contact her.
  • 情态后：You must be able to drive.
  • 不定式：I want to be able to speak Spanish.
否定时 couldn't 也可用于一次性情形：I couldn't open the door. = I wasn't able to.
int_u26.exc:
过去一次性成功 —— 只用 was/were able to，不用 could：I was able to escape（不是 I could escape）。
否定时两种形式皆可：couldn't / wasn't able to。
mistakes: Yesterday I could finally find my keys. → Yesterday I was able to find my keys. —— 一次性成功。
She can to swim. → She can swim. —— 情态后不带 to。
I will can help you. → I will be able to help you. —— will 后用 be able to。

int_u27.text: 第 27 单元 – 可以（特殊含义）
int_u27.note: 你能帮忙吗？我可能是错的。
int_u27.exp:
Unit 27 — could 的补充含义。

1. 礼貌请求：Could you pass the salt, please? — 能请您递下盐吗？
2. 理论可能性（现在/将来）：It could rain later. — 可能会下雨（= might）。
3. 建议：We could go to the beach. — 我们可以去海滩。
4. Could + have + V3 —— 过去的可能性：
  • 错失的机会：You could have told me! — 你该告诉我的！
  • 对过去的推测：She could have missed the train. — 她可能错过火车了。
Couldn't have + V3 —— 确信过去没发生：He couldn't have known — he wasn't there.

与 can 对比：could —— 更柔和、礼貌、不那么肯定。勿混：could-请求与 could-过去 —— 看语境。
int_u27.exc: Could have + V3 = 本可以做但没做：You could have told me!
mistakes: Could you to help me? → Could you help me? —— 不带 to。
It could to rain. → It could rain. —— 情态后用不带 to 的不定式。
He could have told, but he didn't tell. → He could have told me but he didn't. —— 不重复动词。

int_u28.text: 第 28 单元 - 必须和不能：信心
int_u28.note: 她一定很累了。他不可能是认真的。
int_u28.exp:
Unit 28 — must 和 can't 表达逻辑推断（确定性）。

must —— 确信的肯定结论：「必定、一定」。
  • You must be exhausted after the flight. — 你飞行后必然筋疲力尽。
  • He must know — he was there. — 他肯定知道。
can't —— 确信的否定结论：「不可能」：
  • That can't be John — he's in Paris. — 那不可能是 John。
  • She can't be hungry — she just ate.
关于过去：
  • must have + V3：She must have left early. — 她肯定早走了。
  • can't have + V3：He can't have seen us. — 他不可能看见我们。
重要！mustn't ≠ 结论；是禁止（「不可」）。否定推断用 can't，不用 mustn't。
对比：must（逻辑，95%确信）vs. may/might（可能，50%）。
int_u28.exc: 过去的确信：must have + V3 / can't have + V3：She must have left. He can't have seen us.
mistakes: She mustn't be tired — she's smiling. → She can't be tired — she's smiling. —— mustn't 是禁止，非否定推断。
He must to be rich. → He must be rich. —— 情态后不带 to。
You must be joking yesterday. → You must have been joking yesterday. —— 过去用 must have。

int_u29.text: 第 29 单元 – 五月和可能（第 1 课）：可能性
int_u29.note: 可能会下雨。她可能不会来。
int_u29.exp:
Unit 29 — may 和 might 表达现在/将来的可能性。

两者皆表「可能、也许」（约40–60%）。细微差别：might 稍不那么肯定，但常可互换。

公式：
  • (+) may/might + V1
  • (−) may not / might not (mightn't) —— 没有 mayn't 缩写！
  • (?) 罕见 —— 常用 Do you think…?
例句：
  • She may/might be at home. — 她可能在家。
  • It may/might rain tomorrow.
  • I might go to the party, I'm not sure.
may —— 也表正式许可：You may leave now.

情态对比：
  • will —— 100%，must —— 95%，may/might —— 50%，could —— 理论可能。
may/might 后 —— 用不带 to 的不定式，人称不变。
int_u29.exc:
没有 mayn't 缩写。用 may not / might not / mightn't。
May 表许可：You may leave.
mistakes: He mays come. → He may come. —— 情态动词不随人称变化。
She might to call. → She might call. —— 不带 to。
It mayn't rain. → It may not rain. —— 无 mayn't 缩写。

int_u30.text: 第 30 单元 – 五月和可能（第 2 课）：过去
int_u30.exp:
Unit 30 — may/might + have + V3 表达对过去的推测。

公式：may/might (not) + have + V3。意为「可能（没）做过」（约50%）。

例句：
  • She's late — she might have missed the bus. — 她迟到了 —— 可能错过公交。
  • I don't know where he is. He may have gone home. — 可能回家了。
  • They may not have received the letter yet.
过去确信度量表：
  • must have（确信如此）：She must have forgotten.
  • may/might have（可能）：She might have forgotten.
  • can't/couldn't have（确信没有）：She can't have forgotten.
might have 的特殊含义：责备/抱怨 —— 「本该…」：You might have told me! — 你本该告诉我！
int_u30.exc: Might have + V3 也表责备：You might have told me!（本该告诉我）。
mistakes: She may missed the bus. → She may have missed the bus. —— 过去 —— may have + V3。
He might have took the wrong road. → He might have taken the wrong road. —— 需要 V3。
They might been late. → They might have been late. —— 需 have been。

int_u31.text: 第 31 单元 - 必须且必须
int_u31.note: 我必须走了。 / 我得走了。
int_u31.exp:
Unit 31 — must 和 have to：必要性/义务。

must —— 内在必要性、说话人的个人信念、说话人的命令：
  • I must call my mother. — 得打电话给妈妈（我这么认为）。
  • You must see this film! — 你必须看这电影！
have to —— 外部要求：规则、环境、他人：
  • I have to wear a uniform at work. — 得穿制服（规定）。
  • She has to leave early on Fridays.
have to 的形式：(−) don't have to；(?) Do you have to…?；过去 had to，将来 will have to。

must 没有过去/将来形式 —— 用 had to / will have to。
疑问常用 have to：Do I have to go?
否定的重要区别：mustn't = 禁止（不可）；don't have to = 无必要（不必）。
int_u31.exc:
过去 —— 只用 had to（must 无过去）。
Mustn't ≠ don't have to：禁止 vs. 无必要。
mistakes: I musted work late. → I had to work late. —— 过去 —— had to。
She mustn't go — it's optional. → She doesn't have to go. —— mustn't = 不可，非「不必」。
Do I must go? → Do I have to go? / Must I go? —— must 不用 do。

int_u32.text: 第32单元-必须，不可以，不需要
int_u32.note: 你必须停下来。你不用担心。
int_u32.exp:
Unit 32 — must, mustn't, needn't / don't need to。

must —— 义务/必要：You must wear a helmet.
mustn't —— 禁止，「不可」：You mustn't smoke here.
needn't / don't need to / don't have to —— 无必要，「不必」：You needn't come early — we'll wait.

⚠️ 关键区别：mustn't vs. needn't —— 意义相反：
  • You mustn't tell anyone. — 不可告诉任何人。
  • You needn't tell anyone. — 可以不告诉（不必）。
过去：
  • needn't have + V3 —— 不必做但做了（白做）：You needn't have cooked — we ate.
  • didn't need to + V1 —— 无需去做（未做）：I didn't need to cook — we ordered pizza.
needn't —— 情态动词，不带 to；need to —— 实义动词，带 to。
int_u32.exc:
Needn't have + V3 = 本不必（却做了）：You needn't have cooked — we ate.
Didn't need to = 无需（没做）。
mistakes: You mustn't worry — it's not required. → You needn't worry. —— mustn't = 禁止。
She needn't to come. → She needn't come. —— needn't 是情态，不带 to。
You mustn't to smoke here. → You mustn't smoke here. —— 不带 to。

int_u33.text: 单元 33 – 应该（第 1 课）：提示和技巧
int_u33.exp:
Unit 33 — should 表建议、推荐、个人意见。

公式：should (not) + V1。近义词 —— ought to（较正式）。

含义：
  • 建议/推荐：You should eat more vegetables. — 你应多吃蔬菜。
  • 对正确之事的观点：The government should do more.
  • 征询建议：What should I do?
should have + V3 —— 对过去的责备/悔恨（「本该但没做」）：
  • You should have called. — 本该打电话。
  • I shouldn't have said that. — 不该那么说。
对比：should —— 柔和建议；must/have to —— 义务；could —— 一种可能方案。
常用短语：I think you should…, Do you think I should…?, You'd better（较强建议，见 Unit 35）。
int_u33.exc:
Should have + V3 = 本该做但没做（责备/悔恨）：You should have called.
Ought to —— should 的同义词，较正式。
mistakes: You should to see a doctor. → You should see a doctor. —— 不带 to。
She should went earlier. → She should have gone earlier. —— 对过去的悔恨。
You shouldn't to smoke. → You shouldn't smoke. —— 不带 to。

int_u34.text: 单元 34 – 应该（第 2 课）：期望和假设
int_u34.exp:
Unit 34 — should 表预期和假设。

含义：
  • 预期（应该如此）：She should be here by now. — 她现在应该到了。
  • 可能的结果：The package should arrive tomorrow. — 包裹应该明天到。
  • 惊讶/不满（Why should…?）：Why should I pay? — 凭什么我要付？
should have + V3 —— 本该已发生但或许未发生：The letter should have arrived yesterday. — 信昨天本该到（但…）。

正式结构在 It's strange/surprising/funny that…, I'm surprised/sorry that… 之后：It's odd that he should say that. — 他竟那么说，真奇怪。

对比：should（预期，80%）vs. must（确信，95%）vs. might（可能，50%）。
int_u34.exc: Should have + V3 = 本应已发生：The letter should have arrived by now.
mistakes: The bus must be here any minute. → The bus should be here any minute. —— 预期 —— should。
She should has finished. → She should have finished. —— should have，非 has。
The train should to arrive at 10. → The train should arrive at 10. —— 不带 to。

int_u35.text: 第 35 单元 – 是时候了，你最好
int_u35.note: 是时候该走了。你最好快点。
int_u35.exp:
Unit 35 — It's time 和 had better。

It's time + 带 to 的不定式 —— 该（一般）：It's time to go.
It's (high/about) time + 主语 + Past Simple —— 早该（责备动作延误）：It's time you went to bed. — 你早该睡了。虚拟语气，现在意义却用 Past Simple！

had better ('d better) + V1 —— 最好（带负面后果警告的建议）：
  • You'd better hurry or you'll miss the train. — 你最好快点。
  • You'd better not tell her. — 最好别告诉她。
had better 的特点：
  • 指具体当下/近期情境，非一般建议。
  • 形式永远 had，但意义是现在/将来。
  • 否定：'d better not（非 hadn't better）。
  • 比 should 更强：暗含威胁/问题。
int_u35.exc:
It's time 接主语后用 Past Simple：It's time you went（而非 you go）。
Had better —— 只用于现在/将来，不用于过去。
mistakes: It's time you go to bed. → It's time you went to bed. —— 带主语用 Past Simple。
You'd better to hurry. → You'd better hurry. —— 不带 to。
You better not smoke. → You'd better not smoke. —— 需 'd（had）。

int_u36.exp:
Unit 36 — would 的多种功能。

1. 礼貌提议和愿望：
  • Would you like some coffee? — 要咖啡吗？
  • I'd like to book a table. — 我想订桌。
2. 假设/虚构（类型2）：
  • If I had money, I would travel more.
  • What would you do? — 你会怎么做？
3. 过去的反复动作（= used to，但仅指动作）：
  • As a child, I would spend hours reading. — 小时候我常看几小时书。
4. 过去的拒绝（过去的 won't）：The car wouldn't start. — 车发动不起来。

公式：would + V1；would have + V3（类型3和假设的过去）。
⚠️过去的状态 —— 只用 used to 或 Past Simple：I had a dog（不是 I would have）。would like 后 —— 用带 to 的不定式。
int_u36.exc:
Would like + 带 to 的不定式（非 -ing）：I'd like to go.
Would 表过去习惯 —— 只指动作（非状态）：I had a bike（不是 I would have）。
mistakes: Would you like going? → Would you like to go? —— would like 后用带 to 的不定式。
I would to travel more. → I'd like to travel more. —— 需要 like。
When I was a child I would have a dog. → …I had a dog. —— would 不表状态。

int_u37.exp:
Unit 37 — can, could, would, may 表请求与许可。

请求做事（能否做？）：
  • Can you help me? —— 中性。
  • Could you help me? —— 更礼貌。
  • Would you help me? —— 礼貌/正式。
  • Would you mind + V-ing? —— 最礼貌：Would you mind closing the window?
请求许可（我能否？）：
  • Can I…? —— 非正式。
  • Could I…? —— 礼貌。
  • May I…? —— 正式：May I come in?
回答：Sure / Of course / Certainly / No problem / I'm afraid not.
⚠️ Would you mind 的重要点：同意 = No, not at all / No, of course not（= 「我不介意」）。答 Yes 意为「我介意」。Would you mind 后用动名词（-ing），非不定式。
int_u37.exc:
Would you mind 后用动名词：Would you mind opening the window?
对 Would you mind…? 的肯定回答 = No, not at all（= 我不介意）。
mistakes: Would you mind to open the window? → Would you mind opening the window? —— 需 -ing。
Could you to help? → Could you help? —— 不带 to。
Would you mind? — Yes, of course (想答应). → No, not at all. —— yes 表「介意」。

int_u38.text: 单元 38 - 条件类型 1 和 2（如果我这样做/如果我这样做）
int_u38.note: 如果我见到她，我会告诉她。如果我很有钱的话……
int_u38.exp:
Unit 38 —— First 与 Second Conditional 比较。

类型1（真实/可能的将来）：If + Present Simple, will + V1
  • If I see her, I'll tell her. — 若我见到她就告诉她。
类型2（不真实/不太可能的现在或将来）：If + Past Simple, would + V1
  • If I were rich, I would travel the world. — 若我有钱就周游世界。
  • If I knew her number, I'd call her（但不知）。
关键区别 —— 可能性：
  • 类型1：真实情境，可能发生。
  • 类型2：假设，现在不真或不太可能。
虚拟 were 在类型2中用于所有人称：If I were you…（我若是你）—— 标准劝告。口语中 was 可接受。if 后绝不用 will/would。
int_u38.exc:
类型2中 was 常用 were 替代，适用所有人称：If I were you…
if 后不用 will/would。
mistakes: If I was you, I'd study. → If I were you, I'd study. —— 正式用 were。
If I would have time, I would help. → If I had time, I would help. —— if 后无 would。
If he comes, I would tell him. → If he comes, I'll tell him. / If he came, I would tell him. —— 类型匹配。

int_u39.text: 单元 39 – 条件类型 2 和我希望
int_u39.exp:
Unit 39 — I wish 结构表达不真实的现在（= 类型2）。

公式：I wish + 主语 + Past Simple/were —— 对现状的遗憾，希望现在有所不同。

例句：
  • I wish I knew the answer. — 我不知道答案，真可惜。
  • I wish I had more time. — 要是我有更多时间。
  • I wish she were here. — 真遗憾她不在。
  • He wishes he could speak Spanish. — 真可惜他不会西班牙语。
近义 —— If only：表达相同含义但更有情感：If only I knew!

重要：
  • I wish 后 —— 用 Past Simple（非现在时！）表不真实的现在。
  • I wish I were（正式）或 I wish I was（口语）。
  • 情态：can → could。不能 I wish I will/would（对自己）。
int_u39.exc:
I wish I were…（正式）或 I wish I was…（口语）。
I wish 后表现在 —— 用 Past Simple（非现在时）。
mistakes: I wish I have a car. → I wish I had a car. —— 不真实的现在 —— Past Simple。
I wish I am taller. → I wish I were taller. —— 虚拟 were。
I wish I can fly. → I wish I could fly. —— can → could。

int_u40.text: Unit 40 - Conditional Type 3 (If I had done)
int_u40.note: If I had studied, I would have passed.
int_u40.exp:
Unit 40 — Third Conditional：不真实的过去。

公式：If + Past Perfect, would/could/might have + V3。谈论过去未发生之事及想象的后果。

例句：
  • If I had studied, I would have passed. — 若我学习就及格了（但没学，没过）。
  • If you hadn't helped me, I couldn't have finished.
  • She would have come if you had invited her.
混合类型（Mixed Conditional）：过去条件 + 现在结果或反之：
  • If I had taken the job, I would be in Tokyo now. — 若我当时接了工作（过去），现在就在东京（现在）。
  • If I were more organised, I would have finished. — 若我更有条理（性格），就会完成。
⚠️ if 后永远不用 would have。始终用 had + V3。
int_u40.exc: 混合类型：过去条件 + 现在结果：If I had studied, I would know it now.
mistakes: If I would have known, I would have helped. → If I had known, I would have helped. —— if 后用 had + V3。
If she had came, we would have seen her. → If she had come, we would have seen her. —— come 的 V3 是 come。
If you listened, you would pass.（过去）→ If you had listened, you would have passed. —— 完整类型3。

int_u41.text: 单元 41 – 随心所欲的建造
int_u41.note: 我希望我能飞。我希望他没有这样做。
int_u41.exp:
Unit 41 — wish 和 if only 的所有结构。

1. wish + Past Simple —— 对现在遗憾：I wish I had a car. — 真遗憾没车。
2. wish + Past Perfect —— 对过去遗憾：I wish I hadn't said that. — 真遗憾那样说了。
3. wish + would —— 对他人行为不满或盼变化：
  • I wish you would stop smoking. — 真希望你戒烟。
  • I wish it would stop raining.
4. wish + could —— 希望具备能力：
  • I wish I could help. — 我真希望能帮忙。
重要规则：
  • I wish I would… —— 不可（对自己 —— 只用 could）。
  • wish + would —— 关于令人烦的行为，或外部变化。
If only —— 相同公式，语气更情感化/戏剧化：If only I had listened!
int_u41.exc: I wish + would 不能对自己：不能 I wish I would… —— 用 could。
mistakes: I wish I would be taller. → I wish I were taller. —— 对自己不用 would。
I wish I didn't say that yesterday. → I wish I hadn't said that yesterday. —— 过去用 Past Perfect。
If only he will stop. → If only he would stop. —— 需要 would。

int_u42.text: 第 42 单元 - 被动语态，基础知识（第 1 课）
int_u42.note: 窗户被打破了。奶酪是由牛奶制成的。
int_u42.exp:
Unit 42 — 被动语态基础：关注动作/对象，而非执行者。

公式：to be（相应时态）+ V3。

主要时态：
  • Present Simple：is/are + V3：Coffee is grown in Brazil.
  • Past Simple：was/were + V3：The house was built in 1890.
  • Future Simple：will be + V3：The results will be announced tomorrow.
by-短语只在执行者重要时使用：Hamlet was written by Shakespeare. 否则省略：My wallet was stolen（被谁偷无关紧要）。

被动使用时机：
  • 执行者未知或不重要。
  • 动作比执行者更重要。
  • 科技和正式文本。
⚠️不及物动词（arrive, happen, go, sleep, come, exist）不能构成被动。规则动词 —— -ed；不规则 —— V3 表。
int_u42.exc:
不及物动词（arrive, happen, sleep）不能用被动。
不规则动词的 V3 须熟记：break/broken, write/written, take/taken。
mistakes: The window broke by the kids. → The window was broken by the kids. —— 需要 be + V3。
He was stole the money. → The money was stolen. —— 被动针对对象。
The accident was happened yesterday. → The accident happened yesterday. —— happen 不及物。

int_u43.text: 单元 43 – 所有时态的被动语态（第 2 课）
int_u43.note: 正在制作/已被发现/将要建造
int_u43.exp:
Unit 43 —— 所有时态及情态动词的被动。

通则：be 随时态变化，V3 不变。

全表：
  • Present Simple：is made
  • Present Continuous：is being made
  • Present Perfect：has been made
  • Past Simple：was made
  • Past Continuous：was being made
  • Past Perfect：had been made
  • Future：will be made
  • Future Perfect：will have been made
情态 + 被动：modal + be + V3：must be done, can be fixed, should be repaired, might be lost。

例句：
  • The road is being repaired. — 路在修。
  • The letter has been sent. — 信已寄。
  • This can't be true.（情态 + be 的主动形式）。
⚠️ Perfect Continuous 和 Future Continuous 的被动罕见。
int_u43.exc: 情态被动：modal + be + V3：must be done, can be seen, should be repaired.
mistakes: The road is repairing now. → The road is being repaired now. —— 进行时被动 = is being + V3。
The letter has sent. → The letter has been sent. —— 完成时被动 = has been + V3。
It must done. → It must be done. —— 情态后 —— be + V3。

int_u44.text: 单元 44 – 被动语态：特殊情况（第 3 课）
int_u44.note: 有人告诉我。他得到了一本书。
int_u44.exp:
Unit 44 —— 双宾语动词的被动。

动词 give, tell, show, offer, ask, send, teach, pay, promise, lend 有两个宾语（给谁 + 什么）。被动中任一宾语都可做主语：

主动：They gave Anna a present.
被动1（较常见）：Anna was given a present. — Anna 收到礼物。
被动2：A present was given to Anna.（需介词 to）。

更多例子：
  • I was told the truth. — 告诉我实情。
  • He was offered a job. — 他被提供工作。
  • She was shown the results.
⚠️ 特殊动词：
  • explain, describe, suggest, say, mention —— 人不能作被动主语。不能 I was explained → It was explained to me.
频率选择：母语者更常用人（human subject）作被动主语，因话题是人。
int_u44.exc: explain 不能以人作被动主语：不可 I was explained —— 只能 It was explained to me.
mistakes: I was explained the rule. → The rule was explained to me. / I was told the rule. —— explain 特殊。
A book was given me. → A book was given to me. / I was given a book. —— 需要 to。
He was offered by a job. → He was offered a job. —— 直接宾语前不加 by。

int_u45.text: 第 45 单元 – 据说……/据说他是……
int_u45.note: 据说他很有钱。据说他很有钱。
int_u45.exp:
Unit 45 — 被动报道结构：It is said that… / He is said to…。

意见/报告动词：say, think, believe, report, know, expect, consider, understand, assume。用于非人称结构，传达普遍观点、传闻、新闻。

同义两种表达：
  • It is said that he is very rich.（非人称）
  • He is said to be very rich.（人称 + 不定式）
公式：
  • It + is/was + said + that + 从句
  • 主语 + is/was + said + 带 to 的不定式
现在 vs. 过去：
  • 现在：He is believed to live in Spain.
  • 过去：He is believed to have lived in Spain.（完成不定式）
例句：
  • The painting is thought to be genuine.
  • She is known to have been a spy.
int_u45.exc: 此结构的过去：He is said to have been —— 完成不定式：She is believed to have left the country.
mistakes: He is said he is rich. → He is said to be rich. / It is said that he is rich. —— 正确结构。
She is known has been rich. → She is known to have been rich. —— 需 to have + V3。
It said that he is clever. → It is said that he is clever. —— 需 is。

int_u46.exp:
Unit 46 — 使役结构 have/get something done：雇人做事。

公式：have/get + 宾语 + V3。

含义：我们不亲自做 —— 安排别人代劳（或某事发生在我们身上）。

例句：
  • I had my hair cut. — 我剪了头发（去了理发店）。
  • She's having her house painted. — 她在请人给房子刷漆。
  • Where do you get your car serviced? — 你在哪里保养车？
  • I need to get my eyes tested. — 我得验光。
have vs. get：意义相同；get 更口语。

第二含义 —— 不幸：He had his wallet stolen. — 他钱包被偷。

对比：
  • I repaired my car — 亲自修。
  • I had my car repaired — 找人修。
⚠️ 顺序：宾语严格在 V3 之前。
int_u46.exc:
口语中 get something done = have something done：I got my hair cut.
习语中 have 表不幸事件：He had his wallet stolen.
mistakes: I cut my hair yesterday.（其实去了理发店） → I had my hair cut yesterday. —— 非亲自做。
She is having painted her house. → She is having her house painted. —— 顺序：have + 宾语 + V3。
I had repaired my car.（安排）→ I had my car repaired. —— 词序。

int_u47.text: 第 47 单元 - 报告演讲（第 1 课）
int_u47.note: 她说她累了。他告诉我他会来。
int_u47.exp:
Unit 47 — 间接引语：时态变化规则。

主要时态变化（引导动词为过去时）：
  • Present Simple → Past Simple
  • Present Continuous → Past Continuous
  • Present Perfect → Past Perfect
  • Past Simple → Past Perfect
  • will → would, can → could, may → might, must → had to
代词和状语变化：
  • I → he/she, my → his/her, we → they
  • today → that day, tomorrow → the next day, yesterday → the day before, here → there, this → that
例句：
  • "I'm tired." → She said (that) she was tired.
  • "I'll call you." → He said he would call me.
say vs. tell：
  • say (to smb) that…：She said that she was late.
  • tell + 某人 + that…：She told me that she was late. 不能 tell that。
若所述仍真实，可不变时态：She said the Earth is round.
int_u47.exc:
若所述仍真实，时态变化可选：She said the Earth is round.
tell 后须指明对象：She told me…
mistakes: She said me she was tired. → She told me she was tired. / She said she was tired. —— say 后不接对象。
He said he will come. → He said he would come. —— 时态变化：will → would。
She told that she was late. → She said that she was late. / She told me that… —— tell 需要宾语。

int_u48.text: 单元 48 - 报告演讲（第 2 课）：问题和命令
int_u48.note: 他问我住在哪里。她让我等一下。
int_u48.exp:
Unit 48 — 间接疑问、命令、请求。

间接疑问：
  • 特殊疑问：ask + wh-词 + 陈述语序："Where do you live?" → He asked where I lived.
  • 一般疑问（yes/no）：ask + if/whether + 陈述语序："Are you ready?" → She asked if I was ready.
⚠️间接疑问中：
  • 无倒装（where I lived，非 where did I live）。
  • 无 do/does/did。
  • 无问号。
  • 有时态变化。
间接命令/请求：tell/ask/order/warn + 宾语 + (not) 带 to 的不定式：
  • "Wait!" → She told me to wait.
  • "Please help me." → He asked me to help him.
  • "Don't go." → She told me not to go.
引导动词：tell, ask, order, beg, advise, warn, remind, invite, encourage。
int_u48.exc: 间接疑问用陈述语序 —— 无 do/does/did：He asked where I lived（不是 where did I live）。
mistakes: He asked where did I live. → He asked where I lived. —— 间接疑问用陈述语序。
She asked me wait. → She told me to wait. / She asked me to wait. —— 需要带 to 的不定式。
He asked if am I ready. → He asked if I was ready. —— 陈述语序 + 时态变化。

int_u49.text: 第 49 单元 - 所有问题类型（第 1 课）
int_u49.note: 你呢？她是吗？他们有吗？会吗？
int_u49.exp:
Unit 49 —— 各种类型疑问句的构建。

一般疑问（yes/no）：助动词/情态动词与主语倒装。
  • Are you ready?
  • Do you speak French?
  • Has she arrived?
  • Can he swim?
特殊疑问：wh-词 + 助 + 主 + 动：
  • Where do you live?
  • What is she doing?
  • When did he leave?
⚠️ 对主语提问 —— 无倒装，无 do/does/did：
  • Who called?（不是 Who did call?）
  • What happened?
  • Which bus goes to the centre?
带介词的疑问：介词通常在句末：Who are you waiting for? What are you talking about? Where does she come from?
否定疑问（Don't you know? Isn't it strange?）表惊讶或邀请同意。
int_u49.exc: 对主语提问无倒装，无 do/does：Who called? What happened?
mistakes: Who did call you? → Who called you? —— 对主语提问不用 did。
Where she lives? → Where does she live? —— 需倒装 does。
What means this word? → What does this word mean? —— 需 does。

int_u50.text: 第 50 单元 - 简单句和复杂句中的问题（第 2 课）
int_u50.exp:
Unit 50 — 复合句中的嵌入（间接）疑问。

在引导语 Do you know…?, Can you tell me…?, I wonder…, I'd like to know…, Have you any idea…?, Could you tell me…? 之后 —— 用陈述语序，无倒装，无 do/does/did。

对比：
  • 直接：Where does she live?
  • 嵌入：Do you know where she lives?
  • 直接：What is the time?
  • 嵌入：Can you tell me what the time is?
yes/no 疑问用 if/whether：
  • I wonder if he is coming.
  • Do you know whether they are open?
例句：
  • Have you any idea how much this costs?
  • I'd like to know when the meeting starts.
⚠️此规则同样适用于间接引语（Unit 48）。只有整句为疑问句时才加问号。
int_u50.exc:
在 I wonder, Do you know, Can you tell me 后 —— 用陈述语序。
yes/no 疑问 —— 用 if/whether：I wonder if she is coming.
mistakes: Do you know where does she live? → Do you know where she lives? —— 陈述语序。
Can you tell me what is the time? → Can you tell me what the time is? —— 无倒装。
I wonder does he know. → I wonder if he knows. —— 需要 if/whether。

int_u51.text: 单元 51 - 句子中的助动词
int_u51.note: 我也是，她也不能。我很喜欢它！
int_u51.exp:
Unit 51 — 助动词：简短回答、表同意、强调、避免重复。

1. 简短回答：使用问句中的助动词。
  • Are you tired? — Yes, I am. / No, I'm not.
  • Has she left? — Yes, she has.
2. So/Neither + 助动词 + 主语 —— 简短表同意/反对：
  • I'm tired. — So am I. — 我也是。
  • I can't swim. — Neither can I. / Nor can I.
3. 强调性 do/does/did —— 肯定句加强：
  • I do love you! — 我真的爱你！
  • She does know the answer.
  • He did call, I swear.
4. 避免重复：
  • I haven't read the book, but Jane has.
  • A: Who can cook? B: I can.
⚠️ So/Neither 要倒装（助在主前）。Me too / Me neither 是口语形式。
int_u51.exc: So am I / Neither am I —— 倒装。Me too / Me neither —— 口语变体。
mistakes: I like coffee. — So I do. → So do I. —— 倒装：so + 助 + 主。
I don't like it. — Neither I do. → Neither do I. —— 正确顺序。
I do liked it. → I did like it. / I do like it. —— 强调性 do/did + V1。

int_u52.text: 单元 52 — 标签问题：划分问题
int_u52.note: 天气很冷，不是吗？她没有打电话，是吗？
int_u52.exp:
Unit 52 — 反意疑问句（tag questions）用于确认或核实。

规则：肯定句 → 否定尾巴；否定句 → 肯定尾巴。

公式：助动词 + (n't) + 主语代词。

例句：
  • You're tired, aren't you?
  • She can swim, can't she?
  • He hasn't called, has he?
  • They don't know, do they?
特殊情形：
  • I am → aren't I?（非 amn't I）：I'm late, aren't I?
  • Let's → shall we?：Let's go, shall we?
  • 祈使句 → will you / won't you / would you：Close the door, will you?
  • nothing, nobody, no one 后 —— 用 it/they：Nobody called, did they?
语调：
  • 下降 ↓ —— 说话人确信，等待同意。
  • 上升 ↑ —— 真实疑问，不确定。
int_u52.exc:
I am → aren't I?
Let's → shall we?
祈使 → will you / won't you?
mistakes: I'm late, amn't I? → I'm late, aren't I? —— I am 的例外。
Let's go, will we? → Let's go, shall we? —— let's 后用 shall we。
She can swim, can she? → She can swim, can't she? —— 肯定 → 否定尾巴。

int_u53.text: 单元 53 - 动词后的动名词
int_u53.note: 我喜欢游泳。她工作结束了。
int_u53.exp:
Unit 53 —— 后接动名词（-ing）的动词。

关键动词（+ -ing）：enjoy, finish, stop, mind, avoid, suggest, recommend, admit, deny, consider, imagine, risk, practise, postpone, delay, keep, give up, put off, can't stand, can't help, miss, feel like。

例句：
  • I enjoy reading. — 我喜欢读书。
  • She suggested going for a walk. — 她建议去散步。
  • He denied stealing the money. — 他否认偷钱。
  • I can't help laughing. — 忍不住笑。
  • Would you mind waiting?
动名词否定：not + V-ing：She admitted not telling the truth.

常见错误：特别注意 suggest 和 recommend —— 只用 -ing，不用带 to 不定式：He suggested going（不是 to go）。
动名词作名词：可作主语：Swimming is good for you.
int_u53.exc: suggest, recommend 后 —— 只用动名词（非带 to 不定式）：He suggested going by train.
mistakes: I enjoy to swim. → I enjoy swimming. —— enjoy 后用动名词。
She suggested to go to the cinema. → She suggested going to the cinema. —— suggest 后 —— -ing。
I avoid to speak to him. → I avoid speaking to him. —— avoid 后 —— 动名词。

int_u54.text: 单元 54 - 动词后不定式（第一课）
int_u54.exp:
Unit 54 —— 后接带 to 不定式的动词。

关键动词（+ to-inf）：want, need, hope, plan, decide, expect, agree, refuse, offer, promise, threaten, manage, learn, afford, pretend, seem, appear, arrange, tend, fail。

例句：
  • I want to go home. — 我想回家。
  • She decided to stay. — 她决定留下。
  • They promised to help. — 他们答应帮忙。
  • He managed to escape. — 他设法逃脱。
  • We can't afford to buy a new car.
否定：not + 带 to 不定式：He decided not to go.

Help —— 特殊：可带 to 也可不带：help (to) carry。
⚠️ 与 Unit 53 对比：需记住哪些动词接哪种形式。有些两种皆可（见 Unit 56/57）。
int_u54.exc:
Hope, decide, plan, manage —— 只用带 to 不定式。
help 后 to 可有可无：help (to) do。
mistakes: She decided going. → She decided to go. —— decide 后 —— to-inf。
I hope seeing you. → I hope to see you. —— hope 后 —— to-inf。
He managed escaping. → He managed to escape. —— manage 后 —— to-inf。

int_u55.text: 单元 55 - 动词后的不定式（第 2 课）
int_u55.note: 她让我等一下。他请她不要走。
int_u55.exp:
Unit 55 — 结构 动词 + 宾语 + 带 to 不定式（动词 + 谁 + 做什么）。

关键动词：want, ask, tell, expect, allow, advise, encourage, order, remind, warn, invite, persuade, force, teach, help, enable。

例句：
  • She asked me to wait. — 她让我等。
  • They expect us to be on time.
  • He persuaded her to come.
  • I warned you not to go there.（否定：not to）
  • She taught me to drive.
⚠️ 例外（不带 to 不定式）：
  • make smb do sth —— 迫使：She made me laugh.
  • let smb do sth —— 允许：Let me think.
但被动时 make 要加 to：I was made to wait。let 被动用 allowed：I was allowed to leave.

否定：I want you not to worry. 不可混 want that…（不用！）。
int_u55.exc:
make, let 后 —— 不带 to 不定式：She made me wait. Let me go.
但被动：I was made to wait.
mistakes: She made me to wait. → She made me wait. —— make 后不带 to。
He let me to go. → He let me go. —— let 后不带 to。
I want that you come. → I want you to come. —— 结构 want smb to do。

int_u56.text: 单元 56 - 不定式或动名词：一般规则
int_u56.note: 我喜欢游泳/我喜欢游泳 - 区别？
int_u56.exp:
Unit 56 —— 同时允许不定式和动名词的动词。

1. 意义无差别（begin, start, continue, intend, propose, bother）：
  • It started raining / to rain.
2. 细微差别（like, love, hate, prefer）：
  • like + -ing —— 喜欢过程：I like swimming（享受过程）。
  • like + to-inf —— 认为有益/正确：I like to exercise in the morning（习惯，认为重要）。
  • 美式英语更常用 to-inf。
3. would like/love/hate/prefer 后 —— 只用带 to 不定式：
  • I'd like to go to Japan.
  • She'd love to meet you.
例句：
  • I love cooking（过程）vs. I'd love to cook for you tonight（具体）。
⚠️ like = 感到快乐 —— 动名词；like = 认为好 —— 带 to 不定式。但现代英语这一区分越来越不严格。
int_u56.exc: would like/love/hate/prefer 后 —— 只用带 to 不定式，非动名词。
mistakes: I'd like swimming. → I'd like to swim. / I like swimming. —— would + to-inf。
I started to doing yoga. → I started doing yoga. / I started to do yoga. —— 两种皆可，但不混用。
He continued to playing. → He continued playing / to play. —— 选一种形式。

int_u57.text: 单元 57 – 尝试、需要、帮助：不定式或动名词
int_u57.note: 尝试做与尝试做/需要清洁
int_u57.exp:
Unit 57 —— 因形式不同而语义不同的动词。

remember/forget + 带 to 不定式 —— 记得/忘记去做（将来/即将）：
  • Remember to lock the door! — 记得锁门！
remember/forget + -ing —— 记得/忘记做过（过去）：
  • I remember locking the door. — 我记得锁门了。
try + 带 to 不定式 —— 努力尝试：
  • I tried to open the window, but it was stuck.
try + -ing —— 尝试（实验）：
  • Try turning it off and on again.
stop + 带 to 不定式 —— 停下来去做：
  • He stopped to smoke（停下来抽烟）。
stop + -ing —— 停止做：
  • He stopped smoking（戒烟）。
need + 带 to 不定式 —— 需要做：I need to rest.
need + -ing —— 需要被做（被动意味）：My car needs washing = needs to be washed。
regret + 带 to 不定式 —— 遗憾地通知（正式）。regret + -ing —— 后悔做过。
int_u57.exc: Need + -ing 有被动意味：My shoes need cleaning = need to be cleaned.
mistakes: I remember to lock the door yesterday. → I remember locking the door yesterday. —— 过去动作 —— -ing。
She stopped to smoke（戒烟）. → She stopped smoking. —— 停止 —— -ing。
The car needs to clean. → The car needs cleaning. / needs to be cleaned. —— 被动意味。

int_u58.text: 单元 58 – Like/would like：不定式或动名词
int_u58.note: 我喜欢游泳。 / 我想游泳。
int_u58.exp:
Unit 58 — like vs. would like 的差别。

like/love/hate/prefer + -ing（或带 to 不定式） —— 一般性偏好、习惯、总体感受：
  • I like swimming. — 我喜欢游泳（总体）。
  • She hates getting up early.
would like/love/hate/prefer + 带 to 不定式 —— 当下或将来的具体愿望（比 want 礼貌）：
  • I'd like to go home. — 我想回家（现在）。
  • Would you like to come with us? — 想和我们一起去吗？
语义对比：
  • I like dancing —— 我喜欢跳舞（总体）。
  • I'd like to dance with you —— 我想和你跳舞（现在/马上）。
⚠️ 铁律：would 后 —— 只用带 to 不定式，绝不用 -ing。
否定：I wouldn't like to live there.
提议：Would you like + 名词/带 to 不定式：Would you like tea? / Would you like to sit down?
int_u58.exc: 美式英语中 like 后更常用带 to 不定式：I like to swim.
mistakes: I would like swimming. → I would like to swim. —— would like 后 —— to-inf。
I'd love meeting her. → I'd love to meet her. —— would 后 —— to-inf。
Would you like go to the cinema? → Would you like to go to the cinema? —— 需要 to。

int_u59.note: 我更喜欢读书。我宁愿呆在家里。
int_u59.exp:
Unit 59 —— 表达偏好：prefer, would prefer, would rather。

prefer —— 一般性偏好：
  • prefer + -ing + to + -ing：I prefer reading to watching TV.
  • prefer + 名词 + to + 名词：I prefer tea to coffee.
  • prefer + 带 to 不定式 + rather than + V1：I prefer to walk rather than drive.
would prefer + 带 to 不定式 —— 具体时刻：
  • I'd prefer to stay home tonight.
would rather + V1（不带 to） —— 宁愿（具体）：
  • I'd rather stay home.
  • I'd rather not go.（否定）
  • I'd rather coffee than tea.（带名词）
would rather + 主语 + Past Simple —— 希望别人做：
  • I'd rather you didn't smoke here. — 最好你别在这抽烟。
⚠️ 记住：prefer A to B（非 than）；would rather + V1 不带 to。
int_u59.exc:
Would rather + 主语 + 过去时：I'd rather you didn't smoke（希望你别抽烟）。
Prefer A to B（非 than）：I prefer tea to coffee.
mistakes: I'd rather to stay. → I'd rather stay. —— would rather 后不带 to。
I prefer reading than watching TV. → I prefer reading to watching TV. —— prefer 搭 to。
I'd rather you don't tell her. → I'd rather you didn't tell her. —— 主语后用 Past Simple。

int_u60.text: 第 60 单元 - 介词后的动名词
int_u60.exp:
Unit 60 —— 介词后动名词：硬性规则。

规则：任何介词（in, on, at, of, for, from, about, with, without, before, after, by, to（作介词）, instead of, despite, besides）后 —— 永远用动名词（-ing），绝不用不定式。

典型结构：
  • 形容词 + 介词 + -ing：good at cooking, afraid of flying, interested in learning, tired of waiting, keen on dancing。
  • 动词 + 介词 + -ing：think about doing, succeed in passing, apologise for being late, insist on paying。
  • 名词 + 介词 + -ing：chance of winning, reason for coming。
例句：
  • She's good at solving problems.
  • Thanks for helping me.
  • He left without saying a word.
  • By practising daily, she improved.
⚠️ 特别注意 to：当 to 是不定式的一部分（want to go），用 V1。当 to 是介词（look forward to, be used to, object to, get used to），用动名词：I look forward to seeing you.
int_u60.exc: 所有介词（in, on, at, of, for, about, before, after, without, by）后 —— 只用动名词，不用不定式。
mistakes: I'm interested in to learn French. → I'm interested in learning French. —— 介词后 —— -ing。
Thanks for to help me. → Thanks for helping me. —— for 后 —— 动名词。
He left without to say goodbye. → He left without saying goodbye. —— without 后 —— -ing。

int_u61.exp:
Unit 61 — 结构 be/get used to + -ing 描述通过实践养成的习惯：动作不再显得困难或陌生。

公式：be/get used to + 名词 / 代词 / -ing。
  • I'm used to waking up early. — 我习惯早起。
  • She isn't used to the cold weather. — 她不习惯寒冷的天气。
  • He's getting used to living alone. — 他正在习惯独居。

务必区别于 used to + infinitive（过去的规律性习惯，现已不再）：I used to smoke = 我以前抽烟。而 I'm used to smoking = 我习惯身边有人抽烟。前者中的 used 是过去时动词；后者是 be 之后的形容词。信号词：get used to = 正在适应的过程；be used to = 已形成的习惯状态。
int_u61.exc: be/get used to 要求 -ing，而非不定式。Used to do（不带 be）是另一个时态（过去的习惯）。
mistakes: I'm used to wake up early. → I'm used to waking up early. — be used to 之后始终使用动名词。
I used to living here. → I'm used to living here. — 现在的习惯需要 be。
He's getting used to drive on the left. → getting used to driving — 此处 to 之后是动名词。

int_u62.text: 单元 62 - 固定表达方式中的动名词（第 1 课）
int_u62.exp:
Unit 62 — 一些带介词的动词之后始终跟动名词，因为此处的 to 或其他介词并非不定式符号，而正是介词。

表达组：
  • look forward to, object to, be/get used to — 具有欺骗性的 to 是介词：I look forward to meeting you.（期待见到你。）
  • feel like, give up, take up, carry on, go on, put off, keep on, succeed in：I don't feel like going out.（不想出门。）
  • She succeeded in passing the exam. — 她成功通过了考试。

检验方法：如果可以替换为名词（look forward to the trip），说明 to 是介词，需用 -ing。请把这些短语连同介词整体记忆。
int_u62.exc: look forward to / object to / be used to 中的 to 是介词，不是不定式符号。因此后接 -ing。
mistakes: I look forward to see you. → I look forward to seeing you. — 此处 to 是介词。
She gave up to smoke. → gave up smoking — give up 要求动名词。
I don't feel like to go out. → feel like going out — feel like 之后只接 -ing。

int_u63.text: 单元 63 – 固定表达方式中的动名词（第 2 课）
int_u63.exp:
Unit 63 — 继续本话题：动词 + 介词 + 宾语 + 动名词。介词附属于动词，动作通过 -ing 表达。

常见公式：
  • accuse somebody of + -ing：They accused him of stealing.（他们指控他盗窃。）
  • apologise for + -ing：She apologised for being late.（她为迟到道歉。）
  • insist on + -ing：I insist on paying.（我坚持付款。）
  • prevent somebody from + -ing：Rain prevented us from going out.（雨阻止了我们出门。）

此外：thank for, blame for, congratulate on, suspect of, rely on, concentrate on, dream of。这些「动词+介词」组合须作为一个整体记忆，以便自动跟动名词。
int_u63.exc: 请将介词与动词作为单一组合记忆：accuse of, apologise for, insist on, prevent from。
mistakes: She accused me to steal. → accused me of stealing — accuse of + -ing。
He apologised for be late. → apologised for being late — 介词后用动名词。
They prevented him to leave. → prevented him from leaving — 需要介词 from + 动名词。

int_u64.text: 单元 64 - 表示目的的不定式（以便）
int_u64.note: 我是来帮忙的。 / 她离开是为了不吵架。
int_u64.exp:
Unit 64 — 表达动作目的的方法：为了做什么。

简单目的（同一主语）：to-infinitive 或 in order to / so as to（正式）。
  • I called to tell you the news. — 我打电话告诉你这个消息。
  • She left early in order to catch the train. — 她早早离开以赶上火车。
否定目的：只能用 so as not to / in order not to，不能单用 not to。
  • She whispered so as not to wake the baby. — 她小声说话以免吵醒宝宝。
不同主语：so that + 主语 + can/could/will/would。
  • I'll lend you my notes so that you can study. — 我把笔记借给你，以便你学习。

For + -ing 表示物品的功能：a knife for cutting bread，而不是人的动作目的。
int_u64.exc: 否定目的只能用 so as not to / in order not to，不能单独用 not to。
mistakes: I called for tell you. → called to tell you — 目的用 to-不定式，而非 for。
She whispered not to wake the baby. → so as not to wake — 否定目的需要 so as/in order。
He left so that catch the train. → so that he could catch — so that 后需要主语和情态动词。

int_u65.text: 单元 65 - 形容词后的不定式
int_u65.note: 很高兴认识你。她很容易交谈。
int_u65.exp:
Unit 65 — 评价结构中形容词后的不定式。

公式：
  • It is + adj + to-infinitive：It's difficult to understand him.（很难理解他。）
  • It is + adj + of somebody + to-inf（关于性格）：It was kind of you to help.（你真好心来帮忙。）
  • Subject + be + adj + to-inf（对象的特性）：She's easy to talk to.（她很容易交谈。）
  • first, next, last, only 后：She was the first to arrive.（她第一个到。）

easy/hard/impossible 的特点：动作的宾语变为主语，介词保留在末尾（talk to）。Kind/nice/generous 要求 of somebody，而非 for/from。
int_u65.exc: nice/kind/generous of somebody 后跟不定式：It was kind of you to help。easy/hard + to 后宾语变主语：She's easy to talk to.
mistakes: It's difficult understanding. → It's difficult to understand. — 形容词后用不定式，而非动名词。
She's easy to talk. → easy to talk to — 不能省略介词。
It was nice from you to come. → nice of you to come — 用 of，而不是 from。

int_u66.text: Unit 66 – 害怕/感兴趣/抱歉：不定式或动名词
int_u66.exp:
Unit 66 — 既可接动名词也可接不定式但含义有所不同的形容词。

Afraid：
  • afraid of + -ing = 广义的恐惧，怕发生某事：She's afraid of flying.（害怕坐飞机。）
  • afraid to + inf = 因害怕而故意不做：She was afraid to tell him the truth.（不敢告诉。）
Interested：interested in + -ing（对活动的兴趣），interested to hear/know（具体反应）：I was interested to hear the news.
Sorry：sorry for/about + -ing（对过去的行为），sorry to do（当下的遗憾）：Sorry to disturb you. vs. Sorry for disturbing you yesterday.

选择取决于我们谈论的是当下反应还是持续的情感/过去事件。
int_u66.exc: Sorry about/for + -ing — 关于过去的行为；sorry to do — 对当下/未来的遗憾。
mistakes: I'm afraid to fly.（关于广义恐惧）→ I'm afraid of flying. — 广义恐惧要求 of + -ing。
I'm interested to learn French.（关于爱好）→ interested in learning — 对活动的兴趣：in + -ing。
Sorry to wake you up yesterday. → Sorry for waking you up — 关于过去行为：for + -ing。

int_u67.text: 单元 67 — 看到某人做或做
int_u67.note: 我看见他倒下了。 / 我看到他摔倒了。
int_u67.exp:
Unit 67 — 感官动词后，宾语可接省略 to 的不定式或 -ing。

动词：see, hear, watch, feel, notice, listen to。
  • bare infinitive — 动作从头到尾完整：I saw him cross the road.（我看见他穿过马路。）
  • -ing — 动作正在进行中，部分地：I saw him crossing the road.（看见他在穿马路。）
  • I heard her sing the whole song. vs. I heard her singing in the shower.
Make/let/have + 宾语 + 省略to的不定式：She made me wait. Let me go. I had him fix the car.
make 在被动语态中：to 回归：He was made to wait.

比较：help somebody (to) do 两种都允许。
int_u67.exc: make/let 后接省略 to 的不定式。被动语态中：He was made to wait — to 回归。
mistakes: I saw him to cross the road. → saw him cross — 感官动词后用省略 to 的不定式。
She made me to wait. → made me wait — make 后用省略to的不定式。
I heard him singing（整首歌）→ heard him sing — 完整动作用省略to的不定式。

int_u68.text: 单元 68 – 分词短语（-ing 子句）
int_u68.note: 感觉累了，我就去睡觉了。
int_u68.exp:
Unit 68 — 用 -ing 分词短语紧凑表达同时或先后动作。

类型：
  • 同时动作：Feeling tired, I went to bed.（感到疲倦，我去睡觉。）
  • 并行：She walked along the road singing to herself.（她沿路走着自言自语地唱。）
  • 原因：Being ill, he stayed at home.（因为生病，他待在家。）
  • 先发生的动作 — Having + past participle：Having finished lunch, we left.（吃完午饭后，我们离开了。）

主要规则：分词的主语必须与主句的主语一致，否则会产生荒谬（悬垂分词）。文体较书面；口语中更常用带 when/because/after 的完整从句。
int_u68.exc: 分词短语的主语必须与主句主语一致，否则产生「悬垂分词」。
mistakes: Walking in the park, the flowers were beautiful. → Walking in the park, I saw beautiful flowers. — 花不会走路；需要一致的主语。
Having finishing, she left. → Having finished, she left. — having 后用过去分词。
Being tired, so I went to bed. → Being tired, I went to bed. — 分词短语后不需要 so。

int_u69.text: 单元 69 – 可数和不可数（第 1 课）
int_u69.exp:
Unit 69 — 可数（countable）与不可数（uncountable）名词的基本区别。

可数：有复数，可与 a/an, one, two, many, few 搭配：a book → two books。
不可数：无复数，不用 a/an，可与 some, much, little, a piece of 搭配：water, music, money。
俄语中可数但英语中典型不可数的词：advice, information, news, furniture, luggage, baggage, work, progress, research, traffic, weather, bread, money。
  • I need some advice. — 我需要建议。
  • The news is bad. — 消息是坏的。（动词用单数）
  • She has a lot of luggage. — 她有很多行李。

表示单独单位：a piece of advice, a bit of news, an item of furniture。
int_u69.exc: 英语不可数但俄语可数的词：advice, information, news, furniture, luggage, progress, research。不能用 an advice, advices。
mistakes: I need an advice. → some advice / a piece of advice — advice 不可数。
The news are bad. → The news is bad. — news 需单数动词。
I bought new furnitures. → new furniture — furniture 无复数。

int_u70.text: 单元 70 – 可数和不可数（第 2 课）
int_u70.note: 头发/一根头发/一张纸/一张纸
int_u70.exp:
Unit 70 — 根据可数性改变含义的名词。

对比示例：
  • hair（整体头发）vs. a hair（一根头发）：She has long hair. There's a hair in my soup.
  • paper（纸）vs. a paper（报纸、文章、论文）：I need some paper. I read the paper.
  • experience（经验）vs. an experience（经历、事件）：She has a lot of experience. It was a strange experience.
  • time（时间）vs. times（次数）：I have no time. I've been there three times.
  • room（地方、空间）vs. a room（房间）：There isn't much room. Book a room.

另外：light, work/a work of art, glass, iron, coffee (a coffee = 一杯)。语境和冠词提示含义。
int_u70.exc: 一个词两种含义：experience（经验，不可数）vs. an experience（经历，可数）；time（时间，不可数）vs. times（次数，可数）。
mistakes: Can you give me a paper to write on? → some paper / a sheet of paper — a paper = 报纸。
I have a long hair. → long hair — 整体头发不可数。
I had many experiences in my job.（关于技能）→ a lot of experience — 经验/工龄不可数。

int_u71.text: 71 单元 – 文章 a/an 和单词 some
int_u71.note: 一本书/一些书/一些牛奶
int_u71.exp:
Unit 71 — some 在 a/an 不适用时，作为复数可数和不可数名词的「冠词」。

平行对照：
  • 单数：a book — 复数：some books
  • 不可数：some milk, some information
Some vs. any：some — 肯定句和礼貌建议/请求；any — 疑问和否定。
  • I bought some apples. — 我买了一些苹果。
  • Do you have any questions? — 有问题吗？
  • Would you like some tea? — 要茶吗？（邀请 = some）
  • Could I have some water, please? — 可以给我些水吗？（期待同意）

Any 在肯定句中意为「任何」：Take any book you like.。some 的数量不确定但真实存在。
int_u71.exc: 在礼貌建议和请求中用 some，不用 any：Would you like some tea? Could I have some water?
mistakes: I have some questions?（疑问）→ Do you have any questions? — 中性疑问用 any。
Would you like any coffee? → some coffee — 邀请用 some。
I don't have some money. → don't have any money — 否定用 any。

int_u72.text: 第 72 单元 – 第 a/an 条和
int_u72.note: 一辆车→那辆车（同样的事情）
int_u72.exp:
Unit 72 — 从 a/an 到 the 的过渡：如何确定物品的熟悉度。

首次提到（新信息）— a/an。再次提到或从语境已知 — the。
  • I saw a film yesterday. The film was great. — 我昨天看了部电影。电影很棒。
  • Can you shut the door, please? — 从情境已知的门。
直接用 the：
  • 独一无二的事物：the sun, the moon, the world, the Internet；
  • 最高级：the best, the tallest；
  • 有从句限定：the man who called。

A/an 也用于分类：She's a doctor. It's a nice day. 逻辑：a = 许多中的一个；the = 正是这个，双方都知道。
int_u72.exc: 独一无二的事物立即用 the：the sun, the moon, the sky, the Internet。首次提及不改变此规则。
mistakes: I saw the film yesterday. A film was great. → The film was great. — 重复提到用 the。
Sun is shining. → The sun is shining. — 独一无二物品需 the。
She's a best student in class. → the best student — 最高级用 the。

int_u73.text: 73 单元 - 文章（第 1 课）：常见案例
int_u73.exp:
Unit 73 — 必用 the 的情况系统化整理。

带 the：
  • 独一无二的事物：the Earth, the sky, the environment；
  • same, only, first, next, last, right, wrong, main 后：the same problem, the only solution；
  • 形容词作群体：the rich, the poor, the elderly, the unemployed；
  • 结构 the more… the more：The more you practise, the better you get.（练得越多，越好。）
  • 乐器：play the piano；
  • 收音机、电影院：listen to the radio, go to the cinema。
不带 the（一般意义）：go to bed, go to school, go to work, go to church, go home, at home, have breakfast/lunch/dinner。

信号：涉及场所的典型功能 — 不用冠词；涉及建筑本身 — 用 the：go to the school to meet the teacher。
int_u73.exc: 不带 the：go to bed, go to school, go to church, go to work, go home — 一般意义的「学习/睡觉/祈祷」。
mistakes: She has same problem. → the same problem — same 总用 the。
He's only person I trust. → the only person — only 总用 the。
Rich should help poor. → The rich should help the poor. — 群体用 the + adj。

int_u74.text: 74 单元 - 文章（第 2 课）：表达式
int_u74.exp:
Unit 74 — 需整体记忆的带 the 与不带 the 的固定搭配。

带 the：
  • in the morning / afternoon / evening：I work out in the morning.
  • on the way：I met him on the way home.
  • at the same time：They arrived at the same time.
  • in the end = 最终、结果：In the end, we decided to stay.
  • by the way：By the way, did you call him?
不带冠词：at night, at lunchtime, at weekends, by chance, by mistake, on foot, on holiday, on time, at work, at home。
对比：in the end（最后）vs. at the end of（在某物的末尾）：At the end of the film I cried.

这些表达是固定的，偏离会改变含义或听起来不对。
int_u74.exc: At night（夜间）— 不带 the，而 in the morning/afternoon/evening — 带 the。
mistakes: In morning I drink coffee. → In the morning — 固定搭配带 the。
At the night we sleep. → At night — 不带冠词。
At the end of the day I was tired.（= 最后）→ In the end — in the end = 最后。

int_u75.text: 75 单元 - 文章（第 3 课）：一般与具体
int_u75.note: 我喜欢音乐。 / 我喜欢这家咖啡馆的音乐。
int_u75.exp:
Unit 75 — 零冠词（泛指）与 the（特指）之间的选择。

不用冠词 — 谈论概念、物质或群体整体：
  • Music is a universal language. — 音乐是通用语言。
  • I love coffee. — 我爱咖啡（总体）。
  • Children need love. — 孩子需要爱。
用 the — 指上下文中已知的具体实例：
  • The music in this café is great. — 正是这里的。
  • The coffee I had this morning was awful. — 具体的。
  • The children in this class are smart. — 正是这些孩子。

抽象概念（life, nature, society, history, education）— 一般意义不用冠词：Life is short.。但：The life of a writer is lonely. — 通过 of 具体化。
int_u75.exc: Life, nature, society, history 一般意义不用冠词。但 the life of a writer — 具体的生活。
mistakes: I like the music.（总体）→ I like music. — 泛指不用冠词。
<s>Life is short.</s>（总体）— 正确。<s>The life is short</s> — 错误。
<s>Children like playing.</s>（所有孩子）— 正确；<s>The children like playing</s> 只指具体的。

int_u76.text: 单元 76 – 冠词 the（第 4 课）：带有形容词和国籍
int_u76.exp:
Unit 76 — the 用于表示社会和国族群体的形容词前。

The + 形容词 = 人群（复数）：
  • the rich, the poor, the young, the old, the elderly, the unemployed, the homeless, the sick, the blind, the deaf, the disabled, the dead, the injured。
  • The rich get richer. — 富者越富。
  • The government should help the elderly. — 政府应帮助老年人。
以 -sh, -ch, -ese 结尾的国籍 用 the 表示民族整体：the French, the English, the Chinese, the Japanese, the British, the Dutch。
  • The French are proud of their cuisine.（动词用复数）
  • 指一个人 — a Frenchman / a French person。

这些名词没有 -s 结尾（不能 the Frenches）。动词始终用复数。
int_u76.exc: the + adj 的动词始终用复数：The poor are…，不用 is。
mistakes: The French is famous for wine. → The French are famous… — 动词用复数。
The poor needs help. → The poor need help. — 复数一致。
A French person — 指一人正确，但 A French are polite 错误 — 指群体需要 the French。

int_u77.text: Unit 77 - 冠词（第 5 课）：带有姓名和地名
int_u77.note: 泰晤士河/巴黎/阿尔卑斯山
int_u77.exp:
Unit 77 — 地理名称和专有名词的冠词。

不带 the：
  • 大陆、国家、城市、州、单个岛屿：Europe, Japan, Paris, Texas, Sicily；
  • 单座山和湖：Mount Everest, Lake Baikal；
  • 人名：Anna, Mr Smith。
带 the：
  • 河流、大洋、海、运河：the Nile, the Atlantic, the Black Sea, the Suez Canal；
  • 山脉、群岛：the Alps, the Andes, the Bahamas；
  • 复数国家或含 Republic, Kingdom, States, Union：the Netherlands, the UK, the USA, the Russian Federation；
  • 地区和方位：the Middle East, the north of France；
  • 家族：the Smiths（史密斯一家）。

沙漠和大多数海湾 — 带 the：the Sahara, the Gulf of Mexico。
int_u77.exc: 湖不带 the：Lake Baikal, Lake Como。单座山不带 the：Mount Everest。但山脉 — the Alps, the Andes。
mistakes: The Paris is beautiful. → Paris is beautiful. — 城市不带 the。
Mount the Everest → Mount Everest — 单座山不带 the。
Nile is long. → The Nile is long. — 河流带 the。

int_u78.text: 78 单元 – 第 6 条（第 6 课）：街道、机构、组织
int_u78.exp:
Unit 78 — 街道、建筑、组织名称的冠词。

不带 the：
  • 街道、广场、公园：Oxford Street, Times Square, Central Park；
  • 机场、车站：Heathrow Airport, Victoria Station；
  • 不带 of 的大学：Harvard, Cambridge, Oxford；
  • 以所有者命名的商店和餐厅：McDonald's, Harrods。
带 the：
  • 博物馆、剧院、电影院：the British Museum, the Odeon；
  • 酒店、餐厅：the Hilton, the Ritz；
  • 报纸、组织：the Times, the BBC, the EU, the UN；
  • 含 of 的名称：the University of Oxford, the Tower of London, the Bank of England；
  • 历史建筑：the White House, the Kremlin, the Vatican。

of 规则：X of Y 结构几乎总是需要 the。
int_u78.exc: 例外：the White House, the Kremlin, the Vatican。带 of 几乎总用 the：the University of Oxford。
mistakes: I study at the Oxford. → at Oxford — 不带 of 的大学不带 the。
Let's meet at Hilton. → at the Hilton — 酒店带 the。
The Oxford Street is busy. → Oxford Street is busy. — 街道不带冠词。

int_u79.text: 79 单元 - 单元。还有更多数量的名词
int_u79.exp:
Unit 79 — 名词的单复数和与动词的一致。

规则形式：+s, +es（在 -s/-sh/-ch/-x 后），-y→-ies。
不规则：man→men, woman→women, child→children, foot→feet, tooth→teeth, mouse→mice, person→people。
单复数同形：sheep, fish, deer, aircraft, species, series。
总为复数的名词（动词复数）：police, people, cattle, clothes, scissors, trousers, jeans, glasses。
  • The police are investigating. — 警方在调查。
  • My trousers are new. — 我的裤子是新的。
形似复数但为单数：news, mathematics, physics, economics, athletics, politics：The news is bad. Physics is difficult.

Person 复数 = people；persons 只用于正式/法律语境。
int_u79.exc: Person 复数通常为 people。Persons — 仅用于正式/法律语境。
mistakes: The police is coming. → The police are coming. — police 总为复数。
Three sheeps → three sheep — 单复数同形。
Mathematics are difficult. → Mathematics is difficult. — -ics 结尾的学科通常单数。

int_u80.text: 第 80 单元 – 两个相邻的名词（构词法）
int_u80.note: 车门/音乐老师/公共汽车站
int_u80.exp:
Unit 80 — 「名词 + 名词」结构：第一个说明第二个的类型或用途。

公式：noun1 + noun2，noun1 起形容词作用。
  • a car door — 车门（门的类型）；
  • a bus stop — 公共汽车站；
  • a music teacher — 音乐老师；
  • a kitchen table — 厨房桌；
  • a credit card — 信用卡。
第一个名词用单数，即使含义为复数：a shoe shop（不是 shoes shop），a book shop。
例外（保留复数）：a sports car, a clothes shop, a sales manager, a news report, a customs officer。
与 of 比较：a car door（一般类型）vs. the door of the car（某辆车的具体门）。

带数词的复合形容词：a three-year-old boy, a ten-minute walk — 不加 -s。
int_u80.exc: 例外 — 第一个词按意义为复数：a sports car, a clothes shop, a sales manager。
mistakes: A books shop → a bookshop — 第一个名词用单数。
A three-years-old boy → a three-year-old boy — 复合形容词不加 s。
The door of the car（简单「车门」）→ a car door — 对于类型对象首选复合形式。

int_u81.note: 汤姆的车/儿童房/昨天的新闻
int_u81.exp:
Unit 81 — 带撇号 's 的所有格。

形式：
  • 单数：Tom's car, the girl's bag；
  • 规则复数（已以 -s 结尾）：仅加撇号 — the boys' room, my parents' house；
  • 不规则复数：'s — the children's toys, the men's room。
何时用 's，何时用 of：
  • 's — 与人、动物、国家、组织：Mary's book, the cat's tail, Russia's economy, the company's policy；
  • of — 与物品：the top of the page, the end of the film。
时间：yesterday's news, today's paper, this week's meeting。
距离/时长：a day's journey, two weeks' holiday。

双重所有格表示多人中之一：a friend of my brother's = 我哥哥众多朋友中的一个。
int_u81.exc: 双重撇号：以 -s 结尾的复数仅加撇号：the boys' room，而不规则复数 — 's：the children's room。
mistakes: The car of Tom → Tom's car — 人名用 's。
The childrens' room → the children's room — children 是不规则复数。
My parent's house（指双亲）→ my parents' house — 复数所有格。

int_u82.note: 我伤害了自己。她玩得很开心。
int_u82.exp:
Unit 82 — 当主语和宾语相同时用反身代词。

形式：myself, yourself, himself, herself, itself, ourselves, yourselves, themselves。
  • I cut myself while cooking. — 我切到自己了。
  • She enjoyed herself at the party. — 她玩得很开心。
用于强调 — 「亲自/本人」：
  • I did it myself. — 我自己做的。
  • The president himself opened the ceremony. — 总统亲自开幕。
By + reflexive = 独自、无帮助：I live by myself.
不用反身代词的动词（与俄语不同！）：wash, shave, dress, feel, concentrate, relax, meet。
  • I washed and got dressed.（不是 washed myself）
  • We met at 5.（见面）

Each other ≠ ourselves：They looked at each other（互相看），不是 at themselves（各自看自己）。
int_u82.exc: 动词 wash, shave, dress, feel, concentrate, relax, meet 通常不用反身代词：I washed and got dressed，不用 washed myself。
mistakes: We met ourselves at 5. → We met at 5. — meet 彼此不需要 ourselves。
They helped themself. → themselves — self 复数 → selves。
<s>I did it by myself.</s>（= 独自，无帮助）— 正确；<s>I did it myself</s>（亲自）。混淆含义。

int_u83.note: 这是你的吗？我自己的房间
int_u83.exp:
Unit 83 — 绝对物主代词和 own 表达。

绝对形式（不带名词）：mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs。
  • Is this your book? — Yes, it's mine. — 是我的。
  • Their house is bigger than ours. — 他们的房子比我们的大。
A friend of mine — 我的朋友之一（双重所有格）：不能 a friend of me。
My own + noun 强调正是我的，不是别人的：
  • I want my own room. — 我想要自己的房间。
  • She has her own business. — 她有自己的生意。
On my own = by myself = 独自，自己：
  • She lives on her own. — 她一个人住。
  • I did it on my own. — 我自己做的。

own 后没有 of：my own of car → my own car。
int_u83.exc: own 后总跟名词：my own car。不能不带语境地把 my own 当作代词。
mistakes: Is this book your? → Is this book yours? — 独立代词用 yours。
A friend of me → a friend of mine — of 后需要绝对形式。
I have my own of car. → my own car — own 后不用 of。

int_u84.exp:
Unit 84 — 形式主语 there 和 it。

There is / there are — 存在、拥有：
  • There is a problem with the car. — 车有问题。
  • There are many reasons. — 有很多原因。
  • There was an accident. — 发生了事故。
It — 用于时间、天气、距离、总体评价：
  • It's cold today. — 今天冷。
  • It's 10 o'clock. — 10点了。
  • It's 5 km to the station. — 5公里。
  • It's nice to see you. — 见到你真好。
对比：报告存在 — there；描述情境 — it。
习语：there's no point, there's no need；it takes, it seems, it looks like。

一致：there is + 单数/不可数；there are + 复数。
int_u84.exc: It 用于天气、时间、距离；there — 用于存在。混用会改变意思。
mistakes: It is a problem with the engine.（存在含义）→ There is a problem…
There is cold today. → It is cold today. — 天气用 it。
It has many people here. → There are many people here. — 存在用 there。

int_u85.note: 一些牛奶/任何问题/任何人/任何事
int_u85.exp:
Unit 85 — 中级水平的 some、any 及其派生词。

基本分布：
  • some — 肯定句、礼貌建议/请求；
  • any — 疑问、否定、条件句；
  • any 在肯定句中 = 「任何」：Take any seat.
派生词：
  • 人：someone/somebody, anyone/anybody, no one/nobody, everyone/everybody；
  • 物：something, anything, nothing, everything；
  • 地点：somewhere, anywhere, nowhere, everywhere。
示例：
  • Is there anyone at home? — 家里有人吗？
  • I didn't see anybody. — 我没看到任何人。
  • You can sit anywhere. — 你可以坐任何地方。

一致：派生词为单数 — Everybody is here. Somebody has taken my bag.。不能双重否定：I don't know anything，不用 nothing。
int_u85.exc: Any 在肯定句中 = 「任何」：Take any book you want。这不是否定。
mistakes: I have any questions.（肯定）→ some questions — 肯定用 some。
Somebody know the answer? → Does anybody know…? — 一般疑问用 anybody。
There isn't nobody here. → There isn't anybody / There is nobody here. — 避免双重否定。

int_u86.text: 第86单元-没有人，没有人，没有，无处，没有
int_u86.note: 没有人知道。什么也没发生。
int_u86.exp:
Unit 86 — 以 no- 开头的否定代词。

形式：nobody / no one, nothing, nowhere, none。
主要规则：动词始终肯定，因为否定已在代词中。
  • Nobody came to the party. — 没人来聚会。
  • Nothing happened. — 什么也没发生。
  • There was nowhere to sit. — 无处可坐。
No + 名词 vs. none：
  • no + noun：I have no money. No cars were parked here.
  • none 不带名词（独立或与 of）：How much? — None. None of us knew.
禁止双重否定：Nobody didn't come. → Nobody came 或 Anybody didn't come（但前者更好）。

nobody/nothing 之后使用 any-：Nobody said anything.（没人说过什么。）
int_u86.exc: No + 名词（no money）；none — 不带名词（None of us knew）。动词为肯定。
mistakes: Nobody didn't come. → Nobody came. — nobody 后动词肯定。
How much? No. → None. — 独立回答用 none。
Nobody said nothing. → Nobody said anything. — 否定后用 any-。

int_u87.note: 水多/人多/时间少/朋友少
int_u87.exp:
Unit 87 — 数量词 much, many, little, few 以及 a little / a few 的细微差别。

按可数性分布：
  • many, few, a few — 与可数复数；
  • much, little, a little — 与不可数。
关键细微差别 — 语气：
  • little/few（不带 a）= 很少，几乎没有（负面语气）：I have little time. Few people came.
  • a little / a few = 有一些，足够（正面）：I have a little time. A few friends helped.
Much 在肯定句中听起来正式；口语中更喜欢 a lot of / lots of：I have a lot of money。疑问和否定中 much 正常：Do you have much time? Not much.

比较：I have a few friends（几个，好）vs. I have few friends（少，悲伤）。
int_u87.exc: Much 在肯定句中听起来正式；口语中更常用 a lot of：I have a lot of money，而不是 much money。
mistakes: I have many money. → much money / a lot of money — 钱不可数。
A few water → a little water — 不可数用 little/a little。
I have little friends.（中性，「几个」）→ a few friends — 不带 a 意为「几乎没有」。

int_u88.text: 单元 88 – 介词 of 与代词
int_u88.note: 我们大多数人/一些学生/他们都没有
int_u88.exp:
Unit 88 — 「数量词 + of」结构用于有限定词的名词和代词前。

带 of 用于 the, these, those, my, your, us, them 前：
  • most of the students, some of these books, all of my friends, none of us, half of them, both of them, each of us。
不带 of — 泛指，无限定词：
  • most people, some students, all children, many cars。
对比：
  • Most people like music. — 大多数人（总体）。
  • Most of the people at the party were young. — 那些人中的大多数。
Half：允许 half of the cake 或 half the cake；half + 数字：half an hour。
All/both 在名词前可省略 of：all (of) my friends, both (of) the books。

人称代词前 of 必用：all of us，不用 all us。
int_u88.exc: 不带 of：most people, some students（泛指）。带 of：most of the people, some of the students（特定群体）。
mistakes: Most of people like music. → Most people like music. — 泛指不带 of the。
Some the students → some of the students — the 前必用 of。
Half cake → half of the cake / half the cake — 特定群体前。

int_u89.text: 第89单元-两者，要么，都不是
int_u89.note: 两者/任何一条路/都不正确。
int_u89.exp:
Unit 89 — 指代两个物体的代词。

Both（两个都）— 肯定：
  • Both sisters are tall.
  • Both of them came. / They both came.
Either（两个中任一；或…或）：
  • Either road leads to town. — 两条都可以。
  • You can take either.
Neither（两个都不）：
  • Neither answer is correct. — 两个都不对。
  • Neither of them came.
成对连词：
  • both… and：Both Tom and Anna were there.
  • either… or：You can have either tea or coffee.
  • neither… nor：Neither Tom nor Anna came.
一致：either/neither 正式上要求单数（Neither of them knows），口语允许复数。

Both — 仅用于两个；三个及以上用 all。
int_u89.exc: Both — 仅用于两个。三个及以上用 all。neither 后动词正式为单数，口语为复数。
mistakes: Both of answers are correct. → Both answers / Both of the answers — of 需要 the/代词。
Either answers are right. → Either answer is right. — either 要求单数。
Neither of them don't know. → Neither of them knows. — 不双重否定。

int_u90.exp:
Unit 90 — all, whole, every 的区别。

All + noun（全部/所有）：
  • all day, all the time, all students；
  • 带限定词：all my friends, all the books。
Whole（整个，完全）：the whole day = all the day。通常与 the/my/a：
  • She ate the whole cake. — 吃了整块蛋糕。
  • a whole week — 整整一周。
Every — 每一个单独的，单数：
  • every day, every student, every time；
  • Every student has a book.（动词单数）
对比：all day（整天不间断）vs. every day（每天，规律）。
Everybody/everyone/everything/everywhere — 动词单数：Everybody is ready.

Whole 不与不可数：不是 the whole water → all the water。
int_u90.exc: every/everybody/everything 后动词单数：Everybody is ready，不用 are。
mistakes: All day 和 every day 混淆：all day = 整天，every day = 每天。
All the people is here. → All the people are here. — people 复数。
Everybody have a phone. → Everybody has a phone. — everybody 后用 has。

int_u91.note: 每个学生/每个学生 - 细微差别？
int_u91.exp:
Unit 91 — each 和 every 的区别。

Each — 对每个成员的个别看待（常为小群体）：
  • Each student received a certificate. — 每人（单独）都发。
  • She gave each of us a present.
  • They each have a key. — 可以放在主语之后。
Every — 泛化，无例外：
  • Every student passed the exam. — 作为群体。
  • Every day I go jogging.
关键区别：
  • each — 用于两个及以上；every — 仅用于三个及以上；
  • each of us/them/the + noun，但 every 不与 of 搭配 — 需说 each of 或 every one of；
  • every 语序固定：every + noun；each 更灵活：each student, each of the students, the students each。

仅两个物体 — 只用 each，不用 every。
int_u91.exc: Every 不用于两个物体 — 只用 each 或 both。
mistakes: Every of students → Each of the students / Every student — every 不带 of。
Every two students have a book.（两个）→ Each of the two students has a book.
They every have a key. → They each have a key. — 主语后只用 each。

int_u92.text: 92 单元 - 关系从句（第 1 课）
int_u92.note: 那个人/那本书/那个地方
int_u92.exp:
Unit 92 — 限定性从句（relative clauses），用于修饰名词。

主要连词：
  • who — 人（主语/宾语）：the man who called；
  • that — 人和物：the man that called, the book that I read；
  • which — 物：the book which I read；
  • where — 地点：the town where I was born；
  • when — 时间：the day when we met。
位置：紧跟在被修饰的名词之后。
省略连词：当连词为从句中的宾语时可省略：the book (that/which) I read。若连词为主语，不可省略：the man who called（不能 the man called）。
句尾介词：the girl (who) I spoke to。正式：to whom I spoke。

俄语的「который」根据语境由多个连词覆盖。
int_u92.exc: 只有当连词为从句中宾语时才能省略：the book (that) I read。若为主语不能省略：the man who called。
mistakes: The man which lives here → the man who/that lives here — 人用 who/that。
The book who I read → the book which/that I read — 物用 which/that。
The man lives here is my uncle. → The man who lives here is my uncle. — 不能省略主语。

int_u93.text: Unit 93 – 关系从句 with which, that, who
int_u93.exp:
Unit 93 — relative clauses 中 who, which, that 的深入解析。

Who — 人，作主语或宾语：the doctor who treats me, the girl who I met。
Which — 物和动物：the computer which broke down。
That — 人和物通用，但仅在限定性从句中（无逗号）：the man that called, the book that I bought。
Whom — who 的宾格形式，正式：the person whom I met。口语中替换为 who 或省略。
省略规则：
  • 连词为宾语时可省略：the film (that) I saw；
  • 连词为主语时不可省略：the film that was on TV。
不能在从句中重复代词：the man who I saw him → the man who I saw。

That 不可用于非限定性从句：My brother, who lives in Moscow, … — 只用 who。
int_u93.exc: That 不能用于带逗号的描述性（非限定性）从句。
mistakes: My brother, that lives in Moscow, is a doctor. → My brother, who lives in Moscow… — 非限定性不能用 that。
The car who I bought → the car which/that I bought — 物不用 who。
The man who I saw him yesterday → the man who I saw yesterday — 不重复代词 him。

int_u94.text: 单元 94 – 关系从句 with who, who, where
int_u94.note: 拥有汽车的男人/我遇到的女人
int_u94.exp:
Unit 94 — 特殊关系代词：whose, whom, where。

Whose（谁的）— 所属，用于人和物：
  • the man whose car was stolen — 车被偷的那个男人；
  • the book whose cover is torn — 封面撕破的书。
Whom — who 的宾格形式，正式文体，尤其在介词后：
  • the colleague whom I mentioned；
  • the person to whom I wrote（正式）= the person (who) I wrote to（非正式）。
Where — 用于地点：
  • the hotel where we stayed；
  • 等价：the hotel at which we stayed / the hotel (that) we stayed at。
When / why — 时间/原因：the day when we met, the reason why she left。
介词 + whom/which 在正式写作中：the company for which she works。口语：the company (that) she works for。

不要混淆 whose 和 who's（= who is/has）。
int_u94.exc: Whose — 用于人和物：the book whose cover is torn。Whom 几乎不用于口语。
mistakes: The man who's car is red → the man whose car is red — who's = who is。
The company who I work for（正式）→ the company for which I work — 正式文体介词提前。
The place where I went there → the place where I went — 不重复 there。

int_u95.text: 单元 95 – 限制性和描述性条款（第 1 课）
int_u95.note: 我住在巴黎的妹妹 / 我住在巴黎的妹妹
int_u95.exp:
Unit 95 — 限定性（defining）和描述性（non-defining）从句的区别。

Defining — 说明具体是哪一个；无逗号；信息必不可少：
  • The man who called was a doctor. — 正是打电话那个。
  • 可用 that；作宾语的连词可省略。
Non-defining — 提供附加信息；必须用逗号；可删除而不影响意思：
  • My father, who is 60, runs marathons. — 我只有一个父亲，关于他的事实。
  • 只能用 who/which/whose/where/when，不能用 that；
  • 不能省略连词。
关键测试：若信息说明是几个中的哪一个 — defining（无逗号）；若为关于已知物的附加事实 — non-defining（有逗号）。

发音：non-defining 在逗号处有停顿。
int_u95.exc: non-defining 从句中逗号必用，that 禁用，连词不能省略。
mistakes: My father who is 60 runs marathons.（一个父亲）→ My father, who is 60, runs marathons. — 需要逗号。
My sister, that lives in Paris → My sister, who lives in Paris — 不能用 that。
My car which is new is red.（一辆车）→ My car, which is new, is red. — 附加信息 = 逗号。

int_u96.text: 单元 96 – 带有附加信息的条款（第 2 课）
int_u96.note: 她通过了考试，这让所有人都感到惊讶。
int_u96.exp:
Unit 96 — non-defining 从句的深入工作。

Which 可以指前面整个句子，而不仅是一个名词：
  • She passed the exam, which surprised everyone. — 通过考试这一事实令大家惊讶。
  • He was late, which annoyed me. — 他的迟到令我恼火。
non-defining 规则：
  • 始终用逗号；
  • 只能 who, which, whose, where, when；不能 that；
  • 连词不能省略。
信息作用：non-defining 从句添加评论、反应、补充。常用 which 表达说话人态度。
  • My car, which I bought last year, broke down. — 关于车的附加事实。
  • He refused to help, which made me angry. — 对其行为的评论。

不能用 what 代替 which 来指代句子。
int_u96.exc: non-defining 从句中 which 可以指代整个想法：He was late, which annoyed me。
mistakes: She got the job, that was great. → which was great — non-defining 只用 which。
He was late, what annoyed me. → which annoyed me — 此处 what 不允许。
She passed the exam which surprised everyone（无逗号）→ …exam, which surprised everyone — non-defining 前需逗号。

int_u97.note: 与汤姆说话的女人/受伤的男孩
int_u97.exp:
Unit 97 — 分词短语 -ing 和 -ed 作为 relative clauses 的压缩替代。

-ing（主动含义）替换主动语态的从句，带 Present/Past Continuous 或表示正在进行的动作意义：
  • the man sitting by the window = the man who is sitting by the window；
  • Anyone wanting to leave should do it now. = anyone who wants to leave。
-ed（被动含义）替换被动从句：
  • the car stolen last week = the car that was stolen；
  • The money found in the street was returned. = which was found。
不规则动词保留第三形式：stolen, broken, written, taken, made, seen。
文体：更简洁，更常用于书面和正式语言、报刊标题。

分词短语必须修饰最近的名词，否则产生歧义。
int_u97.exc: -ing 短语替换主动从句，-ed 短语替换被动。不规则动词：stolen, broken, written。
mistakes: The man sat by the window（分词）→ the man sitting by the window — 需要 -ing。
The car stealing last week → the car stolen last week — 被动需要过去分词。
The boy injuring → the boy injured — 被动含义：-ed/III形式。

int_u98.exp:
Unit 98 — 带 -ing 和 -ed 后缀的形容词对。

-ing — 感觉源的特性（引起情绪的事物）：
  • The film is boring. — 电影很无聊（引起无聊）。
  • The news was shocking. — 消息令人震惊。
-ed — 经历感觉者的特性：
  • I'm bored. — 我感到无聊。
  • She was shocked by the news. — 她被消息震惊。
常见对：interesting/interested, exciting/excited, tiring/tired, surprising/surprised, amazing/amazed, annoying/annoyed, confusing/confused, embarrassing/embarrassed, disappointing/disappointed, frightening/frightened。
记忆法：-ed 是我（我感受），-ing 是它（它引起）。
  • The teacher is interesting; the students are interested.

-ed 后典型跟 in/by/with/at/about：interested in, surprised by, bored with, shocked at。
int_u98.exc: 几乎所有情感动词都这样配对：surprising/surprised, shocking/shocked, amazing/amazed, annoying/annoyed。
mistakes: I'm very boring. → I'm very bored. — 若自己感受 — -ed。
The film was bored. → The film was boring. — 情绪源 — -ing。
I'm interesting in history. → I'm interested in history. — 感受 — -ed。

int_u99.text: 第 99 单元 - 形容词顺序
int_u99.note: 一座美丽的意大利古老大石屋
int_u99.exp:
Unit 99 — 名词前多个形容词的顺序。

标准顺序：
  1. 看法/评价：lovely, beautiful, awful；
  2. 大小：big, small, tiny；
  3. 年龄：old, new, young；
  4. 形状：round, square, long；
  5. 颜色：red, black；
  6. 来源：Italian, French；
  7. 材质：wooden, silk, stone；
  8. 用途/类型（常为复合名词的一部分）：kitchen, sports。
完整链条示例：a lovely little old round black Italian wooden box。实际中通常 2–3 个形容词。
示例：
  • a big old house — 大小 + 年龄；
  • a beautiful Italian painting — 看法 + 来源；
  • a round wooden table — 形状 + 材质。

逗号用于同层级（看法）形容词之间：a tiring, boring day。
int_u99.exc: 同类形容词之间用逗号或 and：a big, beautiful house。
mistakes: A black old small bag → a small old black bag — 顺序：大小 → 年龄 → 颜色。
An Italian big restaurant → a big Italian restaurant — 大小先于来源。
A wooden round table → a round wooden table — 形状先于材质。
